label: Portrait Drawings on Paper
description: These are portrait drawings and designs from the Rijks Museum collection, predominantly executed on paper using dry media (chalk, pencil) or ink, depicting individuals from various time periods and social positions.

label: French Royal Commemorative Tokens
description: A collection of metal tokens (jetons) from the Paris Museums featuring French and European nobility and royalty from the 13th to 18th centuries, used as commemorative objects and currency alternatives in France.

label: French Historical Commemorative Medals
description: A collection of numismatic medals from French institutions documenting significant political, military, and cultural events in French history from the 17th to 19th centuries, primarily cast in copper alloys and bronze.

label: Japanese Edo Period Paintings
description: A collection of Japanese paintings primarily from the Edo period featuring diverse subjects including figures, nature studies, and cultural scenes, created using traditional ink, color, and precious materials on silk or paper supports.

label: French satirical lithographic prints
description: A collection of 19th-century French lithographic satirical prints and caricatures depicting humorous scenes of everyday life, social commentary, and political figures.

label: 19th Century French Satirical Lithographs
description: A collection of French lithographic prints from the 1840s featuring humorous domestic and social scenes with witty dialogue, representing popular satirical commentary on everyday Parisian life and bourgeois manners.

label: Medieval Illuminated Manuscript Folios
description: These objects are decorative manuscript pages from medieval European manuscripts (French, Flemish, English, German, and Byzantine) featuring religious narratives and scenes rendered in tempera colors with gold leaf and gold paint.

label: Early American Daguerreotype Portraits
description: These objects are cased daguerreotypes, primarily from 19th-century American culture, featuring portrait photographs of families, individuals, and groups captured using this pioneering photographic technique.

label: Dutch Golden Age drawings
description: These objects are drawings and watercolors from the Netherlands and Holland regions, primarily created using ink, chalk, and wash techniques to depict landscapes, daily life scenes, and architectural subjects characteristic of 17th-century Dutch artistic traditions.

label: Northern European Religious Prints
description: A collection of devotional prints depicting Christian saints, created through engraving and etching techniques on paper from various Northern European institutions, primarily the Rijksmuseum.

label: European Textile Fragments
description: These objects are preserved textile fragments from European cultures (primarily France, England, and Netherlands) spanning various weaving, dyeing, and embroidery techniques including brocading, damask, velvet, and metal-thread work.

label: Byzantine Gospel Book Folios
description: These objects are individual text pages from a single Byzantine Gospel Book with Commentaries produced in Constantinople, now held in the Cleveland Museum of Art collection.

label: Early French Portrait Photography
description: A collection of 19th-century French photographic portraits and documentary subjects created using early printing techniques such as salted paper and albumen silver prints, primarily from the mid-to-late 1800s.

label: British Drawings and Watercolors
description: These objects are drawings and watercolors created in England and the United Kingdom, primarily from the 18th-19th centuries, depicting landscapes, portraits, social scenes, and everyday subjects rendered in mixed media techniques.

label: French Etching Prints Collection
description: A collection of original etching prints from French cultural heritage, spanning mythological narratives, allegorical representations, historical scenes, and geographical views, all executed in eau-forte (etching) technique.

label: 19th Century Egyptian Temple Photography
description: English albumen print photographs documenting ancient Egyptian temple architecture and monuments along the Nile Valley, created during the mid-to-late 1800s.

label: Italian Renaissance Portrait Prints
description: A collection of engraved and etched portrait prints on paper, primarily depicting Italian Renaissance figures and dignitaries, created through printmaking techniques popular in 16th-17th century Italy.

label: 19th Century French Religious Prints
description: A collection of devotional woodcut and engraved religious imagery from France (primarily 18th-19th century) featuring saints, biblical scenes, and Christian iconography, produced using techniques such as woodblock printing and intaglio, hand-colored with stencil or watercolor on laid paper.

label: European Prints Collection
description: A diverse collection of European prints from France and Belgium, primarily from the 19th century, created using various intaglio and relief printing techniques including etching, engraving, woodcut, and lithography.

label: Roman Terracotta Oil Lamps
description: These objects are Roman-era lamps primarily made of terracotta, featuring decorative discus designs with varied iconography including mythological, gladiatorial, and everyday scenes.

label: 19th Century English Prints
description: A collection of prints from England spanning the 19th and early 20th centuries, created using various intaglio and relief techniques including etching, drypoint, mezzotint, and lithography, held across multiple museum institutions.

label: Nineteenth-century albumen silver prints
description: This collection comprises card photographs and prints created using albumen silver print technology, predominantly from mid-to-late 19th-century France, featuring portraits of notable aristocrats and cultural figures alongside architectural landmarks and artwork reproductions.

label: Early 20th Century Pictorialist Photography
description: A collection of photogravure and halftone photographs from the early 1900s held at the Miami Art Museum, featuring portraits, urban scenes, and artistic compositions that exemplify the Pictorialist movement's emphasis on aesthetic and tonal qualities.

label: Ancient Greek Drinking Vessels
description: These objects are ancient Greek kylixes (drinking cups) and related vessels, predominantly from Attic culture, made of terracotta with red-figure or black-figure decoration techniques.

label: Pre-Columbian Mesoamerican Artifacts
description: These objects represent diverse ancient Mesoamerican cultures including Maya, Olmec, Aztec, and regional traditions from Mexico, Guatemala, and Honduras, spanning from the Olmec period through the Spanish conquest.

label: French Graphic Arts Drawings
description: A collection of French drawings and graphic artworks from Paris museums, spanning figurative studies, portraits, historical scenes, and preparatory sketches executed in various media including graphite, charcoal, ink, and sanguine.

label: 19th Century French Satirical Lithographs
description: A collection of French lithographic prints from the 19th century, primarily featuring satirical commentary on Parisian society, politics, and daily life through humorous narrative scenes and social observation.

label: French Historical Tokens and Jetons
description: A collection of French numismatic tokens spanning from the Medieval period to the 19th century, including merchant tokens, commemorative jetons, guild tokens, and administrative markers made from various metals such as brass, copper, and tin.

label: Japanese Kabuki Actor Portraits
description: These objects are woodblock prints and paintings from Japan depicting kabuki theater actors in various roles, spanning from the Edo period through the 20th century.

label: Asian Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are ceramics from across East and Southeast Asia (China, Korea, Vietnam, Thailand) spanning various periods and techniques, united by their functional vessel forms and glazed or decorated surface treatments.

label: Vintage Coin and Money Banks
description: These objects are decorative and functional toy banks from the 19th and 20th centuries, designed to encourage saving by storing coins in novelty forms ranging from animals and people to household items and mechanical contraptions.

label: French Stereographic Views
description: These are 19th-century stereograph photographs depicting French landmarks, architecture, landscapes, and urban scenes, primarily made using albumen silver print technology for three-dimensional viewing.

label: Northern European Passion Narrative Prints
description: A collection of prints from 16th-17th century Northern Europe (Germany, Netherlands, Flanders) depicting scenes from Christ's Passion and Redemption, created primarily through woodcut and engraving techniques.

label: Italian Drawings and Studies
description: These are primarily 18th-century Italian figure drawings and studies executed in various media including chalk, pen and ink, and watercolor, showcasing figure studies, portraiture, and compositional sketches characteristic of Italian academic training and artistic practice.

label: Edo Period Landscape Prints
description: Japanese woodblock prints depicting famous landmarks, temples, bridges, and scenic views of Edo (Tokyo) from the 19th century, primarily from Hiroshige's series 'One Hundred Famous Views of Edo'.

label: Nineteenth-Century American Stereograph Views
description: These objects are stereographs depicting American Western landscapes—primarily Colorado and Grand Canyon locations—created in the late 1800s using albumen silver print and photomechanical processes for viewing through stereoscopes.

label: Single Medieval Illuminated Manuscript
description: These are individual folio pages from a single 15th-century illuminated Book of Hours commissioned by Charles the Noble of Navarre, created in Paris and now housed at the Cleveland Museum of Art.

label: American Etching Prints
description: A collection of etching prints created by American artists, primarily from the 19th-20th centuries, depicting landscape subjects and architectural scenes from locations across America and Europe.

label: Block-Printed Decorative Paper Wrappers
description: A collection of paper wrappers created using block printing (relief printing) techniques, featuring repeating patterns of floral, geometric, and botanical motifs designed for wrapping purposes.

label: European Medieval and Renaissance Armor
description: A collection of protective military equipment from European armorers (primarily German, Italian, and French centers) spanning the medieval through Renaissance periods, including helmets, body armor, shields, and supplementary pieces crafted from steel, iron, and brass.

label: French Etchings and Engravings
description: A collection of French graphic art prints from the Paris Museums, primarily executed in etching (eau-forte) and engraving (burin) techniques, depicting portraits, genre scenes, military subjects, and historical events from French culture.

label: Tuti-nama Manuscript Pages
description: These are text pages from the Tales of a Parrot (Tuti-nama), a celebrated Mughal manuscript illuminated during the court of Emperor Akbar in 16th-century India.

label: European Topographical Landscape Prints
description: A collection of etched and hand-colored prints depicting various European cities, rivers, harbors, and landscapes from different regions including the Netherlands, England, France, Germany, Italy, and Central Europe.

label: Etched prints of Roman antiquities
description: A collection of paper-based etching prints documenting Roman archaeological sites, monuments, artifacts, and architectural remains, primarily from the Rijksmuseum's studies of Rome and its classical heritage.

label: Traditional Artisan Tools and Crafts
description: This collection represents the material heritage of traditional trades and crafts from the Bresse region, including woodworking, stonecutting, textile work, and domestic crafts preserved at the Musées des Pays de l'Ain in Bourg-en-Bresse.

label: Pre-Columbian Andean Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are ceramic vessels and musical instruments from ancient Andean and Central American cultures (primarily Peru, with examples from Panama and Bolivia) that served both utilitarian and ceremonial purposes, often featuring figurative, zoomorphic, or portraiture forms.

label: European Decorative Lace Textiles
description: These objects are fine laces and lace-trimmed garment pieces produced across Europe from the 16th-18th centuries using traditional needle lace and bobbin lace techniques, primarily for adorning collars, cuffs, and caps of the upper classes.

label: Dutch Design Drawings and Plans
description: A collection of architectural, decorative, and illustrative design drawings from Dutch and Flemish artists spanning the 16th-18th centuries, created primarily with ink and paper as working designs for various commissions.

label: European Portrait Miniatures
description: These objects are small-scale portrait paintings, primarily from 16th-19th century Europe and America, executed on ivory, vellum, or paper using watercolor and other delicate techniques, often housed in decorative frames.

label: Architectural Etchings by Whistler
description: A collection of prints primarily featuring architectural subjects created by James McNeill Whistler using etching techniques, depicting landmarks from London, European locations, and American cities.

label: Italian Renaissance Religious Drawings
description: These works are primarily Italian Renaissance-era drawings and watercolors depicting Christian religious subjects, saints, and biblical narratives, executed in various media such as chalk, ink, and wash.

label: Japanese Color Woodblock Prints
description: All objects are Japanese color woodcut prints (ukiyo-e) on paper, primarily depicting scenes from Japanese culture, literature, and daily life with both figural and natural subject matter.

label: 19th Century British Documentary Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver prints and photographs documenting British architectural landmarks, estates, clergy, and notable figures from the Victorian era, primarily created in the 1860s.

label: French Silk Textile Panels
description: These are decorative textile panels from France and Lyon, primarily made of silk with various weaving techniques, created for furnishings and dress fabrics during the 18th-19th centuries.

label: French Oil Paintings
description: A collection of paintings from French cultural origins spanning the 18th-19th centuries, predominantly created using oil on canvas and depicting landscapes, genre scenes, and historical subjects.

label: Projection Slides of Artworks
description: Collection of photographic projection plates documenting artworks and objects held at the Petit Palais museum in Paris, used for educational or archival purposes.

label: Early American Portrait Photography
description: These objects are 19th-century photographs from the United States, predominantly portraits made using daguerreotype, ambrotype, and tintype processes that represent the earliest forms of photographic portraiture in America.

label: Roman Engraved Gems and Intaglios
description: These objects are primarily Roman-era engraved gems and intaglios made from semi-precious stones like cornelian and chalcedony, often featuring mythological figures and deities, designed for use in rings or as seals.

label: Dutch Landscape Etchings
description: A collection of etched prints depicting rural Dutch landscapes with buildings, waterways, and figures, primarily from the Dutch Golden Age, featuring scenes of canals, cottages, and countryside activities characteristic of Holland's artistic heritage.

label: Isabella the Catholic's Illuminated Hours
description: Individual folio pages from a single late 15th-century Flemish illuminated manuscript of devotional hours commissioned for Queen Isabella of Spain, featuring religious scenes and calendar illustrations.

label: Chinese Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are primarily Chinese ceramic vessels—bowls, dishes, and boxes—spanning various dynasties and ware types, united by their function as tableware and decorative objects featuring floral, dragon, and nature motifs.

label: Asian and Middle Eastern Textiles
description: A collection of decorative and functional textile objects from Asia and the Middle East, created primarily through needlework, embroidery, and weaving techniques using natural fibers and embellishments like metallic threads, beads, and mirrors.

label: 19th-century plaster cast reproductions
description: These objects are predominantly 19th-century Italian plaster casts reproducing famous classical sculptures, Renaissance artworks, and neoclassical pieces, reflecting the widespread practice of creating affordable educational replicas for art study and display.

label: 19th Century Albumen Silver Prints
description: A collection of landscape and architectural photographs from the Getty Museum's photography department, primarily created using albumen silver print technique during the Victorian era, depicting scenic locations across Australia, Europe, and beyond.

label: Medieval and Renaissance Religious Imagery
description: These objects are devotional artworks from European museums spanning the 13th-16th centuries, predominantly featuring Christian themes such as the Virgin Mary, Christ Child, saints, and Crucifixion scenes created across multiple media and regions.

label: 19th Century Italian Stereograph Photography
description: Collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting Italian architectural landmarks, archaeological sites, and cultural monuments from the 19th century.

label: Alpine landscape stereographs
description: This collection consists of mid-19th century stereographs featuring Alpine landscapes and landmarks in Switzerland, France, and nearby regions, all produced as albumen silver prints for the popular Victorian stereoscope viewing format.

label: Japanese Noh Theater Woodblock Prints
description: These color woodblock prints document performances from Japanese Noh theater, a classical dramatic art form, organized in illustrated series that catalog individual Noh plays and their characteristic scenes.

label: Japanese Woodblock Prints
description: A collection of Japanese woodblock prints (primarily nishiki-e and surimono formats) created using ink and color on paper, predominantly from the Asian Art department.

label: French Revolutionary Political Medals
description: These are commemorative medal dies (coins and poinçons) documenting major French political upheavals from the Revolution through 1871, inscribed with patriotic messages and recording pivotal moments in French republican history.

label: Marcantonio Raimondi engravings
description: Collection of original engravings and prints by the Renaissance master Marcantonio Raimondi, catalogued in the Bartsch reference system and held in Parisian museums.

label: Late 19th Century Trade Cards
description: A collection of chromolithographed French advertising cards from the fourth quarter of the 19th century, printed on cardboard with diverse subjects ranging from moral lessons and flora to geographical references and children's activities.

label: 19th Century Yosemite Stereograph Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting Yosemite Valley's iconic landscapes and geological formations, primarily created in the mid-to-late 1800s for mass-market stereoscopic viewing.

label: Roman Imperial Portrait Coins
description: These coins feature portraits of Roman emperors and imperial family members spanning from the 1st to 4th centuries CE, serving as official currency and propaganda tools that disseminated the likenesses of rulers throughout the empire.

label: Victorian-Era Decorative Valentines
description: These objects are primarily 19th-century English and American valentine cards featuring romantic sentiments, created using mixed media techniques including lithography, collage, hand-coloring, and embossing on decorative paper stocks.

label: African Cultural Objects and Artifacts
description: These objects represent diverse African cultures and traditions, including sculptures, functional items, and ritual objects created from materials like wood, bronze, and iron, primarily held in museum collections focused on African heritage.

label: European Art Drawings Collection
description: These objects are predominantly works on paper from the European Art department, featuring figure studies, landscapes, and narrative scenes created primarily through drawing media such as chalk, graphite, ink, and watercolor across multiple European countries.

label: American Lithographic Prints Collection
description: This collection consists of American prints from the late 19th/early 20th century, predominantly created using lithographic techniques (transfer lithography, lithotint, and drypoint), featuring domestic scenes, portraits, and figure studies.

label: Early 20th Century French Postcards
description: These are photomechanical postcards from late 19th to early 20th century France depicting regional landmarks, natural sites, monuments, and local scenes across various French regions (Auvergne, Provence, Languedoc, etc.).

label: American Gelatin Silver Prints
description: A collection of gelatin silver photographic prints from American culture held in the Getty Museum's photography department, spanning diverse subjects from portraits and documentary scenes to natural and industrial landscapes.

label: European Edged Weapons and Polearms
description: A diverse collection of swords, axes, halberds, and other hand weapons from European and Asian cultures spanning from ancient times through the 18th century, crafted primarily from steel, iron, and wood.

label: Architectural and Urban Landscape Prints
description: These prints depict iconic buildings, bridges, streets, and landmarks from American and European cities, created primarily through etching and lithography techniques during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

label: Louis XIV and Marie-Thérèse Commemorative Tokens
description: These are French numismatic tokens (jetons) from the 1660s commemorating the marriage and reign of King Louis XIV and Queen Marie-Thérèse d'Autriche, made primarily from copper and brass materials.

label: Tokaido Fifty-Three Stations Prints
description: Japanese woodblock prints depicting individual stations along the historic Tokaido Road trade route, primarily from the Edo period with multiple artistic interpretations of the same series.

label: Topographical Landscape Drawings
description: A collection of drawings depicting specific European cities, castles, and landscapes, primarily from the Dutch Golden Age and later periods, executed on paper using various drawing media.

label: Pages from Gotha Missal
description: These objects are individual folios from a single illuminated medieval manuscript—the Gotha Missal—a Parisian liturgical text created with ink, tempera, and gold on vellum.

label: French Sculpture Collections Paris Museums
description: These objects are sculptures from French cultural institutions in Paris, primarily held in the Petit Palais and Musée d'Art Moderne de Paris, created in various materials including bronze, wood, and terra cotta.

label: Crimean War Military Photography
description: These objects document British military personnel and locations from the Crimean War (1853-1856), captured through early photographic techniques including salted paper and albumen prints.

label: Dutch Historical Portrait Prints
description: A collection of printed portraits of notable Dutch figures spanning from the 17th to 19th centuries, created using engraving, etching, lithography, and other printmaking techniques on paper.

label: Dutch Delftware Ceramics
description: These objects are tin-glazed earthenware vessels and decorative items produced primarily in the Delft region of the Netherlands during the 17th-18th centuries, representing the distinctive blue and white pottery tradition that became internationally renowned.

label: Dutch naturalistic photography collection
description: A comprehensive collection of early photographic studies from the Rijksmuseum documenting Dutch nature, wildlife, plants, rural life, and people using photographic paper and baryta paper techniques.

label: 19th-century Niagara Falls photography
description: These objects are stereograph cards and photographs documenting Niagara Falls and its surrounding landscape, primarily created as albumen silver prints in the American and New York regions during the 1800s for popular viewing and tourism.

label: French Oil Paintings Modern Art
description: Collection of French paintings from modern art museums, predominantly created with oil paint and housed in contemporary art departments across Paris institutions.

label: 19th-century European architectural albumen prints
description: A collection of photographs documenting European architectural landmarks and landscapes, primarily from Italy and the Mediterranean coast, produced using the albumen print process on paper and cardboard supports.

label: French decorative design drawings
description: A collection of French graphic art designs for luxury objects and accessories (jewelry, combs, utensils, perfume labels) from the Petit Palais museum, primarily executed in graphite, watercolor, and gouache on paper.

label: Victorian Era Card Photographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print card photographs from the 19th century featuring portraits of unidentified subjects across multiple cultures, primarily using the popular carte de visite format.

label: Dutch Historical Commemorative Medals
description: These objects are primarily Dutch history medals and commemorative medals from the Rijksmuseum collection, documenting significant events, institutions, and figures in Netherlands history from the 17th-19th centuries.

label: French Drawings Late 19th-Early 20th Century
description: A collection of French graphic works primarily executed in black crayon and ink on paper, dating from the late 19th to early 20th century, featuring figurative and architectural subjects by the same artist.

label: Illuminated Manuscript Folios from Single Volume
description: These objects are individual pages from a single 15th-century French illuminated Book of Hours produced in Rouen, characterized by tempera and gold leaf decoration on vellum.

label: Mythological Prints: Gods and Allegories
description: This collection consists primarily of early modern engravings depicting classical Greek and Roman deities and mythological scenes, predominantly created on paper using engraving techniques across European artistic centers.

label: Japanese Woodblock-Printed Books
description: These objects are primarily illustrated books and prints from Japan created using woodblock printing techniques, spanning from the 17th to 19th centuries and encompassing diverse subjects from design guides to narrative literature.

label: Dutch Golden Age Portrait Etchings
description: These prints are predominantly 17th-century Dutch etchings and drypoints, many depicting prominent religious and intellectual figures of the Dutch Golden Age, particularly preachers of the Remonstrants and scholars, created using intaglio techniques on various paper types.

label: Louvre Palace Architectural Decoration Prints
description: These are salted paper prints documenting sculptural decorations and architectural elements that adorned the Louvre Palace in Paris, created by various French sculptors during the 19th century.

label: Nineteenth-Century American Political Portraits
description: A collection of salted paper print portraits of American political figures from the Civil War era, all housed in the Getty Museum's photography department.

label: German Modernist Photography Collection
description: These gelatin silver prints from German cultural institutions represent early-to-mid 20th century modernist photography, documenting industrial subjects, experimental photographic techniques, portraiture, and avant-garde artistic movements including the Bauhaus.

label: Apostles and Evangelists Prints
description: A collection of religious prints depicting the twelve apostles and four evangelists of Christian tradition, created through engraving and etching techniques on paper across multiple European cultural regions.

label: Early Photographic Processes
description: These objects are pioneering examples of early photographic techniques including salted paper prints, photogenic drawings, and paper negatives, primarily created by British photographers in the 1840s.

label: Islamic Manuscript and Calligraphic Arts
description: These objects represent the rich tradition of Islamic book arts, including decorated Qur'anic folios, Persian literary manuscripts, and illuminated pages created across Islamic regions from North Africa to Central Asia, united by their use of calligraphy, gold leaf, and artistic illustration on paper or parchment.

label: Byzantine Trebizond Silver Coins
description: A collection of unattributed silver coins from the Byzantine Empire's Trebizond region, held in the Getty Museum's numismatic collection.

label: French Photography Collection
description: A series of photographic objects from French cultural heritage preserved in the Palais Gallie department of Paris Museums.

label: Dutch Maritime Etchings and Prints
description: A collection of Dutch Golden Age prints and designs, predominantly etchings on paper, depicting various ships, vessels, and maritime scenes that showcase the Netherlands' significant naval and maritime heritage.

label: Hill & Adamson Scottish Portraits
description: These are salted paper prints from paper negatives created by the pioneering Scottish photography partnership of David Octavius Hill and Robert Adamson in the 1840s, primarily depicting notable Scottish intellectuals, artists, and public figures.

label: South Italian Terracotta Production
description: These objects are terracotta molds and figurine fragments from ancient South Italy (primarily Tarentum and Campania), used in the mass production of Greek-inspired decorative and votive figures.

label: Hollandia East Indiaman shipwreck artifacts
description: These objects are all recovered artifacts from the wreck of the Dutch East Indiaman Hollandia, a 17th-century merchant vessel, including personal items, weapons, cargo fixtures, and ballast materials.

label: Medieval Mediterranean Luxury Textiles
description: These are fragmented silk and metal-thread textiles from Spain and Italy (13th-17th centuries) that represent high-status woven fabrics including damasks, brocades, and lampas weaves used in ecclesiastical and aristocratic contexts.

label: French political satire medals
description: A collection of satirical and humorous medals from the late 18th to late 19th century that critiqued French political events, including the Revolution, Republic, elections, and key political figures.

label: Stereograph Photography Collection
description: These objects are all stereographs—a popular 19th-century photographic format using albumen or gelatin silver prints that depict American and Caribbean landscapes, architectural views, botanical subjects, and still life arrangements intended for three-dimensional viewing.

label: Early photographic portraiture prints
description: These objects are primarily albumen silver prints from the Getty's photograph collection depicting individual and group portraits from the 19th century, representing the dominant photographic printing technique of that era.

label: Belle Époque Entertainment Prints
description: These are French lithographic prints from the late 19th century depicting performers, entertainers, and scenes from Parisian cafés, cabarets, theaters, and music halls during the Belle Époque period.

label: Eighteenth-century Roman archaeological etchings
description: These are prints, predominantly etchings from the 18th century, documenting ancient Roman architectural ruins and archaeological remains in Italy, created to study and document classical antiquity.

label: 16th Century Dutch Engravings
description: A collection of prints and engravings primarily from the Netherlands during the 16th century, predominantly created through the engraving technique on laid paper, featuring allegorical, mythological, historical, and portrait subjects.

label: 19th Century Albumen Print Photographs
description: A collection of albumen print photographs from the mid-to-late 1800s featuring prominent individuals, performers, clergy, and military figures, primarily in portrait formats including cartes de visite, cabinet cards, and stereocards.

label: 19th Century French Works on Paper
description: This collection comprises French drawings, watercolors, and sketches from the 18th-20th centuries, predominantly featuring figurative, religious, and landscape subjects executed on paper using various media including graphite, charcoal, watercolor, and pastel.

label: European Lace and Embroidered Borders
description: These textile objects are decorative borders from European cultures (primarily Italy, Flanders, and England) created using lace-making and embroidery techniques on linen and other fibers for functional applications like altar cloths and table covers.

label: Early Photography of Japan
description: These objects are albumen silver prints and colored collotypes depicting Japanese daily life, culture, and landscapes from the late 19th century, representing early Western photographic documentation of Japan.

label: Commercial advertising cartes-de-visite
description: These are carte-de-visite cards (small photographic prints on cardboard) from the 19th century that served as advertisements for businesses, products, and services, often featuring portraits of owners, celebrities, or product images.

label: Victorian albumen print photographs
description: These objects are primarily 19th-century albumen silver print photographs (mostly stereographs) depicting Scottish landmarks, landscapes, and cultural sites including castles, monuments, and scenic locations associated with Scottish heritage and literary figures.

label: 19th Century American Sketch Studies
description: A collection of 19th-century graphite drawings from Harvard's collection featuring dual-sided sketches that document American landscapes, figures, and architectural studies, reflecting the working practices of 19th-century American artists.

label: Chinese Snuff Bottles Collection
description: These objects are decorative snuff bottles from China made from various semi-precious stones, crystals, and glass, all held in the Asian Art Department and classified as utilitarian tools from a specific historical period.

label: French Guild and Municipal Tokens
description: These are French numismatic tokens (jetons) from the 17th-18th centuries representing various trades, guilds, and civic offices during the reigns of Louis XIV, XV, and XVI.

label: Engravings of Notable Dutch Figures
description: A collection of portrait engravings created in Amsterdam, featuring prominent figures from Dutch society including clergy, nobility, military officers, and scholars from the 17th-18th centuries.

label: East Asian Tomb Figurines
description: These objects are earthenware and ceramic figurines from Chinese and East Asian tombs, primarily from the Tang Dynasty and earlier periods, depicting court officials, musicians, warriors, attendants, and animals that were placed in burial chambers to serve the deceased in the afterlife.

label: French Parish Church Méreaux Tokens
description: These are brass, copper, and lead tokens (méreaux) from Parisian churches and confraternities dating from the Middle Ages through the Ancien Régime, used for administrative or charitable purposes within religious institutions.

label: Early American Portrait Photography
description: These objects are cased photographic portraits from the 19th century, primarily using ambrotype and tintype processes, predominantly depicting seated figures from American culture.

label: Bartsch catalogue etchings collection
description: These are French original etchings from the Paris Museums catalogued in the Bartsch reference system, featuring landscape and genre scenes with rural and architectural subjects.

label: Oil paintings from Rijksmuseum collection
description: All objects are oil paintings on canvas or panel from the Rijksmuseum's collection, spanning Dutch and Flemish art from various periods including portraits, landscapes, genre scenes, and historical subjects.

label: French Historical Figures Engraved Portraits
description: A collection of engravings and etchings depicting prominent French political, religious, and intellectual figures from the 16th-18th centuries, created through interpretive and original printmaking techniques.

label: Early Italian Photography Prints
description: A collection of 19th-century albumen silver prints documenting iconic Italian architectural and landscape landmarks, primarily produced by British photographers during the early age of photographic travel documentation.

label: Napoleonic Imperial Commemorative Medals
description: Collection of French numismatic medals and seals celebrating Napoleon I and Napoleon III's imperial rule, key political events, and military achievements during the 19th century.

label: Mexican Broadside Prints
description: These are relief-printed Mexican broadsides from the late 19th and early 20th centuries, combining woodcut and letterpress techniques to create popular narratives about social events, political satire, and everyday life.

label: Goya's Satirical Print Series
description: These objects are prints from Francisco Goya's satirical series (Los Caprichos, Los Proverbios, and Disparates) created in 18th-19th century Spain, characterized by etching and aquatint techniques used to critique social follies and human vices.

label: Ancient Mediterranean Amphora Vessels
description: These objects are amphorae—large storage and transport vessels from ancient Greek, South Italian, and Roman cultures—made primarily of terracotta and ceramic materials, representing a fundamental vessel type used across Mediterranean civilizations for storing and transporting goods.

label: Dutch Educational Prints for Youth
description: These are 18th-century Dutch prints featuring moralizing verses and narratives designed to educate and entertain children through visual storytelling and didactic poetry.

label: Ancient Egyptian Art and Objects
description: These objects represent diverse forms of Egyptian artistic and funerary practices spanning from the Old Kingdom through the Greco-Roman period, including sculptures, reliefs, architectural elements, and religious artifacts.

label: Stereograph photographs of daily life
description: A collection of stereograph images using albumen silver print technology, depicting posed scenes of 19th-century social activities, leisure, and domestic life.

label: Historical Velvet Textiles
description: A collection of velvet fabric artifacts spanning from the 15th century to the 19th century, originating primarily from Italy, Spain, China, Turkey, and France, showcasing various velvet production techniques including cut, voided, and brocaded methods.

label: West African Textile Traditions
description: These objects represent the diverse fiber arts and clothing traditions of West African cultures, primarily made from cotton and featuring various techniques including weaving, dyeing, embroidery, and needlework.

label: Commemorative Medals from Museums
description: These objects are medals made primarily of bronze or gilt bronze, created across the 18th-20th centuries in Europe and America, held in major museum decorative arts departments and used to commemorate historical events, notable figures, and cultural achievements.

label: Italian Renaissance Study Drawings
description: These are preparatory sketches and studies from Italian Renaissance and Baroque periods, predominantly featuring religious and mythological subjects on paper with recto-verso compositions using chalk, ink, and watercolor techniques.

label: Landscape Etchings and Intaglio Prints
description: These works are primarily landscape-focused prints created using etching and related intaglio techniques, depicting scenic locations across North America and Europe.

label: 19th Century American Wood Engravings
description: All objects are wood engravings created in the United States during the 19th century, depicting scenes of daily American life, leisure activities, notable figures, and historical events.

label: Madonna and Child Religious Prints
description: A collection of devotional prints from Northern European and Italian workshops depicting the Virgin Mary with Christ Child and various accompanying saints, created primarily through engraving and etching techniques on paper.

label: Embroidered Textile Samplers
description: These objects are needlework samplers from multiple cultures and centuries, created using embroidery techniques on various fabric bases to demonstrate stitching skills and patterns.

label: 19th Century French Sculpture
description: This collection comprises primarily sculptural works created in or associated with France during the 19th century, featuring portrait busts, figurative studies, and allegorical pieces executed in materials such as bronze, marble, plaster, and ivory.

label: Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte commemorative medals
description: A collection of French numismatic medals documenting key events, public ceremonies, and political moments in the life and presidency of Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte from 1848-1852.

label: European Decorative Drinking Vessels
description: A collection of goblets, wine glasses, and punch bowls from various European regions (Germany, Netherlands, Bohemia, England, France, Austria, Italy) spanning from the 16th-19th centuries, made from glass or silver and often featuring decorative techniques like gilding, engraving, and enameling.

label: Central Park stereograph documentation
description: These albumen silver print stereographs from the Getty and Art Institute document Central Park's landmarks, scenic features, and recreational spaces in 19th-century New York through a popular Victorian-era photographic format.

label: Italian Renaissance and Baroque Studies
description: These objects are primarily drawings and paintings from Italy spanning the Renaissance through Baroque periods, featuring figure studies, religious narratives, allegorical scenes, and landscape studies that demonstrate the artistic traditions of Italian master workshops.

label: Black and White Silver Gelatin Prints
description: A comprehensive collection of mid-20th century French photographic works executed as black and white silver gelatin prints, encompassing portraiture, fashion, landscapes, and documentary subjects.

label: Japanese Netsuke and Miniature Carvings
description: These objects are small decorative Japanese carved accessories, primarily netsuke (toggle fasteners for hanging pouches), made from various materials including wood, ivory, and antler, dating from Japan's feudal and edo periods.

label: Italian Baroque Prints and Etchings
description: A collection of predominantly 17th-century Italian prints created through etching techniques, featuring decorative, narrative, and ornamental subject matter from the Baroque period.

label: Ancient Greek Silver Coinage
description: These objects are silver coins from ancient Greek and Hellenistic kingdoms, primarily tetradrachms and related denominations, featuring deities, rulers, and mythological figures from various Greek city-states and dynasties.

label: Tuti-Nama Manuscript Illustrations
description: These paintings are individual folios from a Mughal Indian illustrated manuscript of the Tuti-Nama (Tales of a Parrot), a Persian narrative text where a magical parrot tells stories to entertain and counsel a merchant's wife over fifty nights.

label: Chinese and Japanese Porcelain Decorative Vessels
description: These objects are glazed porcelain containers from China and Japan, predominantly from the Ming and Qing periods, featuring blue-and-white or polychrome decoration with botanical, figural, and auspicious motifs.

label: Portrait Prints of Historical Figures
description: A collection of engraved and lithographed portrait prints depicting notable American historical figures, political leaders, and dignitaries from the colonial and early United States periods.

label: Hindu Epic and Devotional Narrative Art
description: These objects are paintings and prints depicting scenes from Hindu sacred texts including the Ramayana, Bhagavata Purana, and Gita Govinda, created across various Indian regional schools and traditions from the medieval period through the 19th century.

label: Ancient Greek Wine-Mixing Vessels
description: These objects are terracotta kraters from ancient Greece and South Italy, primarily decorated with red-figure and black-figure techniques, used ceremonially for mixing wine and water in symposium contexts.

label: Functional Vessels Across Cultures
description: This collection comprises utilitarian ceramic, glass, and metal vessels—jugs, jars, mugs, and related containers—made across Europe and America from the 16th to 19th centuries, representing everyday domestic and commercial use.

label: Decorative Porcelain and Ceramics
description: This collection consists of woodburytype photographic prints documenting ornamental ceramic objects, including vases, tableware, figurines, and decorative vessels, likely representing a museum's decorative arts collection.

label: 19th Century Waterfall Stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting North American waterfalls and cascades, primarily created during the American Civil War era as popular landscape photography subjects for stereoscopic viewing.

label: Paris Medical Faculty Commemorative Tokens
description: A series of copper and metal tokens (jetons) issued across the 17th and 18th centuries to commemorate the deans of the Faculty of Medicine of Paris, serving as official records and insignia of academic leadership.

label: Decorated handkerchiefs across cultures
description: These objects are handkerchiefs and kerchiefs from diverse cultures (Europe, Asia, Americas, Africa) spanning multiple centuries, united by their function as personal textiles enhanced through various decorative techniques including embroidery, lace-work, and printed or drawn-thread embellishments.

label: Wood Engraving Prints Collection
description: All objects are prints created using wood engraving technique, housed in the same museum department and representing a diverse range of religious, historical, and portrait subjects from a unified collection.

label: Ancient Terracotta Figurative Sculpture
description: These objects are predominantly terracotta sculptures depicting human figures—heads, full-body statues, and mythological subjects—created across Greek, Roman, and Cypriot cultures from antiquity through the Graeco-Roman period.

label: 16th-17th Century Female Portrait Prints
description: A collection of etched and engraved prints from German, Antwerp, and London workshops depicting women's portraits with detailed attention to contemporary fashionable headwear, collars, and accessories.

label: Historical coins from global empires
description: A collection of coins from various empires and nations spanning the 16th to 20th centuries, representing monetary systems from Europe, Asia, Africa, and colonial territories across the Dutch, Japanese, Spanish, Portuguese, and other imperial powers.

label: Japanese Woodblock Birds and Flowers
description: These objects are Japanese color woodblock prints depicting birds perched on or flying near flowering plants and trees, representing a popular genre of decorative prints from the Edo and Meiji periods.

label: Mid-Century American Aviation Photography
description: A collection of gelatin silver prints documenting American aircraft, pilots, and aviation operations from the mid-20th century, including military planes, Snowbirds pilots, and aircraft manufacturing details.

label: Ancient Greek Attic Pottery
description: These objects are terracotta vase fragments from ancient Attica featuring distinctive red-figure and black-figure decorative techniques, representing a major tradition of Greek ceramic production.

label: Parisian Landmarks and Streets
description: These graphic art prints document the architecture, streets, bridges, and notable buildings of Paris through various printmaking techniques including etching, lithography, and woodcut.

label: Turner's Liber Studiorum Prints
description: These are plates from J.M.W. Turner's Liber Studiorum, an ambitious 19th-century English print series combining landscape, historical, and mythological subjects using etching and engraving techniques.

label: Kalpa-sutra Manuscript Folios
description: These objects are illustrated manuscript pages from the Kalpa-sutra, a sacred Jain text depicting the life of Mahavira, created primarily in 15th-century Gujarat using traditional painting techniques.

label: English Landscape Etchings by Seymour Haden
description: A collection of landscape and architectural prints from 19th-century England created primarily through etching and drypoint techniques, depicting rural scenes, farms, cottages, and English waterways.

label: Miao Traditional Jackets
description: These objects are traditional jackets from the Miao culture of China, characterized by silk and cotton construction often embellished with decorative elements like sequins, beads, and metal accents.

label: 19th Century Washington D.C. Stereographs
description: A collection of stereograph photographs documenting American government buildings and monuments in Washington D.C., created primarily using albumen silver print technology during the mid-to-late 1800s.

label: Asian Personal Adornment Jewelry
description: These objects are decorative jewelry and accessories from South, Southeast, and East Asian cultures, spanning from ancient periods to the 19th century, made primarily from precious metals and gemstones for personal wear.

label: Japanese Edo Period Sword Guards
description: A collection of iron and metal tsuba (sword guards) from Japan's Edo period (1615-1868), featuring both plain and decoratively carved designs with natural and mythological motifs.

label: European Printmaking Techniques
description: This collection comprises European prints from the 15th-20th centuries created using various printmaking techniques including woodcut, etching, engraving, lithography, and aquatint, held in museum departments of European art.

label: European Decorative Textiles
description: A collection of functional and ornamental textile objects from various European regions spanning multiple centuries, including panels, curtains, tapestries, and accessories made with diverse techniques such as embroidery, weaving, printing, and lace-making.

label: Victorian Era Portrait Photography
description: These objects are photographs and prints from 19th-century Britain and England featuring portraits of notable Victorian figures, created using early photographic techniques such as albumen prints, carbon prints, and photogravure.

label: Magdalénian Bone Carved Objects
description: Upper Paleolithic bone and antler artifacts from the Magdalénian period decorated with incised and engraved markings, held in French archaeological museum collections.

label: Japanese Buddhist Religious Art
description: These objects are sculptures and paintings from Japanese museums depicting Buddhist deities, bodhisattvas, and religious iconography spanning the Heian through Edo periods.

label: Parisian Architectural Etchings Series
description: These prints comprise a 19th-century series of etchings depicting iconic Parisian landmarks and streets, primarily featuring Notre-Dame Cathedral and its surrounding urban architecture.

label: East Asian Reflective Metalware
description: These objects are mirrors from China, Japan, and India spanning multiple dynasties, primarily crafted from bronze, jade, or lead, serving both functional and decorative purposes with elaborate designs featuring mythological creatures and celestial motifs.

label: French rural life documentary sketches
description: A collection of graphite drawings from the mid-20th century documenting provincial French landscapes, architecture, and rural occupations across different regions of France.

label: Wisconsin Dells Landscape Photography
description: These objects are photographs and stereographs documenting the natural landscapes, geological formations, and tourist attractions of Wisconsin Dells and surrounding Wisconsin locations from the 19th century.

label: French Social Commentary Prints
description: A collection of French prints from the late 19th and early 20th centuries using lithography and etching techniques to depict and critique social issues, war, politics, and everyday life.

label: English Gold Coins Through Centuries
description: A comprehensive collection of gold coins from English monarchs spanning from the Anglo-Saxon period through the Victorian era, showcasing the evolution of royal coinage denominations and design conventions.

label: Ancient Coinage with Deity Imagery
description: These objects are ancient coins from Mediterranean and Near Eastern civilizations featuring depictions of gods, goddesses, animals, and symbolic imagery used as currency and cultural markers.

label: Asian Edged Weapons and Components
description: These objects are bladed weapons and related components from South, Southeast, and East Asian cultures, spanning from the Bronze Age to the 20th century, including daggers, swords, kris blades, and their sheaths and scabbards.

label: Flight into Egypt Biblical Narrative
description: These objects are artistic representations of the biblical narrative of the Flight into Egypt, depicting the Holy Family's escape to Egypt, rendered across multiple mediums and periods primarily from European institutions.

label: Edward Curtis North American Indian Project
description: These photogravure prints and photographs document Native American tribes and cultures, primarily from Edward Curtis's monumental "The North American Indian" photographic survey created in the early 20th century.

label: Shawls Across Cultures and Centuries
description: These objects are shawls and shawl fragments from diverse geographic origins (India, Kashmir, Paisley, France, Scotland, Peru) spanning the 18th-19th centuries, representing a globally significant textile form produced through various weaving and embroidery techniques.

label: Thomas Annan's Glasgow Documentation
description: These objects are photographs and prints documenting Glasgow's old closes and streets, primarily created by Scottish photographer Thomas Annan for the Glasgow City Improvement Trust between 1868-1899.

label: Japanese Gelatin Silver Prints
description: A collection of gelatin silver print photographs from Japanese culture held at the Getty, documenting landscapes, cultural practices, architecture, and human experiences across various prefectures and time periods.

label: 19th Century American Wood Engravings
description: A collection of wood-engraved prints from the United States depicting scenes of daily life, theatrical performances, maritime incidents, and notable figures, likely published in illustrated periodicals or newspapers during the 19th century.

label: Albumen silver prints of classical sculpture
description: A collection of 19th-century albumen silver photographic prints documenting classical and neoclassical sculptures, primarily from Vatican collections and European museums, featuring mythological and allegorical subjects.

label: Ashtasahasrika Prajnaparamita Manuscript
description: These objects are folios and covers from a single illuminated Buddhist manuscript of the Perfection of Wisdom in Eight Thousand Lines, a foundational Mahayana Buddhist text, preserved at the Cleveland Museum of Art.

label: Rembrandt's Biblical Narrative Prints
description: These objects are etchings and drypoints depicting scenes from the Passion of Christ and other biblical narratives, primarily created by Rembrandt van Rijn in 17th-century Holland using intaglio printmaking techniques.

label: Egyptian Funerary Worker Figures
description: These are shabti figurines from ancient Egypt, small sculptured servants placed in tombs to perform labor for the deceased in the afterlife.

label: Documentary Photography Collection
description: A curated collection of black and white photographs from the mid-20th century by French photographers documenting diverse subjects including social conditions, cultural practices, and human experiences across Europe, North Africa, Asia, and the Middle East.

label: Civil War Documentary Photography
description: These albumen prints document American Civil War battlefields, fortifications, military installations, and casualties across Virginia and Pennsylvania, primarily from Alexander Gardner's photographic survey of the conflict.

label: Magdalenian Engraved Reindeer Bone Tools
description: A collection of semi-rounded bone rods carved from reindeer antler during the Magdalenian period (Middle to Upper), primarily decorated with engraving and incision techniques as functional or symbolic Upper Paleolithic implements.

label: German Religious Prints and Engravings
description: These objects are devotional prints from German-speaking regions depicting saints, apostles, and religious figures created through engraving, woodcut, and etching techniques.

label: Dutch Hard-Paste Porcelain Tableware
description: This collection consists of domestic porcelain vessels and tableware produced by Dutch porcelain manufactories (primarily Oud-Loosdrecht, Weesp, and Amstel) during the late 18th century, featuring hand-painted decoration and hard-paste porcelain construction.

label: American and European Silver Tableware
description: These objects are decorative silver vessels and serving pieces from 18th-19th century American and European tea and coffee services, representing domestic luxury metalwork traditions.

label: Ancient Egyptian Vessels
description: These objects are containers and vessels from ancient Egypt spanning multiple periods, made from diverse materials including stone, ceramic, and faience, and used for storage, cosmetics, and ceremonial purposes.

label: French numismatic dies and stamps
description: Steel coins and punches used to strike French medals and currency from the 18th-19th centuries, featuring portraits of French royalty, political figures, and national symbols.

label: American Color Photographic Prints
description: A collection of chromogenic color prints from the Getty Museum's photograph collection documenting American landscapes, architecture, and everyday scenes from the late 20th century.

label: Pastoral and Etched Landscapes
description: A collection of prints on paper from the Rijksmuseum primarily depicting rural and pastoral landscapes with figures such as herders, travelers, and animals, predominantly created using etching and engraving techniques.

label: Miao Culture Baby Carriers
description: These textile objects are embellished baby carriers created by Miao communities in China, crafted from cotton and silk using needlework and decorative elements like metallic threads, sequins, and studs.

label: Archbishop Affre's Death 1848
description: Commemorative medals depicting the death of Denis Affre, Archbishop of Paris, who was killed on the barricades during the June Uprising of 1848.

label: American Urban Photography Mid-20th Century
description: These objects are gelatin silver prints depicting American cities—primarily New York and New Orleans—capturing urban architecture, street scenes, and cultural events during the modernist era.

label: German Medieval Manuscript Folios
description: These objects are folios from a German medieval manuscript collection, primarily featuring illustrated scenes of animals, fables, and human activities rendered in ink and watercolor.

label: Japanese Ceramics and Vessels
description: This collection comprises decorative and functional vessels from Japan spanning the Muromachi through Meiji periods, primarily featuring porcelain and stoneware with various glazing and decoration techniques including underglaze blue, overglaze enamels, and natural ash glazes.

label: English Textile Panels
description: A collection of decorative textile panels from England, created using various techniques including embroidery, printing, weaving, and brocading across multiple fiber types from the ARTIC museum collection.

label: Cigarette Card Collectibles
description: These are promotional cigarette and tobacco cards issued by major tobacco companies in the late 19th century, featuring various subjects from actresses to natural history to serve as packaging inserts.

label: American Stereograph Albumen Prints
description: These objects are stereographs created using albumen silver print technology, a popular 19th-century format for viewing paired photographic images through a stereoscope viewer.

label: Ancient Chinese Bronze Ritual Vessels
description: These objects are ceremonial vessels from ancient China (primarily Shang and Zhou dynasties) made of bronze, used for ritual purposes including wine serving, food offerings, and religious ceremonies.

label: Ancient Glass and Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are functional containers—bottles, flasks, and vessels—created across Mediterranean and Near Eastern cultures from the Roman period through medieval times, primarily using glass-working techniques like blowing and mold-blowing.

label: Buddhist Sutra Manuscript Folios
description: These objects are individual pages from a single illuminated Buddhist manuscript of the Ashtasahasrika Prajnaparamita-sutra (Perfection of Wisdom in Eight Thousand Lines), a foundational Mahayana Buddhist text.

label: Merovingian Period Archaeological Finds
description: Collection of artifacts from the Merovingian period (6th-7th centuries) primarily consisting of metalwork, weapons, and ornamental objects housed in the Val-d'Oise Archaeological Museum.

label: French Revolutionary Era Tokens
description: Collection of metal tokens (jetons) from France and related territories dating 1790-1801, documenting revolutionary ideals, institutional affiliations, and commemorative events during the French Revolution and its immediate aftermath.

label: Hollstein Catalogue Portrait Engravings
description: A collection of original and interpretive engravings from the 16th-17th centuries, primarily created using etching and burlins techniques, catalogued in the Hollstein reference work and held by Paris museums.

label: Giant Sequoia Stereograph Documentation
description: A collection of 19th-century stereograph photographs documenting California's giant sequoia groves (Mammoth Trees) in Calaveras and Mariposa counties, primarily using albumen silver print technology to showcase the scale and majesty of these ancient trees.

label: Goya's War and Conflict Series
description: These are prints by Francisco Goya documenting the horrors of war and human suffering, primarily from his series on the Peninsular War and broader commentaries on violence and conflict.

label: Buddhist and Hindu Religious Sculptures
description: These objects are sculptural representations of Buddhist and Hindu deities and spiritual figures from South and Southeast Asian cultures, spanning multiple centuries and created in various materials including stone, bronze, and wood.

label: French political lithographic satire
description: These objects are French lithographic prints from the early 19th century that use humor, caricature, and social commentary to address contemporary political events, military affairs, and social behaviors of the period.

label: 17th Century Dutch Animal Studies
description: A collection of etched and engraved prints from the Dutch Golden Age depicting pastoral scenes with livestock, herds, and rural laborers in naturalistic settings.

label: 17th Century German Mythological Etchings
description: A series of German etched prints from the 17th century depicting scenes from classical mythology, literary narratives, and cultural subjects, primarily from Ovid's Metamorphoses.

label: 18th-century European dress garments
description: These objects are primarily caps, coats, waistcoats, and bodices from the 18th century, predominantly from European origins (France, England, Germany) and America, representing fashionable upper-class clothing constructed from silk, cotton, linen, and embellished with embroidery and metallic threads.

label: Ancient Asian Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are functional ceramic and pottery vessels from various Asian cultures spanning from the Neolithic period to the 15th century, including storage jars, bowls, and decorative containers from China, Korea, Thailand, Vietnam, and Japan.

label: British Staffordshire Ceramics
description: These objects are predominantly lead-glazed earthenware ceramics from Staffordshire, England, including functional tableware and decorative figures that exemplify 18th and 19th-century British pottery manufacturing traditions.

label: 19th-century New England stereograph views
description: These objects are primarily stereographs documenting landmarks, public spaces, and notable buildings in Boston and surrounding Massachusetts and Maine communities during the mid-to-late 1800s.

label: Early French Photography Collection
description: A collection of French photographs from the Petit Palais museum, primarily from the late 19th and early 20th centuries, predominantly featuring glass negatives and documentary images of sculptures, architectural details, domestic scenes, and portraits.

label: Figurative Studies and Character Sketches
description: A collection of Dutch Golden Age and Renaissance drawings depicting human figures and characters in various poses and activities, primarily executed in ink and pen on paper.

label: Western Survey Exploration Photography
description: These objects are albumen prints and stereographs documenting geological formations, Native American peoples, and settlements from U.S. government surveys of the American West, particularly the territories west of the 100th Meridian during the late 19th century.

label: Early 20th Century Chicago Architecture
description: These objects are architectural drawings and plans primarily from Chicago and the surrounding region, created during the early 1900s, including notable buildings, civic projects, and the influential Plan of Chicago 1909.

label: American Printmaking Across Centuries
description: These objects are all prints from American and North American cultural traditions spanning from the 19th to 21st centuries, created using diverse printmaking techniques including lithography, etching, screenprinting, and digital methods.

label: French Commemorative and Portrait Medals
description: This collection consists of French medals from the 18th-20th centuries, primarily cast in bronze or silvered bronze, commemorating historical events, notable figures, institutions, and civic achievements of France.

label: Roman Bronze Everyday Objects
description: These artifacts are predominantly small-scale bronze implements, tools, and functional objects from Roman and ancient cultures used for daily activities, craftsmanship, and commerce.

label: Scottish Albumen Silver Prints
description: A collection of 19th-century albumen silver print photographs predominantly depicting Scottish cultural subjects, landscapes, and portrait subjects from the Getty Museum's photograph collection.

label: American Seating Furniture
description: These objects are primarily chairs, armchairs, and seating furniture crafted by American makers from the 18th and 19th centuries, predominantly from major cultural centers like New York, Boston, Philadelphia, and Newport.

label: Northern Renaissance Religious Prints
description: A collection of devotional prints from German and Netherlandish artists of the 15th-16th centuries depicting Christian biblical narratives and saints, primarily using woodcut and engraving techniques.

label: Jacques Callot's Etched Prints
description: A collection of etchings on paper primarily attributed to French artist Jacques Callot, featuring diverse subjects ranging from military scenes and allegorical figures to natural studies and exotic creatures.

label: Chinese Silk Textiles and Garments
description: These objects are decorative and functional textiles from Chinese culture, predominantly featuring silk construction with embroidery, brocading, and gold-leaf embellishment techniques used for both garments and furnishings.

label: Roman Silver Denarius Coins
description: These objects are all silver denarii coins from the Roman Empire or modern reproductions thereof, representing the primary currency of ancient Rome spanning from the Republican period through the Imperial era.

label: Ancient Greek Pouring Vessels
description: These objects are oinochoes and related pitcher forms used for pouring liquids in ancient Mediterranean cultures, spanning from the Geometric period through the Roman era across Greek, Cypriot, Etruscan, and South Italian pottery traditions.

label: Hunting and Trapping Equipment
description: This collection comprises traditional hunting, trapping, and game-catching implements from European and Mediterranean cultures, including various animal traps, ammunition accessories, and hunting calls dating primarily from the 19th-20th centuries.

label: Chinese Porcelain Cups and Saucers
description: A collection of Chinese porcelain tableware featuring decorated cups and saucers made with glazed porcelain, often embellished with gold or cobalt, displaying landscapes, family coats of arms, and ornamental designs for European patrons.

label: German Etched Prints of Architecture and Landscapes
description: These objects are predominantly German-produced etchings (and some aquatints) from the 17th-19th centuries depicting architectural views, gardens, palaces, and theatrical scenes, many after Italian and European monuments.

label: Ancient Greek Water Vessels
description: These objects are hydriae (water jars), a functional vessel form from ancient Greek cultures made primarily of terracotta, decorated with black-figure and red-figure painting techniques.

label: Japanese Hanging Scroll Paintings
description: These objects are all Japanese paintings created primarily in the hanging scroll format using traditional materials such as ink and color on silk or paper, held in the Asian Art department of the museum.

label: American Glass Decorative Objects
description: This collection comprises primarily 19th-century American glass vessels and decorative items, including pressed glass, blown glass, and pattern glass pieces from various cultural contexts such as Pittsburgh and Wheeling traditions, held predominantly in the Department of Arts of the Americas.

label: 18th Century Porcelain Tableware
description: These objects are decorated ceramic tableware pieces, predominantly from Worcester porcelain manufactory, representing 18th-century British and Continental dining service traditions with soft-paste porcelain construction and underglaze blue or polychrome enamel decoration.

label: Stefano della Bella Engravings
description: These are original graphic artworks (estampes) from various numbered series created by or after Stefano della Bella, an Italian-French Baroque printmaker, catalogued by de Vesme and Dutuit references.

label: 19th Century Brazilian Documentary Photography
description: These objects are albumen silver prints from the Getty's photography collection documenting Brazilian landscapes, geographic features, and infrastructure during the colonial and early imperial periods, predominantly featuring Pernambuco and northeastern Brazilian regions.

label: Ancient Portrait Sculpture Heads
description: These objects are portrait heads and busts from ancient Greek and Roman cultures, created primarily in marble and bronze to commemorate rulers, philosophers, and notable citizens.

label: 19th Century Egypt and Nubia Prints
description: A multi-volume set of color lithograph prints documenting Egyptian and Nubian monuments, temples, and landscapes created by English artists during the 19th century.

label: Early American Portrait Photography
description: These objects are portraits from 19th-century America created using early photographic techniques such as tintypes, salted paper prints, and platinum prints, many hand-colored to add personal detail to the subjects.

label: Jeu des reines renommées print series
description: A 52-piece engraving series depicting famous queens from classical antiquity, mythology, and history, catalogued with de Vesme and Dutuit references and housed in Paris museums.

label: French Republican Coins Collection
description: These objects are French coins minted during the Third and Fourth Republics (1873-1950), representing various denominations and metal compositions that document France's monetary history across multiple decades.

label: 19th Century Asian Travel Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver print photographs documenting colonial-era views of China, India, and Singapore, primarily created during the mid-to-late 1800s by Western photographers.

label: Native American Turquoise Jewelry
description: These objects are predominantly Native American jewelry and adornments from the United States featuring turquoise as a primary or secondary material, often combined with silver, shell, and coral in traditional forms like najas, necklaces, and pendants.

label: Georges Michel's Parisian Landscape Studies
description: A collection of pencil drawings by 19th-century French artist Georges Michel depicting rural and urban scenes around Paris, including the Butte Montmartre, the Seine River, and surrounding countryside with figures and vehicles.

label: Northern European Landscape Prints
description: A collection of prints from the Netherlands and Holland, primarily produced through etching and engraving techniques, depicting rural landscapes, pathways, and pastoral scenes from the 16th-17th centuries.

label: French merchant provost tokens
description: A series of brass numismatic tokens commemorating the Prévôts des marchands (merchant provosts), administrative officials of Paris, spanning from the early 17th to late 18th centuries.

label: 19th Century French Professional Badges
description: A collection of metal insignia and medals issued to identify workers and employees across various Parisian professions and institutions during the 19th century, from market porters to omnibus conductors to bank employees.

label: Edo Period Kyōka Poetry Anthology
description: These objects are individual spreads from a multi-volume illustrated collection of kyōka (humorous light verse) depicting scenes of Japanese life during the Edo period, housed at the Cleveland Museum of Art.

label: French gelatin silver print photographs
description: Collection of photographs from Paris museums created using gelatin silver print (tirage gélatino-argentique) technique, spanning diverse subjects from portraits to street scenes and international locations.

label: Roman Imperial Billon Tetradrachms
description: These coins are Roman tetradrachms minted in billon (a silver-copper alloy) during the early 3rd century CE, commemorating emperors Caracalla and Macrinus during a period of significant monetary change in the Roman Empire.

label: Ancient Greek Black-Figure Pottery
description: These objects are all fragmentary terracotta vessels from Attic Greece featuring the characteristic black-figure technique of decoration, specifically belonging to the eye cup vessel form.

label: Italian and French Prints
description: These objects are primarily etchings and engravings from Italian and French origins, predominantly depicting mythological narratives, classical subjects, and allegorical themes created for European art collections.

label: European wedding attire and accessories
description: These objects are primarily decorative and functional items associated with European wedding ceremonies and formal occasions from the 19th-20th centuries, including bridal garments, ceremonial crowns, jewelry, and festive bouquets.

label: Southwestern American Cyanotype Documentation
description: A collection of cyanotype prints from the early 20th century documenting Pueblo communities, Native American cultures, and landscapes of the American Southwest, particularly New Mexico.

label: Photomechanical Prints Collection
description: This is a collection of photomechanical prints (primarily collotype and photochrom processes) from the Rijksmuseum featuring architectural landmarks, cultural monuments, portraits, and notable locations across Europe and America from the late 19th/early 20th century.

label: Chinese Jade Carved Objects
description: These objects are predominantly jade carvings from Chinese dynasties spanning from the Shang dynasty through the Qing dynasty, including pendants, plaques, bowls, and ornamental pieces featuring animals, dragons, birds, and masks.

label: Roman Fresco Fragments
description: These objects are all fragmentary remains of Roman frescoes, a wall painting technique where pigment was applied to wet plaster, now preserved in the Getty Museum's paintings collection.

label: Mirror of Holiness Manuscript Folios
description: These are pages and folios from Father Jerome Xavier's Mir'at al-quds (Mirror of Holiness), a Mughal-era Persian manuscript created in Allahabad for a Mughal prince that combines Christian Gospel narratives with Islamic artistic traditions.

label: Correspondence with Henry Brokman
description: A collection of autograph letters and envelopes addressed to or mentioning Henry Brokman from prominent French cultural figures dating from 1903 to 1914, representing early 20th-century artistic and literary correspondence.

label: Rembrandt's Etching Collection
description: A comprehensive collection of original etchings and engravings by Rembrandt van Rijn, catalogued through the Bartsch numbering system and held in French museum collections, encompassing portraits, biblical scenes, landscapes, and studies of everyday figures.

label: American Quilts 19th Century
description: These objects are American-made quilts from the 19th century, showcasing various construction techniques including piecing, appliqué, and embroidery, held primarily in the Miami and Art Institute of Chicago collections.

label: White Mountains Stereograph Series
description: A collection of 19th-century American stereographic photographs documenting the scenic landscapes, geological formations, and notable landmarks of the White Mountains region in New Hampshire, primarily created as albumen silver prints for stereoscopic viewing.

label: Eadweard Muybridge Motion Studies
