description: These objects are sequential photographic prints from Eadweard Muybridge's groundbreaking motion analysis studies, created using iron salt process photography to document animal and human locomotion through multiple sequential frames.

label: Ancient Greek Stater Coins
description: This collection comprises stater coins from ancient Greek city-states and kingdoms, spanning diverse regions from mainland Greece to South Italy and the Persian Empire, primarily minted in precious metals (gold and silver) between the 5th-4th centuries BCE.

label: Asian Furniture and Woodwork
description: A collection of functional furniture and wooden objects primarily from China, Japan, India, and the Philippines, predominantly from the Ming and Qing dynasties, featuring traditional materials like huanghuali hardwood and showcasing utilitarian and decorative design principles.

label: Dutch Animal Study Prints
description: A collection of etched prints from the Netherlands and Amsterdam depicting domestic livestock and pastoral animals, created primarily on paper using etching techniques.

label: German Renaissance Marian Devotional Prints
description: A collection of early 16th-century German woodcuts and engravings depicting scenes from the Life of the Virgin Mary and related religious subjects, reflecting the Northern Renaissance focus on Marian devotion and narrative biblical cycles.

label: Block-printed textile pattern samples
description: A collection of paper sheets displaying repeating decorative patterns (strooipatronen) created through block printing techniques, featuring florals, foliage, and geometric motifs used as design templates for textile production.

label: 19th Century American Woven Coverlets
description: These objects are decorative bed coverlets produced in the United States during the 19th century, primarily using jacquard looms to create patterned textiles from cotton and wool.

label: Medieval Reliquaries and Sacred Vessels
description: These objects are liturgical containers and devotional items from medieval Europe (11th-16th centuries) designed to house, display, and venerate Christian relics and saints' remains, predominantly crafted from precious metals with decorative techniques like enameling and gilding.

label: Eighteenth-Century French Printed Textiles
description: These furnishing fabrics from French manufacturing centers (primarily Jouy-en-Josas, Nantes, and Rouen) feature narrative, historical, and mythological scenes produced through roller and copperplate printing techniques on cotton and silk during the late 1700s.

label: French Railway Construction Documentation
description: These albumen silver prints document the engineering infrastructure and military units associated with 19th-century French railway construction, including viaducts, stations, tunnels, and the National Guard battalions involved in the project.

label: Ovid's Metamorphoses illustrated prints
description: A series of letterpress prints by Virgilius Solis depicting mythological transformation scenes and narratives from Ovid's Metamorphoses, primarily from the Rijksmuseum collection.

label: Decorative Gold Leaf Papers
description: A collection of ornamental papers from the Rijksmuseum, primarily from Augsburg and German regions, embellished with gold leaf and featuring repetitive patterns of flora, fauna, and decorative motifs used for wrapping and brocade applications.

label: Adam and Eve Biblical Narrative
description: These objects depict scenes from the Book of Genesis featuring Adam and Eve, including their creation, life in Paradise, the forbidden fruit, and expulsion from Paradise across multiple artistic media and European cultures from the 16th-19th centuries.

label: Katsushika Hokusai's Mount Fuji Series
description: These objects are individual pages and volumes from Hokusai's Fugaku hyakkei (One Hundred Views of Mount Fuji), a 19th-century Japanese woodblock print series depicting Mount Fuji from varied perspectives and locations.

label: Double-sided Study Sheets
description: These are drawings and watercolors from European and American artists that feature sketches and studies on both the recto and verso sides of single sheets, serving as working documents for artistic exploration.

label: Miao Silver Torque Neck Rings
description: These are silver torque neck rings from the Miao culture of China, representing traditional adornment jewelry that demonstrates the metalworking expertise and aesthetic traditions of the Miao people.

label: French Aeronautics and Flight Innovation
description: These medals commemorate pioneering achievements in hot air balloons, airships, and aerial communication in France from the late 18th century through the 19th century, including the Siege of Paris balloon expeditions, Universal Expositions, and early aeronauts like Blanchard and Montgolfier.

label: Metropolitan Museum Collection Diversity
description: These objects represent the encyclopedic range of the Metropolitan Museum of Art, spanning religious imagery, portraiture, landscape painting, performance arts, decorative arts, and historical artifacts from multiple cultures and centuries.

label: Ancient Decorative Metal Attachments
description: This collection comprises small-scale metal ornaments and functional attachments—including appliqués, lamellae, and vessel components—primarily from Roman, Greek, and Bactrian cultures, crafted in bronze, silver, and gold for decorative or structural purposes on larger objects.

label: French Art Deco Jewelry Designs
description: A collection of French decorative arts designs from the Art Deco period featuring brooches, belt buckles, and chain links with naturalistic and fantastical motifs, created primarily through drawings and sketches using graphite, gouache, and ink on paper, with some realized metal pieces.

label: Franco-Prussian War and Paris Commune
description: These commemorative medals document major military events and political upheaval in France during 1870-1871, including battles from the Franco-Prussian War and subsequent social unrest of the Paris Commune.

label: Rembrandt's Self-Portrait Etchings
description: This collection comprises primarily etched self-portraits by Rembrandt and related works, showcasing the Dutch Golden Age master's exploration of his own image across different poses, costumes, and printmaking techniques.

label: Nineteenth-Century Albumen Print Photography
description: These objects are all photographs created primarily using albumen print technology, a dominant photographic process from the 1850s-1890s, with many being stereoview cards that provided three-dimensional viewing experiences.

label: Indonesian Ceremonial and Everyday Textiles
description: These objects are primarily textiles from Indonesia and surrounding regions, including ceremonial garments, wraps, and cloths that served both ritual and daily wear purposes, created using traditional techniques such as batik, ikat, and plain weave with decorative embellishments.

label: African Ceremonial Masks and Headdresses
description: These objects are primarily wooden masks and headdresses from African cultures used in ceremonial, spiritual, and social contexts, often decorated with natural materials like pigments, fibers, shells, and feathers.

label: Alfred Stieglitz's Photographic Collection
description: A curated selection of photographs by Alfred Stieglitz and his circle, featuring portraits of artists, family members, experimental abstractions, and landscapes created using various early photographic printing techniques.

label: Roman Billon Tetradrachm Coins
description: These objects are all Roman coins of tetradrachm denomination made from billon alloy, held in the Getty Museum's numismatic collection.

label: Ancient Greek and Italic Pottery Vessels
description: This collection comprises ceramic vessels from ancient Greek, Italic, and Etruscan cultures dating from the 7th to 3rd centuries BCE, including functional forms such as amphorae, lécythes, rhytons, and drinking vessels, many featuring distinctive shapes and decorative techniques characteristic of their respective periods and regions.

label: Historical Door and Chest Hardware
description: These objects are functional metal components used to secure doors, chests, and other structures across various cultures and time periods, including locks, keys, latches, bolts, and decorative hardware elements.

label: 19th Century Colonial India Photography
description: These are albumen print photographs and prints created in the 19th century by English photographers documenting architectural landmarks, forts, and significant sites across India during the colonial period.

label: Rembrandt's Etched Portrait Studies
description: A collection of etched prints primarily by Rembrandt featuring character studies of heads and busts, including portraits of his wife Saskia and various depictions of old men, demonstrating the artist's exploration of human expression and physiognomy through the etching medium.

label: Andean Indigenous Textiles and Clothing
description: These objects are traditional woven garments and textiles from indigenous cultures of the Andes region (Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador, and surrounding areas), primarily made from natural fibers like alpaca and wool using traditional weaving techniques.

label: Chinese Porcelain and Stoneware Vases
description: A collection of decorative vessels from Chinese culture, primarily porcelain and stoneware vases with various glazing techniques, shapes, and ornamental designs spanning multiple historical periods.

label: Early Photography of Colonial India
description: These objects are albumen silver prints and early photographic documents created during the British colonial period in India, depicting Indian people, occupations, cultural practices, and colonial social structures.

label: Canaletto's Vedute of Venice
description: These are etched prints depicting Italian architectural views and Venetian landscapes, primarily from Canaletto's famous Vedute series showcasing iconic Italian monuments and cityscapes.

label: European Lace Flounces and Borders
description: These objects are decorative lace or embroidered textile flounces (ornamental edgings) produced across Europe from the 16th-19th centuries using traditional techniques including bobbin lace, needle lace, and embroidery.

label: Japanese Calligraphy and Ink Art
description: These objects are all examples of Japanese calligraphic works created with ink on paper or silk, primarily in hanging scroll format, representing a core tradition of East Asian artistic expression.

label: Chalk sketches and figure studies
description: A collection of drawings primarily executed in chalk on paper, featuring figure studies, compositional sketches, and observational drawings of people, animals, and architectural elements from various periods and artists.

label: French Military Photography Collection
description: A documentary photographic archive depicting French and Allied soldiers during World War I and II, capturing military operations, daily life, leisure activities, and the Liberation of Paris through gelatin-bromide prints.

label: Civil War Medical Documentation Photographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print card photographs documenting Union soldiers who were Civil War victims, often showing visible injuries or amputations, created for medical or military records purposes.

label: Curtis Native American Photography Series
description: These photogravure prints are from Edward S. Curtis's multi-volume documentary series depicting Native American tribes and cultures across North America in the early 20th century.

label: Candlesticks and Candelabra
description: These objects are decorative lighting fixtures—candlesticks, candelabra, and torchères—made from diverse materials across multiple European and American cultures spanning from the 15th to 19th centuries.

label: Decorative Vessels and Tableware
description: These objects are functional or decorative containers spanning multiple cultures and centuries, including cups, bowls, salt cellars, and covered dishes made from various materials such as glass, silver, ivory, and ceramics.

label: Historical photographs of Dutch cities
description: These are late 19th and early 20th-century documentary photographs depicting architectural landmarks, urban scenes, and streetscapes from Amsterdam, Utrecht, and other Dutch locations, primarily printed as cabinet photographs and mounted on cardboard.

label: French Stereographs, 19th Century
description: These are primarily French stereographic photographs from the 19th century, made using daguerreotype and albumen silver print processes, depicting domestic scenes, portraits, and genre subjects both hand-colored and plain.

label: Northern Renaissance Etched Prints
description: A collection of etched prints on paper from 16th-century Antwerp and Haarlem, predominantly featuring allegorical 'Death' encounters and naturalistic studies characteristic of Northern European printmaking traditions.

label: Japanese Ceramics from Edo-Showa Periods
description: These objects are porcelain and stoneware ceramics from Japan spanning the Edo through Showa periods, comprising functional tableware and decorative vessels that showcase traditional Japanese motifs and glazing techniques.

label: European Ceramic Tableware
description: These objects are primarily functional ceramics from European collections, including plates, dishes, and serving vessels made from various glazed earthenwares and decorated ceramics dating from the colonial through industrial periods.

label: Civil War Era Southern Photographs
description: These objects are albumen prints and photographs documenting American Civil War battlefields, military sites, and Southern landscapes from the 1860s, primarily featuring Georgia, South Carolina, and related conflict locations.

label: Holy Family Religious Prints
description: A comprehensive collection of paper prints depicting the Holy Family and related biblical figures, primarily from Italian and Northern European artistic traditions, featuring various compositions of Mary, Jesus, Joseph, and accompanying saints like John the Baptist and Anne.

label: Woodcuts from Renaissance printed books
description: These objects are woodcut illustrations and pages from books printed in the 15th and 16th centuries across Northern Europe and Italy, representing the widespread use of woodcut technology for illustrating printed texts during the Renaissance period.

label: French Glass Paperweights
description: Collection of glass paperweights primarily from Saint-Louis-lès-Bitche, a renowned French glassmaking center, held in European applied arts departments.

label: Watkins Glen Stereograph Series
description: A comprehensive collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting the scenic gorges, waterfalls, and geological formations of Watkins Glen and nearby glens in New York during the 19th century American tourism era.

label: 19th-Century French Satirical Prints
description: This collection comprises lithographic prints primarily from France and Belgium, largely featuring satirical social commentary and caricature that appeared in publications like Le Charivari and La Caricature during the 19th century.

label: German Engravings on Paper
description: A collection of prints created using engraving techniques on paper, predominantly associated with German culture, depicting allegorical virtues, vices, biblical scenes, and occupational figures.

label: Antwerp Artists Carte-de-visite Portraits
description: A collection of photographic portrait cards (cartes-de-visite) from the Rijksmuseum depicting 19th-century Antwerp painters, sculptors, and other visual artists, printed on cardboard and paper.

label: Illuminated Manuscript Pages from Single Work
description: These objects are individual folios and page fragments from a single illuminated book of hours commissioned by Queen Isabella the Catholic, created in late 15th-century Flanders by Ghent-Bruges workshops.

label: Goya's Caprichos series prints
description: This collection comprises Spanish satirical etchings from Francisco Goya's Caprichos series, characterized by their use of etching and aquatint techniques on paper to depict social critique and commentary on human folly.

label: Miao and Hmong Traditional Aprons
description: These objects are aprons from Miao (Chinese) and Hmong (Thai) cultures, representing significant ceremonial and daily wear garments traditionally crafted from cotton, silk, and decorative materials like beads, metal, and embroidery.

label: Ancient Peruvian Woven Textiles
description: These objects are textile fragments from pre-Columbian Peru, created using various weaving and embroidery techniques with cotton and camelid wool fibers, representing diverse cultures and time periods from the Paracas and later Andean civilizations.

label: Intaglio prints on paper
description: These objects are all intaglio printmaking works (etchings, drypoints, and transfer lithographs) held in the Department of Arts of the Americas at Miami-based institutions.

label: Academic figure and anatomy studies
description: These are preparatory chalk drawings on paper from European art institutions, primarily featuring studies of human figures, body parts, hands, arms, and drapery used for artistic training and preparation of larger compositions.

label: Dutch Marbled and Decorated Papers
description: A collection of decorative marbled and dribbled papers from the Rijksmuseum, featuring various traditional marbling techniques (comb, stone, spiral, wave, dribble) created by applying painted patterns to paper.

label: German Medieval Illuminated Initials
description: These are decorated manuscript letters from German medieval manuscripts created with tempera colors and precious metal leaf, characteristic of luxury manuscript illumination practices.

label: Scottish Country Houses Albumen Photographs
description: These objects are albumen silver print photographs documenting Scottish estates, mansions, and notable properties from the 19th century, primarily featuring distant and landscape views of aristocratic residences and their grounds.

label: Decorative Snuff Boxes Across Europe
description: These objects are luxury snuff boxes from the 17th-19th centuries, crafted by European artisans in major centers like London, Paris, Dresden, and Amsterdam using precious materials such as gold, enamel, hardstones, and ivory.

label: 19th Century Canadian Stereograph Views
description: These albumen silver print stereographs document Canadian landscapes, architecture, and urban scenes from the mid-1800s, primarily featuring Montreal, Quebec, and Ottawa.

label: Color Photography of American Places
description: A collection of chromogenic and inkjet color prints documenting American landscapes, storefronts, interiors, and street scenes from various locations across the United States.

label: Female figures in Dutch prints
description: A collection of paper-based prints from the Rijksmuseum featuring women as central subjects, created through various printmaking techniques including lithography, engraving, and etching.

label: French Medieval Architectural Stone Elements
description: These objects are limestone and stone architectural fragments and decorative elements from medieval French buildings, primarily featuring capitals, columns, and ornamental details from religious structures like churches and cloisters.

label: Papal Medals and Commemorative Portraits
description: This collection comprises medals and sculptural portraits commemorating various popes and papal officials from the Renaissance through the 19th century, primarily created in Italy using bronze, silver, and gilt metals.

label: Henri IV Brass Tokens Early 17th Century
description: A series of French brass numismatic tokens commemorating King Henri IV, dated between 1603-1610, representing royal authority and symbolic currency from the early Baroque period.

label: Louis XVI Brass Tokens
description: A collection of French brass numismatic tokens commemorating King Louis XVI's reign (1774-1792), primarily from the late revolutionary period (1790-1791).

label: Italian Aerial Photography Collection
description: A cohesive series of gelatin silver prints documenting Italian aviation, airfields, aircraft, and aerial perspectives from the early-to-mid 20th century, including experimental aerial propaganda campaigns.

label: Japanese Ink Paintings
description: A collection of traditional East Asian-influenced paintings from Japanese museums spanning the Muromachi through Edo periods, predominantly featuring landscapes, birds, flowers, and figures executed in ink and color on paper or silk formats.

label: European Domestic Metalware
description: These objects are functional household items made from precious and non-precious metals (silver, pewter, copper) produced across Europe from various cultural centers, primarily for serving, storing, and measuring food and beverages.

label: Decorative Fans Across Cultures
description: A diverse collection of hand-held fans from Europe, Asia, and India spanning multiple centuries, crafted from luxury materials including ivory, bone, silk, and paper, representing both functional accessories and decorative art objects.

label: 19th-century Eastern travel photography
description: French photographic documentation of ancient monuments and architecture across Egypt, Nubia, Palestine, and Syria from the 1852 album 'Egypte, Nubie, Palestine et Syrie.'

label: Hindu and Buddhist Deities
description: These objects are sculptures and decorative items depicting major Hindu gods and divine figures from South and Southeast Asian religious traditions, spanning multiple centuries and cultures.

label: Wedgwood Pottery from Burslem
description: A collection of decorative and functional ceramic objects produced in Burslem, Staffordshire, primarily using jasperware stoneware techniques characteristic of Wedgwood manufacturing, featuring classical motifs, cameos, and refined tableware from the 18th-19th centuries.

label: European Porcelain Tableware
description: These objects are primarily porcelain cups, saucers, and related tableware from 18th-century European manufactories including French (Sèvres, Vincennes, Saint-Cloud), German (Vienna, Ludwigsburg, Höchst, Chantilly), and other European producers, made using soft-paste or hard-paste porcelain techniques with decorative enamels and gilding.

label: 19th Century English Albumen Photographs
description: These objects are photographs from 19th century England created using the albumen print process, a popular early photographic technique that produced warm-toned images on paper coated with egg white and silver salts.

label: Dutch Orange-Nassau Dynasty Portraits
description: These objects are prints, engravings, and drawings depicting members of the House of Orange-Nassau ruling family of the Netherlands from the 16th to 19th centuries, serving as official portraits and commemorative images of Dutch monarchs and princes.

label: American Arts Collection
description: These objects are all held in the Department of Arts of the Americas at Miami (MIA) and represent diverse artistic works created in the United States.

label: Museum Collection Reinstallation Documentation
description: Photographic records documenting the process of reinstalling artworks and artifacts in Paris museums, capturing handling, packing, display preparation, and conservation details.

label: Early Photography of Japan
description: These prints are primarily 19th-century photographs and hand-colored albumen silver prints documenting Japanese people, landscapes, and daily life, created by English photographers during the period of Japan's opening to Western contact.

label: American Works on Paper
description: These objects are all drawings from American cultural traditions spanning the 18th to 21st centuries, created using various paper-based and drawing media techniques.

label: Childhood Activities and Play Scenes
description: These are Dutch prints depicting various scenes of children engaged in everyday activities, games, learning, and play, created primarily through etching and engraving techniques during the early modern period.

label: Magdalenian Reindeer Bone Hunting Tools
description: These objects are Upper Paleolithic (Magdalenian period) hunting weapons and tool components made from reindeer bone and decorated with carved or incised designs, preserved at the National Archaeology Museum.

label: Photographic reproductions of artworks
description: These objects are photographic reproductions (fotoreproducties) of paintings, drawings, and prints created using various photographic printing techniques such as albumen print, photoaquatint, and platinum processes, mounted on cardboard and paper supports.

label: Portrait photographs by Mapplethorpe
description: A collection of photographic portraits, primarily gelatin silver prints from the American photography collection, representing subjects photographed in the style and era associated with Robert Mapplethorpe's portrait work.

label: Roman Lead Votive Fish Offerings
description: These objects are small lead fish sculptures from Roman culture, likely created as votive offerings left at religious or sacred sites, now held in the Getty's sculpture collection.

label: Ancient Greek Terracotta Drinking Vessels
description: These objects are Greek ceramic cups and cup fragments, primarily from Attic workshops, made of terracotta using the black-figure decorative technique and representing everyday vessels used for drinking in ancient Greece.

label: Heliogravure Prints of Decorative Designs
description: French Renaissance and Early Modern design prints created using heliogravure technique, featuring ornamental designs by master artists and architects held in the Getty's photographs collection.

label: Edvard Munch's Printmaking Works
description: These objects are prints and drawings by Norwegian Expressionist artist Edvard Munch, created using various printmaking techniques including lithography, woodcut, etching, and drypoint.

label: Spanish Legal Procedure Manuscript
description: These illuminated folios from a Spanish manuscript use decorative initials to illustrate various legal and commercial scenes, including court proceedings, contracts, disputes, and judgments, likely depicting scenes from a legal code or judicial manual.

label: Depression-era American labor photography
description: Collection of gelatin silver print photographs documenting working-class Americans, miners, factory workers, and their families during the Great Depression era, primarily from industrial regions of the eastern United States.

label: French sculptural medallions and plaques
description: A collection of French medallions, plaques, and sculptural reliefs created primarily in terra cotta and plaster, ranging from classical allegorical subjects to modern commemorative portraits and thematic compositions.

label: 18th-19th Century European Decorative Furniture
description: These objects are fine furniture pieces from European cultures (primarily French, Italian, and German) spanning the 18th-19th centuries, characterized by sophisticated veneering techniques, exotic wood inlays, marble tops, and ornamental embellishments reflecting the aesthetic refinement of their period.

label: 19th Century French Cliché-Verre Prints
description: These objects are primarily French prints from the 19th century created using the cliché-verre technique, a hybrid process combining photography and printmaking that was particularly popular among French artists during this period.

label: European Decorative Textiles, 17th-20th Century
description: A collection of luxury fabric lengths featuring silk, brocade, and damask weaves from France, Italy, and England, primarily from the 18th century, representing high-quality decorative and furnishing textiles used in aristocratic contexts.

label: Moche Stirrup Spout Ceramics
description: These objects are ceramic vessels from ancient Peru, primarily from the Moche culture of the North Coast, characterized by their distinctive stirrup spout handles and decorated with relief, painted, or modeled motifs depicting animals, mythological figures, and warriors.

label: Small Decorative Boxes and Containers
description: These objects are miniature functional or ornamental boxes from 18th-19th century Europe, crafted from precious materials like enamel, metal, and gold for storing small personal items such as patches, pills, or jewelry.

label: 1848 Revolutionary Paris Club Medals
description: Commemorative medals issued by diverse Parisian political and social clubs during the 1848 Revolution and its aftermath, documenting the proliferation of republican, socialist, and democratic organizations across Paris neighborhoods.

label: Utah Territory Stereographic Views
description: Albumen silver print stereographs documenting Utah landscapes, settlements, and Union Pacific Railroad infrastructure during nineteenth-century westward expansion.

label: Dutch Letterpress Prints and Popular Narratives
description: A collection of paper-based prints from Amsterdam featuring narrative themes, folk characters, and educational content produced through letterpress and related printing techniques in the 19th century.

label: Asian Incense Burning Vessels
description: These objects are functional vessels designed for burning incense across Asian cultures, crafted from diverse materials including ceramics, jade, bronze, and stoneware, spanning multiple dynasties and periods from Tang through Qing China, Japan, and Iran.

label: Silver Tea and Tableware
description: These objects are primarily metalwork pieces—including teapots, teaspoons, creamers, and related serving vessels—predominantly made from silver or silver-plated materials and designed for tea and coffee service across American and European design movements from the Federal period through the Bauhaus era.

label: Russo-Japanese War Propaganda Prints
description: Japanese woodblock prints (nishiki-e) documenting military battles, heroic soldiers, and naval victories from the Russo-Japanese War (1904-1905), created as patriotic propaganda and popular media.

label: American Oil Paintings 19th Century
description: These objects are oil paintings from American institutions dating primarily to the 19th century, including portraits, literary subjects, and landscape works that represent the development of American artistic traditions.

label: European Drawings and Watercolors
description: A collection of works on paper spanning from the 16th to early 20th century, primarily featuring figurative, landscape, and portrait subjects created through drawing and watercolor techniques.

label: East Asian Ceramic Jars
description: These objects are primarily Chinese and Korean ceramic vessels, predominantly jars and jarlets, spanning various dynasties and representing diverse glazing techniques, decorative methods, and functional forms.

label: French glass paperweights
description: These objects are decorative glass paperweights produced by renowned French crystal manufacturers including Baccarat, Clichy, and Lunéville, representing 19th-century European applied arts.

label: Late 20th Century Works on Paper
description: A collection of predominantly untitled artworks created between 1975-2000 by French and Spanish artists, executed on white paper using various media including acrylic, oil, gouache, and mixed techniques, primarily held in the CAPC museum collection.

label: Illustrated Religious Calendar Saints
description: These prints depict saints organized by their feast days throughout the liturgical calendar year, arranged chronologically by date.

label: Czechoslovak Modernist Photography
description: These objects are gelatin silver prints created by Czechoslovak photographers from the 20th century, predominantly featuring experimental photographic techniques including photograms, still lifes, and abstract compositions that exemplify the modernist movement in Central European photography.

label: Persian and Islamic Textile Fragments
description: These objects are silk and metal-thread textile fragments from Iran and Iraq spanning from the Abbasid period (8th century) through the 19th century, featuring decorative weaving techniques including brocade, lampas, and compound weaves.

label: Holbein's Biblical and Memento Mori Prints
description: A collection of woodcut and engraved prints from the Renaissance, primarily by Hans Holbein the Younger, depicting Old Testament narratives, New Testament scenes, and the allegorical Dance of Death (Danse Macabre) theme where Death confronts figures from various social stations.

label: Japanese Lacquerware Boxes
description: These objects are decorative and functional containers from Japan spanning the Heian to Meiji periods, primarily crafted using lacquer techniques often embellished with gold, mother-of-pearl, and other inlays.

label: French historical administrative documents
description: A collection of handwritten letters, decrees, and official certificates from French institutions spanning the 17th-20th centuries, documenting administrative, official, and personal correspondence from Paris.

label: Iron Age to Medieval Artifacts
description: These objects span from the Bronze Age through the Medieval period, featuring domestic tools, weapons, and pottery from French archaeological collections that illustrate technological and cultural development across centuries.

label: Native American Woven Textiles
description: These objects are predominantly wool woven blankets, rugs, and garments created by Indigenous North American cultures, particularly Navajo/Diné artisans, spanning from the 19th century transitional and classic periods through post-contact eras.

label: Chinese Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are predominantly ceramic bowls, dishes, and vessels from China spanning multiple dynasties (Song through Qing), primarily produced at the renowned Jingdezhen kilns in Jiangxi Province, featuring decorative techniques including glazing, underglaze painting, and overglaze decoration.

label: Microcosm of London studies
description: These objects are primarily preparatory watercolor and ink drawings for Thomas Rowlandson and Augustus Pugin's 'Microcosm of London,' a series depicting notable London institutions and landmarks in early 19th-century England.

label: Early Modern Seating Furniture
description: These objects are chairs, armchairs, and seating pieces from European and American museums, spanning the 17th-19th centuries, representing diverse regional furniture-making traditions and materials including carved wood, mahogany, and upholstered designs.

label: Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji
description: These woodblock prints are part of Katsushika Hokusai's famous series depicting Mount Fuji from various vantage points across Japan, primarily from early 19th-century Edo period.

label: American Landscape and Portrait Photography
description: A collection of dye imbibition color prints documenting American landscapes, natural landmarks, and portraits from the mid-20th century, primarily featuring locations across the United States.

label: Chinese ink drawings on paper
description: These objects are all Chinese artistic drawings created with ink on paper, primarily held at Harvard with one comparable work at the Rijksmuseum, representing traditional East Asian sketch and painting techniques.

label: Renaissance Italian Portrait Medals
description: A collection of commemorative medals from Renaissance Italy (primarily 15th-17th centuries) depicting members of prominent Florentine and Italian families, created using lead, wax, or bronze casting techniques.

label: 19th-Century California Stereographic Views
description: These objects are albumen silver print stereographs and photographs documenting California landscapes and urban landmarks, primarily San Francisco locations, created in the mid-to-late 1800s as popular visual documentation of American scenery.

label: Still Life Oil Paintings
description: A collection of still life paintings from European museums spanning the 17th-19th centuries, primarily executed in oil on canvas or panel, depicting arrangements of flowers, food, objects, and occasionally fauna.

label: Camera Work Magazine Issues
description: These are individual bound volumes or issues from Camera Work, a groundbreaking early 20th-century photography journal that featured photogravure reproductions of artistic photographs and promoted modernist photographic aesthetics.

label: Ancient Mediterranean Terracotta Vessels
description: These objects are all ceramic vessels and vessel fragments made from terracotta, originating from ancient Mediterranean cultures spanning from Etruscan, Greek, South Italian, and Roman civilizations.

label: French Lampwork Glass Figurines
description: Decorative figurative sculptures made using the French lampwork technique (verre de Nevers), featuring hand-crafted glass figures with metal armatures depicting figures from religious, theatrical, and everyday life.

label: Ancient Egyptian Personal Adornment
description: These objects are decorative items and accessories worn by ancient Egyptians, including jewelry, beads, and ornaments that served both aesthetic and symbolic/religious purposes across different time periods.

label: Ancient Egyptian Scarab Amulets
description: These objects are decorative scarab beetle amulets from ancient Egypt, crafted from materials like faience and steatite, featuring religious symbols, hieroglyphs, and divine motifs that served protective and ceremonial purposes.

label: Domestic Bedding Textiles
description: These objects are decorative and functional bedcover and pillow textile items from England and the United States, created primarily between the 18th-20th centuries using embroidery, appliqué, quilting, and other embellishment techniques.

label: Early 20th Century British Photography Prints
description: A collection of British cultural portraits, landscapes, and architectural subjects created primarily through photographic printing techniques such as platinum print, photogravure, and collotype, representing prominent figures and sites from the early 1900s.

label: Polaroid Dye Diffusion Photographs
description: These objects are American prints created using Polaroid diffusion (instant) photography techniques, held in the Getty Museum's Department of Photographs.

label: Tarantine Terracotta Female Heads
description: These objects are terracotta sculptural heads depicting women with distinctive melon-shaped coiffures from ancient South Italian (Tarantine) culture, likely representing a popular hairstyle or idealized feminine beauty standard of the region.

label: Metropolitan Museum diverse collections
description: This is a heterogeneous collection of objects spanning multiple cultures, time periods, and artifact types from the Metropolitan Museum of Art, connected only by their institutional provenance rather than thematic, chronological, or cultural unity.

label: Second Empire commemorative medals
description: French medals from the Second Empire period (1852-1870) commemorating major state events, exhibitions, and institutional achievements under Napoleon III's rule.

label: European Decorative Drinking Glassware
description: A collection of functional and ornamental drinking vessels and related glass objects crafted across European regions from the 18th century onward, including wine glasses, ale glasses, and decorative serving pieces made through various glassmaking techniques.

label: 19th-century French Jerusalem photography
description: A collection of French photographic prints and photographs documenting Jerusalem's religious sites, monuments, and architectural details, primarily created using salted paper print techniques during the early photographic era.

label: Chinese Shadow Puppet Theater Components
description: These objects are carved puppet heads used in traditional Chinese shadow puppet theater, a form of storytelling performance where intricately crafted figures are backlit to cast shadows on a screen.

label: Italian Bobbin Lace Edgings
description: These objects are decorative lace edgings and insertions made using bobbin lace techniques, primarily from Genoa and Abruzzi regions of Italy, featuring characteristic pointed designs and needle lace techniques.

label: Ancient Safety Pin Fasteners
description: These objects are fibulae, ancient brooch-like fastening pins used across Mediterranean and European cultures from the Etruscan period through the early medieval era, primarily made of bronze and precious metals.

label: East Asian Ceramic Traditions
description: These objects represent diverse ceramic wares from China, Japan, and Southeast Asia, including porcelain vessels (Arita, Imari, Kutani), stoneware pieces (Seto, Satsuma), and decorative tableware spanning multiple centuries of Asian ceramic craftsmanship.

label: Embroidered Textiles from Diverse Cultures
description: This collection comprises embroidered textiles and garments spanning multiple continents and centuries, united by their use of decorative needlework techniques with silk, metallic threads, and other embellishment materials to create functional and ceremonial objects.

label: Native American Basketry Vessels
description: A collection of handwoven baskets and basket-like vessels created by various Indigenous peoples of North America, including the Pacific Northwest Coast, California, Great Basin, and Arctic regions, serving functional purposes such as storage, cooking, food serving, and daily use.

label: Medical Dermatology Educational Stereographs
description: A collection of American color photomechanical stereograph cards depicting various skin diseases and dermatological conditions, created for medical education and documentation purposes.

label: Albert Flamen's Natural History Engravings
description: A series of original engravings by French painter-engraver Albert Flamen depicting various species of fish and birds, organized in thematic parts and catalogued by Robert-Dumesnil and Bartsch.

label: Ancient Egyptian Textile Fragments
description: These objects are textile fragments from ancient Egypt, primarily composed of linen and wool created using various weaving techniques including tapestry, slit weave, and compound weaves, with some Byzantine period examples incorporating gold foil.

label: Ancient Egyptian funerary objects
description: This collection comprises artifacts from ancient Egypt and the Greco-Roman period, including shabtis, amulets, sarcophagi, and figurines primarily housed in French museums, reflecting beliefs about the afterlife and divine protection.

label: Japanese Buddhist Ceremonial Robes
description: These objects are kesa, sacred Buddhist vestments worn by Japanese monks, distinguished by their luxurious silk construction often embellished with gilt or gold-leaf-covered paper strips in various weaving techniques.

label: Yellowstone National Park Stereographs
description: A collection of 19th-century stereograph photographs documenting the geothermal features and landscapes of Yellowstone National Park, primarily using albumen silver print technology to capture iconic sites like geysers, hot springs, and canyons for popular viewing.

label: Albumen print photographs of classical sculptures
description: These are 19th-century albumen print photographs documenting classical and ancient sculptures housed in major European museums, primarily from Italian collections including the Vatican, Uffizi Gallery, and Capitoline Museums.

label: Nineteenth-century Indian architectural photography
description: These objects are albumen prints and photographs documenting Mughal monuments and palaces in Agra and surrounding regions, created primarily by English and British photographers during the colonial period.

label: Roman Imperial Bronze Sestertii
description: Bronze coins from the Roman Empire featuring portrait busts of emperors, empresses, and imperial family members, primarily used as medium-denomination currency during the Imperial period.

label: French Decorative Ceramics and Glass
description: A collection of domestic and ornamental vessels from French museum collections, primarily comprising ceramics (porcelain, faïence, grès) and glass objects with enameled decoration, representing functional tableware and decorative arts.

label: Goryeo Dynasty Celadon Ceramics
description: These objects are Korean ceramics from the Goryeo Dynasty (918-1392) characterized by celadon glazing and decorative techniques including incised, inlaid, and relief floral motifs such as peonies, chrysanthemums, and lotus designs.

label: 17th-18th Century English Prints
description: A collection of engraved and etched portrait prints on paper depicting notable English and London figures from the 17th and 18th centuries, predominantly created using intaglio printing techniques.

label: French Revolutionary Medals 1848
description: These objects are commemorative medals from the Paris Museum collections documenting key political figures and events during the 1848 French Revolution, particularly General Eugène Cavaignac's role in suppressing the June Days and Étienne Cabet's utopian socialist movement.

label: Native American Portrait Photographs
description: These are early photographic portraits of Native American individuals and groups from various tribes, primarily created using platinum print and salted paper print processes, documenting tribal leaders, warriors, and community members.

label: Eight Views Series Prints
description: These Japanese woodblock prints are all part of 'Eight Views' (hakkei) series that adapted the classical landscape theme to various local regions and modern settings during the Edo period.

label: 19th century Paris lithographic prints
description: A collection of paper-based lithographic prints produced in Paris during the 19th century, depicting everyday urban scenes, transportation, indigenous clothing, hunting activities, and street life.

label: Japanese Arms and Armor
description: This collection comprises weapons, protective equipment, and decorative martial accessories from Japan, spanning various periods, including swords, bows, arrows, helmets, armor components, and ornamental sword guards (tsuba) that reflect the martial traditions and craftsmanship of Japanese warfare and samurai culture.

label: Illuminated Medieval Calendar Pages
description: These objects are decorative folio pages from medieval manuscripts across European cultures that depict monthly activities and seasonal labor through illuminated tempera and gold leaf artwork.

label: Historical Footwear Across Cultures
description: These objects are shoes, boots, slippers, and related footwear from various time periods and cultures, primarily held in museum textile and costume departments and representing diverse manufacturing techniques including leather, silk, macramé, and embroidery.

label: Early Medieval Egyptian Tunic Ornaments
description: These textile fragments are decorative elements from tunics produced in Egypt during the Byzantine, Umayyad, and Abbasid periods (7th-9th centuries), featuring woven and embroidered ornamental bands and clavi that adorned sleeves, shoulders, and necklines.

label: Chinese Export Porcelain Tableware
description: These objects are decorative porcelain plates, platters, and serving vessels manufactured in China for European and international markets, often featuring armorial crests, family coats of arms, and ornamental designs with enamel and gilded decoration.

label: Silver Tableware and Metalwork
description: These objects are primarily silver spoons, ladles, and related table utensils spanning from the 17th century to the Arts and Crafts period, representing domestic dining practices across England and America.

label: 19th-Century German-Austrian Stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs depicting architectural landmarks, monuments, and urban views throughout German-speaking Central Europe, primarily from the mid-to-late 1800s.

label: Architectural Views in Lithographic Prints
description: These are paper-based lithographic prints depicting landmarks, buildings, and urban scenes primarily from Paris and other French locations, created during the 19th century as part of a documented series of architectural and topographical views.

label: German Romantic Landscape Etchings
description: A collection of prints primarily from German-speaking regions (Germany and Austria) spanning the 16th-19th centuries, predominantly featuring landscape scenes with natural elements, architectural features, and figures created through etching techniques.

label: Miao Culture Skirts
description: A collection of skirts from the Miao ethnic group of China, made primarily from cotton with various embellishments, held in the Asian Art department.

label: Rubens Masterworks Reproductive Engravings
description: French interpretive engravings from the Dutuit collection reproducing compositions by Flemish master Peter Paul Rubens, spanning religious, mythological, and portrait subjects.

label: Ancient Peruvian Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are decorated ceramic jars and vessels from pre-Columbian Peru and nearby regions (Nazca, Moche valleys), featuring abstract and stylized depictions of human figures, animals, geometric patterns, and ritual performers created using ceramic and pigment techniques.

label: Louis-Napoléon's 1848 Presidential Election
description: Commemorative medals documenting Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte's election and inauguration as President of the French Republic in December 1848.

label: Scottish Etched Prints Collection
description: A collection of primarily etched prints associated with Scottish culture, featuring architectural landmarks, landscapes, and portraits created using various intaglio techniques on laid or wove paper.

label: 19th Century American West Photography
description: These objects are albumen silver prints and stereographs documenting American western landscapes, infrastructure, and settlements from the 19th century, primarily featuring scenic views of the Columbia River region, California coast, and railroad developments.

label: Goya's Disparates Series
description: These prints are from Francisco Goya's Disparates (Follies) series, a set of satirical etchings created in early 19th-century Spain that critique social follies, political absurdities, and human nature through surreal and darkly humorous imagery.

label: French Architectural and Urban Etchings
description: These objects are predominantly 19th-century prints, primarily etchings by French artists depicting French architectural landmarks, urban streets, ports, and cityscape views including Paris, Bordeaux, Amsterdam, and other European locations.

label: Historical Carpets and Rugs
description: These objects are hand-woven carpets and rugs from diverse cultures across Asia, Europe, and North Africa, created using traditional weaving techniques with materials like wool, cotton, and silk.

label: Pages from Single French Album
description: These are individual pages or photographs from The Madame B Album, a French photographic collection featuring albumen prints with hand-applied watercolor embellishment created as a bound album.

label: Navajo Silver and Turquoise Bracelets
description: These objects are Diné (Navajo) bracelets crafted from silver and turquoise, representing a signature tradition of Native American jewelry-making held in museum collections.

label: Ancient Egyptian Amulets
description: These objects are protective talismans from ancient Egypt made from various materials including faience, stone, and precious minerals, featuring symbolic forms of deities, celestial bodies, animals, and royal regalia worn as charms for spiritual and physical protection.

label: Prague Baroque Portrait Prints
description: A collection of etched and engraved portrait prints on paper depicting notable figures from Prague culture, likely from the 17th century Baroque period.

label: Old Testament Biblical Narratives
description: These objects depict scenes from Old Testament stories—primarily the lives of David, Abraham, and their families—rendered across multiple media and cultures from the 16th-19th centuries.

label: Northern Plains Indigenous Beadwork and Hide Objects
description: These objects represent traditional clothing, accessories, and containers created by Northern Plains and Great Lakes Indigenous nations (A'aninin, Nakoda, Lakȟóta, Anishinaabe, Cree, Blackfeet, and others) using animal hide decorated with beadwork, quills, pigments, and metal embellishments.

label: Northern European Glass Beakers
description: A collection of decorative drinking vessels made from glass, primarily from German-speaking regions and surrounding areas (Germany, Bohemia, Netherlands, Rhineland, Austria) dating from the medieval and early modern periods, featuring various techniques including enameling, gilding, and blown glass construction.

label: Dutch Children's Ceremonial Garments
description: These objects are delicate white garments for infants and young children from the Netherlands, created for special occasions like christenings and formal visits, featuring elaborate decorative techniques including embroidery, lace, and openwork.

label: Japanese Provincial Landscape Woodblock Prints
description: These objects are woodblock prints from 19th-century Japan depicting famous locations across different provinces, primarily from two series: 'One Hundred Famous Views in the Various Provinces' and 'Famous Places in the Sixty-odd Provinces.'

label: Franco-Prussian War commemorative medals
description: Collection of numismatic medals held in Paris museums commemorating the Franco-Prussian War (1870-1871), German military victories, and the figures of German and Dutch royalty central to this period.

label: 19th-20th Century French Nude and Bathing Prints
description: This collection comprises prints predominantly from France featuring nude figures and bathing scenes, created using various techniques including etching, lithography, and drypoint across the 19th and 20th centuries.

label: Upper Paleolithic Portable Art Objects
description: These objects represent portable art from the Upper Paleolithic period (Aurignacian through Magdalenian), primarily made from animal materials like ivory, bone, and antler, and include personal ornaments (pendants), figurative sculptures, and decorated teeth.

label: Bullfighting in Art and Prints
description: These objects are artistic representations of bullfighting, spanning multiple print techniques and media, primarily depicting Spanish bullfighting traditions, notable matadors, and historical bullfighting scenes.

label: Ancient Greek Drinking Vessels
description: These objects are primarily terracotta and ceramic drinking cups (skyphoi and kantharoi) from ancient Greek and South Italian cultures, spanning from Attic to Apulian pottery traditions.

label: Medieval Illuminated Manuscript Initials
description: These objects are decorated initial letters from liturgical manuscripts and religious texts, primarily from Italy and France, featuring figurative scenes and ornamental embellishments created with tempera, gold leaf, and ink during the medieval period.

label: Medieval and Renaissance Ecclesiastical Vestments
description: These objects are decorative textile components and complete garments used in Christian religious ceremonies, primarily from European sources spanning the medieval through early modern periods.

label: Victorian Stereograph Views of Egypt
description: These objects are stereograph cards featuring albumen silver prints of Egyptian and Nubian archaeological sites, created by English photographers during the 19th century for popular stereoscopic viewing.

label: Magdalénian Engraved Stone Plaques
description: These are fragmented stone plaques from the Magdalénian period (Upper Paleolithic) created through incision and engraving techniques on various stone materials including schist, sandstone, and limestone.

label: British Colonial India Albumen Photographs
description: A collection of albumen print photographs documenting British colonial administrative personnel, military officers, viceregal officials, and Indian nobility in late 19th-century India.

label: Ancient Funerary and Commemorative Reliefs
description: These objects are relief sculptures and architectural fragments from ancient Mediterranean cultures (Greek, Roman, Etruscan, Palmyran) created to commemorate, honor, or mark the graves of the deceased.

label: Pitchers across cultures and materials
description: These objects are all pitchers or cream pitchers representing diverse cultural traditions, time periods, and manufacturing techniques, including ceramics, glass, silver, and metalwork from European, American, and pre-Columbian sources.

label: Chinese Ceramic Storage Vessels
description: These objects are jars and covered vessels from China spanning multiple dynasties (Eastern Zhou through Qing), made primarily from stoneware and porcelain, used for storage and domestic purposes.

label: Japanese Immortal Poets Series
description: These objects are Japanese woodblock prints and paintings depicting classical poets from the Six Immortal Poets (Rokkasen), Thirty-Six Immortal Poets, and related poetic tradition series, often presented in fashionable or satirical reinterpretations.

label: Classical Deities in Renaissance Prints
description: These are Northern European engravings and etchings from the 16th century depicting Greco-Roman gods and goddesses, often shown presiding over human activities or paired with mythological narratives.

label: Qing Dynasty Glass Snuff Bottles
description: A collection of miniature decorative vessels produced in China during the Qing Dynasty (1644-1911), primarily made from glass and used for storing powdered tobacco or snuff.

label: Galerie Contemporaine Portrait Series
description: A collection of woodburytype portrait photographs of prominent 19th-century French and European cultural figures published in the periodical 'Galerie Contemporaine Littéraire, Artistique.'

label: 19th-Century Albumen Prints of Italy
description: A collection of albumen print photographs documenting Italian archaeological sites, architectural landmarks, and museum sculptures, primarily from the Naples region including Pompeii, created during the height of albumen printing technology in the 1800s.

label: Peter Henry Emerson's Norfolk Waterways
description: A collection of photographs and photogravures documenting the landscape, waterways, and working life of Norfolk and Suffolk in England, primarily from Peter Henry Emerson's 'Wild Life on a Tidal Water' series and related works.

label: Chushingura theatrical narrative prints
description: These Japanese woodblock prints illustrate different acts from the Chushingura play, a celebrated Edo-period drama about forty-seven loyal retainers, produced in multiple series with varying artistic treatments including perspective views and beauty parodies.

label: Musical Instruments Across Cultures
description: A diverse collection of string, wind, and percussion instruments spanning multiple continents and centuries, representing the universal human expression through music across African, Asian, European, and Middle Eastern cultures.

label: Books of Hours and Liturgical Manuscripts
description: These objects are decorated medieval and Renaissance devotional manuscripts—primarily Books of Hours and liturgical texts—featuring illuminated pages with religious imagery, decorative initials, and ornamental borders created across Western Europe from the 15th-16th centuries.

label: Commemorative portraits of French notables
description: A collection of medals, medallions, and sculptural portraits commemorating prominent French intellectuals, military leaders, scientists, and political figures from the 18th-19th centuries, primarily created by Parisian institutions.

label: Old Master Drawings Collection
description: These are primarily ink and paper drawings from the Rijksmuseum, spanning religious narratives, classical scenes, and allegorical subjects created by European masters and anonymous artists from the Renaissance through Baroque periods.

label: Portrait Drawings from Old Master Period
description: A collection of portrait drawings from the Rijksmuseum spanning the 16th-17th centuries, executed primarily in chalk, ink, and pencil on paper or parchment, depicting unknown or identified individuals in various poses and costumes.

label: French Lithographic Albums 19th Century
description: A collection of French lithographic albums from the early 19th century featuring narrative scenes of domestic life, feminine portraiture, and social subjects, primarily created for annual or thematic publications.

label: Edo Period Japanese Iron Armor
description: These objects are iron-crafted weapons and armor components from Japan's Edo period (1615-1868), primarily sword guards (tsuba) and shin guards, representing functional and decorative elements of samurai martial equipment.

label: Moroccan Amazigh Cultural Textiles
description: These objects represent the traditional dress, adornment, and domestic textiles of Moroccan Amazigh (Berber) communities, featuring characteristic weaving techniques, embroidery, and decorative elements including silk, wool, and embellishments like sequins and metallic threads.

label: Surrealist black and white photography
description: A collection of French surrealist and experimental black and white photographs from mid-20th century, predominantly featuring dreamlike imagery, mysterious figures, and references to notable artists like Max Ernst, Joan Miró, and Marcel Duchamp.

label: Miao and Yao Ethnic Textiles
description: Collection of handmade clothing, accessories, and household textiles from the Miao and Yao ethnic minority cultures of Southeast Asia, primarily characterized by cotton and silk construction with decorative needlework, embroidery, and embellishments.

label: 19th-Century Stereograph Photography
description: These objects are albumen silver print stereographs from the Getty Museum's photography collection, primarily depicting architectural landmarks and urban scenes in Mexico and Latin America, created during the era when stereographic photography was a popular medium for documenting and viewing distant places.

label: Japanese Kabuki Theater Actor Portraits
description: These are woodblock prints (nishiki-e) depicting Japanese kabuki actors in various theatrical roles, created during the Edo and Meiji periods as a popular form of theatrical documentation and fan artwork.

label: European Decorative Tableware
description: A collection of 18th-19th century ceramic dining and serving objects from European manufacturers, primarily French, featuring plates, platters, and salt dishes made using techniques like faience, porcelain, and tin-glazed earthenware.

label: Nineteenth-century photographs of Italian antiquities
description: These are albumen print and early photographic documents depicting archaeological sites and monuments in Rome and Pompeii, created during the 19th century when photography was used to record classical ruins and historical architecture.

label: Victorian British Cathedral Stereographs
description: A collection of gelatin silver print stereograph cards documenting the interior architectural details and spaces of prominent English medieval cathedrals and priories, including Winchester, Canterbury, Ely, Christchurch, Gloucester, and Salisbury, created for Victorian stereoscopic viewing.

label: Decorative Typography and Ornamental Design
description: These objects represent decorative and functional uses of alphabetic letterforms and ornamental elements across different cultures and time periods, from Renaissance French woodcut initials to Japanese metalwork embellishments.

label: Ancient Mediterranean Oil Containers
description: These objects are small vessels designed for storing and dispensing oil, produced across Mediterranean cultures including Greek, Cypriot, and Eastern Mediterranean regions from the Archaic through Hellenistic periods, using materials such as terracotta, glass, and stone.

label: Renaissance engraving series collections
description: These are original engravings organized as numbered series on biblical and allegorical themes, likely from a 16th-century Northern European printmaker, each part of multi-piece suites cataloged in standard art historical references.

label: Religious and Royal Narrative Prints
description: A collection of early modern prints depicting scenes from the lives of Mary and royal figures, particularly Margaret of Austria and Philip III, produced across Northern Europe using etching and engraving techniques.

label: Tiraz Textiles from Islamic Dynasties
description: These objects are fragments and complete examples of tiraz—prestigious inscribed textiles produced in medieval Islamic Egypt and Iraq—characterized by woven or embroidered calligraphic bands and often incorporating gold thread, spanning from the 9th through 12th centuries.

label: French Regional Headdresses
description: A collection of decorative headdresses from 19th-century France, characterized by intricate embroidery, lace, and textile techniques, representing regional folk costume traditions.

label: Les Caprices 50-print series
description: These engravings are individual prints from a famous 50-piece series of capricci (imaginative scenes) originally created in Florence and later re-engraved in Nancy, combining Renaissance architectural landmarks, commedia dell'arte figures, and fantastical Florentine scenes.

label: Historical Decorative Timepieces
description: A collection of ornamental watches from European cultural centers (16th-19th centuries) featuring precious metal cases, often embellished with enameling, chasing, filigree, or narrative scenes.

label: Kuba Textile and Fiber Arts
description: These objects represent the sophisticated textile, fiber, and decorative arts traditions of the Kuba people from the Democratic Republic of Congo, featuring distinctive raffia work, embroidery, and elaborately decorated garments.

label: Dutch menswear clothing collection
description: This collection comprises historical Dutch garments from the Rijksmuseum, primarily consisting of vests, jackets, and trousers from various periods, made from materials like wool, silk, linen, and cotton, many featuring decorative elements such as embroidery, buttons, and ornamental details.

label: Photographs of Classical and Academic Sculptures
description: These objects are photographic prints (primarily albumen silver and salted paper prints) documenting classical sculptures, allegorical statues, and academic artworks featuring mythological and figurative subjects.

label: Nineteenth-Century Portrait Card Photographs
description: These are card photographs from the 19th century featuring unidentified men and couples, predominantly made using albumen silver print technology, characterized by Victorian-era facial hair styles like moustaches and muttonchops.

label: Sámi Portrait Collotypes
description: A collection of collotype prints featuring portraits of Sámi individuals, created through French photographic technology and documented in the Getty Museum's photography department.

label: Architectural fragments medieval Brittany
description: These objects are primarily Romanesque architectural elements, particularly capitals and columns dating from the 12th-15th centuries, carved from stone and preserved mainly at the Musée de Bretagne in Rennes.

label: Roman Ceramic Vessels
description: Collection of Roman-period pottery vases and fragments made from turned ceramic or clay, primarily housed in the Musée Antoine Vivenel in Compiègne.

label: Miao Textile Arts and Crafts
description: These objects represent the diverse textile traditions of the Miao people, including embroidery, batik, quilting, and needlework techniques using cotton, silk, and metal embellishments.

label: American Prints from Arts of Americas
description: This collection comprises primarily wood engravings and other prints held in the Miami Museum of Art's Department of Arts of the Americas, featuring both portraits of notable American literary and cultural figures and imaginative literary subjects from the United States.

label: Chicago Fire Documentary Photography
description: These albumen silver print stereographs and prints document the ruins and devastation of the Great Chicago Fire of October 9th, 1871, capturing both iconic buildings and street scenes from the disaster.

label: Dutch Architectural Topographical Drawings
description: These are drawings depicting specific architectural structures, landscapes, and settlements across the Dutch landscape, primarily from the Netherlands and surrounding regions, created using various combinations of paper, ink, chalk, and watercolor.

label: Architectural Photography of Historic Cathedrals
description: These objects are primarily 19th-century photographic prints and photographs documenting the interior and exterior architectural details of Westminster Abbey and other significant British cathedrals and chapels.

label: Jingdezhen Porcelain, Qing Dynasty
description: A collection of Chinese porcelain vessels from the Qing Dynasty (primarily Kangxi and Yongzheng periods) produced at the renowned Jingdezhen kilns in Jiangxi Province, showcasing imperial-quality ceramics with distinctive forms and glazes.

label: Pages from French Sketchbook
description: These objects are individual pages from a single bound French sketchbook created in graphite, containing diverse observational studies of figures, animals, architecture, and landscapes.

label: French Decorative Watchmaking Objects
description: A collection of French horological objects from the decorative arts, primarily watch keys and timepieces crafted with precious metals, including gold, silver, and gilding, dating from the Paris museums collection.

label: Pre-Columbian Peruvian Textiles
description: These objects are woven and textile artifacts from various pre-Columbian Peruvian cultures (Nasca, Wari, Chimú, Chancay, and Recuay) spanning from the 8th century through the 15th century, created using traditional techniques such as tapestry weaving, braiding, and interlocking with materials like cotton and camelid wool.

label: 17th Century Bohemian Etchings
description: A collection of etchings from the Bohemian culture period, primarily depicting architectural landmarks, portraits, and allegorical scenes created on laid and wove paper.

label: Russian Imperial Fabergé Objects
description: These are decorative luxury items from St. Petersburg, Russia, crafted with precious materials like gold, gemstones, and enamel, characteristic of the renowned Fabergé workshop's exquisite miniature and ornamental designs.

label: Italian Renaissance Fresco Photographs
description: These are albumen print photoreproductions of Renaissance frescoes and paintings from major Italian churches and palaces, primarily documenting works by masters like Raphael, Benozzo Gozzoli, and Buffalmacco in locations such as the Camposanto in Pisa, the Farnesina in Rome, and Florentine churches.

label: 19th-century European architectural photography
description: These objects are cabinet photographs and albumen prints documenting notable buildings, public spaces, and architectural landmarks across the Low Countries, Central Europe, and Austria from the 19th century, primarily mounted on cardboard with paper supports.

label: Commemorative medals from exhibitions and societies
description: These are numismatic medals issued to commemorate late 19th and early 20th century European exhibitions, professional congresses, sporting events, and organizational celebrations, primarily from Belgium and France.

label: Romantic Opera and Musical Lithographs
description: French 19th-century lithographic prints illustrating scenes from Romantic operas, musical compositions, and literary works by composers like Wagner, Berlioz, and Schumann.

label: French Medieval Architectural Documentation Prints
description: A series of prints from the 'Architecture Pittoresque' publication documenting 15th and 16th-century French castles, châteaux, and historic monuments across various French regions.

label: Pages from French Florilegium
description: These objects are individual folio pages from a single French bound volume of botanical and natural history watercolor illustrations created as a comprehensive study of flowers, plants, insects, and wildlife.

label: Eighteenth-century embroidery design patterns
description: These objects are primarily French 18th-century drawings and designs for decorative floral and botanical motifs intended for embroidered waistcoats (gilets), with one example showing the finished embroidered garment.

label: Chinese Buddhist Sutra Protective Textiles
description: These objects are silk textile covers from China designed to protect and preserve Buddhist sutras, featuring elaborate weaving techniques and often embellished with gold-leaf-over-lacquered-paper strips.

label: Early 20th-century ethnographic sketches
description: These works are pencil and ink drawings documenting rural French folk life, occupations, and traditional practices from regions like Franche-Comté and Pyrénées Orientales, created primarily in the second quarter of the 20th century.

label: Parisian Architecture and Urban Landmarks
description: A series of etched prints documenting historical Parisian buildings, bridges, churches, and urban sites, predominantly from the 19th century, created to preserve the visual record of the city's architectural heritage.

label: Spanish architectural stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereograph photographs documenting Spanish religious and historic architecture, primarily from Andalusia and Catalonia, created for English-speaking audiences during the 19th century.

label: Pages from Verona Sketchbook
description: These objects are individual pages from a single 18th-century Italian bound sketchbook featuring anatomical studies and figure drawings executed in chalk.

label: 19th Century French Lithographic Prints
description: Collection of French lithographic and graphic art prints from Paris museums, primarily featuring humorous domestic scenes and Parisian street views from the 19th century.

label: Classical and Mythological Subject Prints
description: A French lithographic print series depicting episodes from ancient Greek and Roman history, mythology, and literature, created for the Paris museums collections.

label: Jewish Ceremonial Objects
description: These are religious and cultural objects used in Jewish rituals and observances, primarily from European Jewish communities, including items for holiday celebrations, Torah adornment, and prayer practices.

label: Miao Silver Adornments and Jewelry
description: These objects represent the distinctive silver jewelry and ornamental traditions of the Miao people of China, showcasing their cultural practices of personal adornment across multiple wearable forms.

label: European Ecclesiastical Vestments
description: These objects are chasubles, ornate sleeveless outer vestments worn by clergy during religious ceremonies, predominantly from Italy, Spain, and France, crafted from luxurious silk and embellished with metallic threads and embroidery.

label: German Expressionist Etching Prints
description: These are primarily German etching and aquatint prints from the 19th-20th centuries, many from thematic series like 'A Life,' 'A Glove,' and 'On Death' that explore existential and emotional subjects characteristic of German Expressionism.

label: Early European Photographic Techniques
description: These objects represent the evolution of photographic printing methods from the 1840s through early 20th century, including albumen prints, calotypes, gelatin silver prints, photogravures, and salted paper prints, primarily documenting European subjects and landscapes.

label: Clichy Glass Paperweights
description: These objects are decorative glass paperweights from the Clichy glassworks, a renowned 19th-century French manufacturer known for producing ornamental pressed and molded glass objects.

label: Magdalenian bone and antler tools
description: These objects are Upper Paleolithic hunting and ceremonial implements crafted from reindeer antler and deer bone, featuring perforations and carved decorations typical of Magdalenian period material culture.

label: 1848 French Monetary Competition Dies
description: These objects are dies, punches, and metal rings used to strike coins submitted for the 1848 French monetary design competition, representing the technical apparatus of experimental numismatic production during the Second Republic.

label: Maya Textile Cultural Dress
description: These objects are traditional woven and embroidered textiles from various Maya cultures (K'iche', Kaqchikel, Tzotzil, and Tzeltal) that serve as distinctive clothing and adornment items reflecting indigenous identity and cultural practices.

label: Moche Ceramic Sculptural Vessels
description: These objects are Peruvian ceramic vessels from the Moche culture featuring handle spouts and narrative scenes depicting animals, humans, daily activities, and supernatural subjects characteristic of Moche artistic expression.

label: Japanese Woodblock Printed Books
description: These objects are instructional and artistic woodblock printed books from Japan, featuring illustrated guides for various subjects including picture albums, design inspiration, and technical instruction for artisans and artists.

label: Renaissance and Baroque Putto Studies
description: These objects are drawings and watercolors from European museums depicting putti (cherubic figures), primarily from Italian Renaissance and Baroque artistic traditions, executed in various media including chalk, ink, and wash techniques.

label: Judith and Holofernes Narratives
description: These objects depict the biblical story of Judith beheading the Assyrian general Holofernes, a popular subject across European art from the Renaissance through modern periods, represented in multiple media including prints, paintings, drawings, and sculpture.

label: Pictorialist Nature Photography Platinum Prints
description: These objects are primarily platinum print photographs from the early 20th century featuring naturalistic subjects like landscapes, plants, and water, characteristic of the Pictorialist movement's aesthetic approach to photography.

label: French Street Vendors and Occupations
description: A collection of French graphic prints depicting common tradespeople, street criers, and laborers from Paris, primarily from the Carnavalet Museum's historical holdings.

label: American Social Documentary Photography
description: A collection of gelatin silver prints documenting American life and labor during the mid-20th century, focusing on working-class subjects, poverty, and urban/rural landscapes across regions including the American South, Cuba, and New York City.

label: 17th Century French Etching Series
