description: These prints are etched works from France, primarily from the 17th century, featuring character studies and satirical scenes organized in thematic series such as 'The Caprices,' 'Varie Figure Gobbi' (grotesque hunchbacked figures), and depictions of commedia dell'arte performers and noblemen.

label: Nineteenth-century Chicago stereograph views
description: These objects are albumen silver print stereographs documenting Chicago's downtown architecture, streets, and landmarks from the mid-to-late 1800s, created for three-dimensional viewing.

label: French Religious Etching Series
description: These are primarily 17th-century French etched prints depicting scenes of martyrdom of apostles and saints, along with scenes from the Life of the Virgin, representing key religious narrative series produced in France during this period.

label: Cyanotype Botanical Fern Studies
description: A collection of cyanotype photographic prints documenting fern species from Britain and the British Empire, created using the distinctive blue-toned cyanotype technique.

label: Pages from Single Italian Sketchbook
description: These objects are individual pages from the same Italian Sketchbook held at the Cleveland Museum of Art, created by an American artist using pencil to sketch Venetian scenes, figures, and architectural elements during travels in Italy.

label: Japanese Woodblock Print Books
description: These are illustrated books from the Rijksmuseum collection featuring Japanese color woodcut prints (ukiyo-e style), predominantly depicting landscapes, flora, fauna, design patterns, and cultural scenes from Japan.

label: American and European Furniture
description: This collection comprises domestic furniture pieces from the United States and England spanning the 18th-20th centuries, including chairs, tables, and storage pieces in styles such as Chippendale, Hepplewhite, and Sheraton.

label: Venetian Architectural Stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs depicting famous landmarks and scenic views of Venice, Italy, created as popular 19th-century tourist souvenirs for three-dimensional viewing.

label: Hercules and Classical Labors
description: These prints depict scenes from the Twelve Labors of Hercules and related mythological narratives, created across multiple European cultures using various printmaking techniques including engraving, lithography, and woodcut.

label: German Renaissance Ornamental Engravings
description: These are decorative prints from 16th-century Germany featuring engraved ornamental designs with classical and allegorical motifs such as putti, friezes, and mythological figures on paper.

label: Haarlem School landscape prints
description: These are prints from the Haarlem cultural tradition depicting Dutch rural and natural landscapes, created using etching and lithography techniques on paper during the 19th century.

label: Dance of Death engravings
description: A series of paper prints from the Rijksmuseum depicting allegorical scenes of Death encountering various social classes and figures, a popular Northern European artistic theme from the medieval and Renaissance periods.

label: World War I Documentary Drawings
description: A collection of early 20th-century French drawings depicting scenes from World War I, including military figures, battles, troop movements, and civilian experiences during the conflict.

label: Chinese landscape paintings
description: A collection of landscape paintings from Chinese dynasties (Yuan, Ming, and Qing) featuring mountains, rivers, scholars, and nature scenes executed primarily in ink and color on silk or paper.

label: Medieval Illuminated Manuscript Elements
description: These objects are decorative and narrative components from medieval liturgical manuscripts (choirbooks and antiphonaries), created using traditional illumination techniques with tempera, gold, and colored inks to embellish text initials and borders.

label: Indian Textile Fragments and Remnants
description: These objects are textile fragments from India spanning centuries, created using diverse techniques including resist dyeing, ikat, brocading, and printing in both silk and cotton materials.

label: 19th Century Lucknow Photography
description: These albumen prints document Lucknow's architecture and monuments during the British colonial period, capturing iconic structures like the Kaiser Bagh, Residency, and Hussainabad Imambara complex.

label: Northern European Biblical Prints
description: A collection of prints from the Netherlands and Flanders depicting Old Testament narratives, primarily created using etching, engraving, and drypoint techniques during the 16th-17th centuries.

label: Passion of Christ Narrative Prints
description: These are early modern prints depicting scenes from the Passion of Christ (crucifixion, carrying of the cross, deposition) created primarily through etching and engraving techniques on paper by Northern European artists.

label: Early Photography of Parisian Palaces
description: These objects are nineteenth-century photographs and prints documenting the architectural features, pavilions, and facades of the Louvre Palace and Tuileries Palace in Paris, created using early photographic techniques such as albumen prints, salted paper prints, and heliogravure.

label: Japanese Noh Theater Costumes
description: These objects are garments and textiles created for Noh theater performances, including specialized robes like karaori, chōken, nuihaku, and sobatsugi, predominantly made from silk with decorative techniques such as brocading, embroidery, and gold leaf work.

label: Magdalenian Bone and Antler Chisels
description: A collection of prehistoric chisels crafted from reindeer and deer bone/antler during the Magdalenian period, decorated with engraved and incised details, preserved in French archaeological museums.

label: British Silver Vinaigrettes
description: These objects are decorative silver metalwork containers from 18th-19th century Britain, primarily from Birmingham, designed to hold aromatic salts and featuring varied ornamental forms including purses, eggs, watches, and shells.

label: Central Asian Decorative Textiles
description: These objects are embroidered, dyed, and woven textiles from Central Asia (primarily Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan) created using traditional techniques like ikat, suzani embroidery, and chain stitch, representing both functional household items and decorative wall hangings.

label: Medieval hunting manuscript illuminations
description: These are decorated manuscript folios from medieval French and Franco-Flemish sources depicting hunting scenes and wildlife, created with tempera, gold, and silver paint typical of illuminated manuscripts.

label: Asian Skirts and Tube Skirts
description: These objects are skirts and tube skirts from various Asian cultures and regions, primarily made from natural fibers like silk, cotton, and hemp, collected by the Museum of International Art's Asian Art department.

label: Chinese Jade Artifacts
description: These objects are primarily jade pendants, ornaments, and ceremonial disks from ancient China, carved into zoomorphic and symbolic forms and used as jewelry, adornment, and ritual objects.

label: Chinese Figurative and Zodiac Objects
description: This collection comprises predominantly Chinese ceramic figurines and sculptures featuring human figures, zodiac animals, and decorative objects from various dynasties, primarily held in the Asian Art department.

label: Christ-centered narrative drawings
description: These are ink and paper drawings from the Rijksmuseum depicting various scenes and moments from the life, passion, and teachings of Christ, created primarily using pen and brush techniques.

label: French Napoleonic Military Study Sketches
description: These objects are preliminary graphite and wash drawings from early 19th-century France, primarily depicting military subjects including soldiers, horses, cavalry, and equipment alongside figure studies and animal sketches.

label: Lorraine Ducal Ceremonies in Engravings
description: These objects document major ceremonial events in Nancy involving the Dukes of Lorraine through a series of detailed engravings, including the 1608 funeral procession of Charles III and the 1610 entry of Henri II.

label: Bijouterie émaillée régionale bressane
description: Collection de bijoux et accessoires de la région de Bresse utilisant la technique de l'émail champlevé, datant principalement du 19e et 20e siècles, caractérisés par des motifs colorés polychromes et l'association de métaux précieux (or, argent) et de matériaux nobles (perles, verres).

label: Mary Cassatt's Printmaking Works
description: This collection comprises prints and drawings by American Impressionist artist Mary Cassatt, predominantly featuring intimate domestic scenes of women and depicting everyday activities such as bathing, reading, tea drinking, and leisure moments, created using various printmaking techniques including etching, aquatint, drypoint, and softground etching.

label: Ancient Greek and Roman Rings
description: A collection of finger rings from ancient Greek and Roman cultures, primarily featuring engraved bezels with mythological figures, deities, and symbolic imagery.

label: Stereograph Views of Philadelphia
description: A collection of 19th-century stereograph photographs primarily documenting Fairmount Park and other Philadelphia landmarks using albumen silver print technology.

label: Title pages for print series
description: These objects are decorative title pages and opening sheets created using intaglio printing techniques (etching, engraving, lithography) on paper to introduce multi-print series spanning various subjects from natural history to narrative cycles across European cultures.

label: Ancient Egyptian Flint Harvest Tools
description: These are flint sickle blades from Dynasty 12 Egypt used for harvesting grain during the Middle Kingdom period.

label: Momoyogusa illustrated series volumes
description: These objects are individual volumes or chapters from the Meiji-period Japanese illustrated work 'Flowers of a Hundred Worlds' (Momoyogusa), featuring decorative designs organized thematically around plants, seasonal scenes, mythological subjects, and everyday objects.

label: French Electro-Physiological Photography Series
description: These albumen silver prints are numbered figures from a single French scientific publication documenting physiological phenomena through electrical stimulation photography, likely from Guillaume-Benjamin Duchenne de Boulogne's mid-19th century research on facial expressions and muscle movement.

label: Medieval Apocalypse Manuscript Illustrations
description: These objects are illuminated folios and manuscript pages depicting scenes from the Book of Revelation, created in medieval English and Franco-Flemish cultures using tempera, gold leaf, and ink.

label: Medieval and Renaissance Altar Textiles
description: These objects are decorative and functional textiles used in Christian religious ceremonies, primarily altar frontals and chalice covers from European churches (Spain, Italy, France, Germany) made from the 16th-18th centuries using silk, linen, and gold/silver threads with embroidery and ornamental techniques.

label: English Landscape Mezzotint Prints
description: A collection of mezzotint prints depicting English countryside scenes, primarily based on paintings by John Constable, created in the 19th century and held across major museums.

label: Ragamala and Barahmasa Series
description: These objects are illustrated pages from Indian musical and seasonal narrative cycles, primarily from Rajasthan and the Himalayan regions, created with opaque watercolor and gold depicting ragas (melodic modes) and seasonal themes with romantic or devotional imagery.

label: Folios from a Medieval Book of Hours
description: These objects are individual pages from a single illuminated manuscript—the Hours of Charles the Noble—a 15th-century Paris-made devotional book featuring religious text and saint imagery.

label: French Colonial and Metropolitan Coinage
description: This collection comprises coins from the French Third Republic and French protectorates/colonies spanning the late 19th to early 20th centuries, representing France's monetary systems across its metropolitan and overseas territories.

label: Late Antique Textile Fragments
description: These are woven textile fragments from the 4th-11th centuries, predominantly composed of wool and linen, preserved at the Musée du Moyen Âge in Paris, featuring decorative motifs ranging from geometric patterns to figurative scenes.

label: Dutch maritime and landscape etchings
description: A collection of original etchings (estampes) depicting Dutch and Parisian scenes with emphasis on maritime subjects, Amsterdam views, and seaports, unified by their Hollstein catalogue references and production as graphic arts prints.

label: Early 20th Century Vendée Regional Studies
description: A series of drawings and sketches documenting the landscapes, architecture, and local heritage of the Vendée region in western France, created primarily in the first half of the 20th century using various drawing and painting techniques on paper.

label: Italian Renaissance Allegorical Engravings
description: A collection of 15th-16th century Italian prints depicting classical and cosmic themes including planetary bodies, virtues, muses, liberal arts, and allegorical figures, primarily from Ferrara and representing a systematic iconographic program.

label: Renaissance and Baroque Pendants
description: These objects are decorative pendants from European museums, primarily from the 16th-18th centuries, crafted with precious materials like gold and enamel, often featuring religious imagery, mythological subjects, or naturalistic forms worn as costume accessories or jewelry.

label: 18th-19th Century European Textile Design
description: These objects represent the design and production of printed and woven textiles from France and England during the 18th-19th centuries, including both finished fabrics and preparatory design documents that showcase printing techniques such as woodblock, copperplate, and block printing.

label: Biblical narratives of Moses
description: These objects are prints, drawings, and designs from the Rijksmuseum depicting scenes from the life and ministry of Moses across various Old Testament episodes.

label: European Portrait Art, 16th-19th Century
description: These objects are portraits and sculptural representations of notable European figures from the Renaissance through the 19th century, primarily from Italian and Spanish cultural contexts, created using oil painting and marble sculpture techniques.

label: French Lithographic Print Series
description: A collection of 19th-century French lithographic and etching prints, primarily depicting rural scenes, countryside life, and architectural subjects, mostly organized as part of a numbered album series.

label: Byzantine Gold Imperial Coinage
description: These objects are gold coins (solidi, nomismata, and tremisses) issued by Byzantine emperors from the 4th to 11th centuries, featuring imperial portraiture and serving as symbols of state authority and currency throughout the Byzantine Empire.

label: Ancient Greek Lekythos Vessels
description: These objects are all lekythos vessels—narrow-necked oil jars used in ancient Greece for storing and pouring oil, made primarily from terracotta using various decorative techniques (black-figure, red-figure, and white-ground), and dating to the Classical period.

label: 19th Century French Albumen Photographs
description: A collection of albumen print photographs and prints documenting French locations and military subjects, primarily from the Camp de Châlons military encampment and Parisian landmarks, created during the mid-1800s.

label: Post-WWII Documentary Photography
description: A collection of gelatin silver print photographs documenting human conditions, war aftermath, and daily life across Asia, Europe, and North America in the post-World War II era, featuring subjects including prisoners of war, refugees, and social documentation.

label: French Treasury Administration Tokens
description: These numismatic tokens from medieval through 18th-century France document the official seals and authorizations of royal treasury officials and military treasurers, serving as administrative credentials or accounting markers.

label: Maya Huipil Blouses
description: These objects are traditional handwoven blouses (huipiles) from various Maya cultures, created using cotton and other fibers with decorative techniques like supplementary weft patterning and embroidery, representing important aspects of Maya women's clothing and cultural identity.

label: European Flintlock Firearms
description: These objects are early modern European firearms featuring flintlock or miquelet firing mechanisms, primarily from the 17th-18th centuries, representing decorative and functional arms from major gunmaking centers across Europe.

label: Historical Glass Vessels and Containers
description: These objects are primarily hand-blown and decorative glass vessels—including flasks, bottles, and drinking glasses—produced across North America and Europe from the 19th to early 20th centuries, representing various regional glassmaking traditions and manufacturing techniques.

label: Eighteenth-Century French Fashion Prints
description: These are hand-colored engravings and etchings from French fashion publications documenting contemporary clothing styles, hairstyles, and social scenes from the 1700s.

label: O'odham Basketry Collections
description: These objects are traditional baskets and basketry items created by the Akimel O'odham (Pima) and Tohono O'odham (Papago) peoples of the American Southwest, crafted from natural plant fibers and held in museum collections.

label: 18th-19th Century French Fashion Plates
description: These objects are illustrated fashion plates and journals from France spanning the late 18th to early 19th century, documenting contemporary clothing styles through hand-colored prints published in periodicals dedicated to fashion.

label: Paris Modern Art Museum Collections
description: These objects are all held in the Modern Art Museum of Paris (Musée d'Art Moderne de Paris) and represent diverse contemporary and modern artistic practices including photography, painting, drawing, and installation art from the 20th-21st centuries.

label: Ancient Asian Ceremonial and Military Objects
description: These objects represent weapons, ceremonial implements, and decorative items from ancient China and Persia, primarily dating to early Bronze Age and Zhou Dynasty periods, crafted from jade, bronze, and other precious materials for ritual, warfare, and aristocratic use.

label: Italian Landscape Drawings
description: A collection of drawings depicting idealized or imaginary Italian landscapes, architectural ruins, and pastoral scenes created by Northern European artists during the 16th-18th centuries.

label: Colorado Landscape Stereographs
description: A collection of 19th-century stereograph photographs documenting the natural landmarks and geological formations of Colorado, particularly around the Garden of the Gods, Pike's Peak, and Manitou region, using albumen silver print technique.

label: Japanese Decorated Papers
description: These objects are decorative papers from Japan featuring various geometric and floral patterns, often produced in identical series sets, representing traditional Japanese paper arts.

label: 13th-Century French Illuminated Bible
description: These objects are pages from a single illuminated Bible manuscript produced in southern France (likely Toulouse) during the 13th century, featuring historiated initials that illustrate biblical narratives and figures.

label: Decorative design pattern plates
description: These are French graphic art drawings categorized as decorative 'planches' (plates) featuring various historical and cultural styles including Greco-Roman, Renaissance, Louis XV-XVI, Empire, and Indian motifs, created with graphite pencil and ink on paper for ornamental design purposes.

label: Miao Cultural Textile Accessories
description: These objects are functional and decorative textile accessories from the Miao ethnic culture of China, primarily belts and headbands made from cotton and natural materials, often part of traditional dress ensembles.

label: Late 19th Century Commemorative Trading Cards
description: A series of chromolithographed trading cards from the fourth quarter of the 19th century featuring prominent figures from French and European history, including medieval kings, statesmen, military leaders, and cultural figures, produced using gold leaf on cardboard.

label: Chinese Ethnic Headwear and Accessories
description: A collection of decorative hats, garments, and ornamental objects from Chinese ethnic minority cultures (particularly Miao and Bai peoples) made from traditional materials like silk, cotton, and embellished with beads, metal, and sequins for ceremonial and daily wear.

label: European Linen Needle Lace
description: A collection of decorative linen laces primarily from Italy and Spain (15th-19th centuries) created using needle lace techniques including filet, reticella, and drawnwork, featuring Renaissance and floral motifs.

label: Italian Needle Lace Fragments
description: A collection of lace fragments primarily from Italy (especially Venice) spanning the 1600s-1800s, made using needle lace techniques including cutwork, reticella, and drawnwork on linen.

label: James McNeill Whistler Works
description: These objects are drawings, paintings, and studies by American artist James McNeill Whistler, spanning his career with works depicting landscapes, figures, and scenes from Europe and America rendered in various media including oil, watercolor, pencil, and pastel.

label: Miao Silver Paired Bracelets
description: These objects are silver bracelets from the Miao culture, presented as matching pairs that served as traditional adornment within Asian heritage.

label: Drinking Vessels Across Cultures
description: These objects are tankards—functional drinking vessels made from diverse materials including silver, ceramic, glass, and pewter—created across multiple cultures and time periods from Europe to Asia to the Americas.

label: Swiss Photographer's Greece Series
description: A collection of heliogravure and photolithograph prints by a Swiss photographer documenting landscapes, archaeological sites, and daily life throughout Greece, including Peloponnesian regions, islands, and classical monuments.

label: 19th Century Portrait Prints
description: A collection of lithographic and engraved portraits from 19th-century Paris museums, depicting prominent figures including actors, musicians, writers, and historical personalities, primarily in the medium of graphic arts.

label: Islamic Ceramic Vessels and Tableware
description: These objects are decorative and functional ceramics produced across the Islamic world (primarily Iran, Iraq, Syria, and Turkey) from the 10th through 18th centuries, characterized by sophisticated glazing techniques, painted and incised decoration, and regional ceramic traditions such as Mina'i ware, Kashan ware, and lusterware.

label: East Asian Lacquerware Objects
description: These objects are decorative and functional vessels from East Asia (primarily China, Korea, and Japan) created using lacquer techniques including carved cinnabar lacquer, mother-of-pearl inlay, and painted lacquer decoration, spanning from the medieval period through the Joseon Dynasty.

label: Ancient Egyptian Funerary Objects
description: These objects are artifacts from ancient Egypt designed to accompany or represent the deceased in the afterlife, including coffins, shabtis (servant figurines), mummies, funerary portraits, and texts like the Book of the Dead.

label: Early Photography of Egypt and North Africa
description: A collection of 19th-century salted paper prints documenting Egyptian monuments, temples, and North African sites, created by early photographers using paper and waxed paper negatives to capture ancient architectural heritage.

label: Decorative Textile Trims
description: These objects are all decorative trims and braids made using passementerie techniques, used as embellishments on clothing and furnishings across European and American collections.

label: Goya's Los Caprichos Series
description: These are individual prints from Francisco Goya's satirical series Los Caprichos, created using etching and aquatint techniques to critique 18th-century Spanish society, superstition, and human folly.

label: Venetian Character Head Studies
description: A series of etched prints from 18th-century Venice depicting diverse male character types—old men, bearded figures, and men in exotic headdress—created by the Tiepolo workshop as compositional studies for narrative paintings.

label: European Decorative Textile Covers
description: These objects are embroidered and decorated textile covers from European cultures (primarily Italy, Spain, Russia, Hungary, and Mexico) created using various techniques including embroidery, lace, and drawn work on linen, silk, and wool bases.

label: European Decorative Vases and Vessels
description: These objects are primarily vases and vessel forms from European decorative arts traditions, spanning from delftware and porcelain to Art Nouveau and Bauhaus design, manufactured across England, France, Germany, Austria, and the Netherlands.

label: Domestic Metalware and Tableware
description: A collection of functional household vessels and dining objects crafted from various metals including pewter, silver, and Sheffield plate, primarily from England and Europe dating from the Queen Anne period through the Arts and Crafts movement.

label: Early American Landscape Photography
description: A collection of 19th-century photographic prints documenting American western and northeastern landscapes, geological features, and settlements using pioneering photographic techniques such as salted paper and albumen silver processes.

label: Hercules Mythological Labors Series
description: A collection of engravings and etchings depicting scenes from the Twelve Labors of Hercules and other episodes from classical mythology, predominantly created by German artists in the 16th-17th centuries.

label: Dutch Rural Landscape Print Series
description: These are etched prints from three interconnected 17th-century Dutch landscape series depicting peasant life, farmyards, villages, and countryside scenes from the Netherlands.

label: Eighteenth-century French embroidery designs
description: This collection comprises design drawings for ornately embroidered waistcoats (gilets) from 18th-century France, featuring diverse decorative motifs including floral patterns, mythological scenes, insects, and architectural elements intended to guide embroidery craftspeople.

label: Ethnographic documentation drawings 1920s-1950s
description: These objects are primarily pencil and gouache drawings on paper created during the early-to-mid 20th century to document French folk culture, traditional crafts, proverbs, and domestic architectural details from regions like Brittany and the Loiret.

label: Ancient Seals and Signet Gems
description: These objects are functional seals from Bronze Age through later ancient cultures, used for authentication and decoration, crafted from natural stone and gemstones across Mediterranean, Near Eastern, and Asian civilizations.

label: Ancient Terracotta Vessel Fragments
description: These objects are ceramic vessels and vessel fragments made from terracotta, primarily from Mediterranean cultures (Greek, Roman, South Italian, Cypriot, and Etruscan), spanning multiple centuries and serving various functions from storage to ritual use.

label: American Portrait and Figurative Sculpture
description: These objects are primarily American sculptures from the 19th-20th centuries depicting portraits, historical figures, and figural compositions, predominantly created in bronze and housed in major American museums.

label: Japanese Woodblock Travel Route Maps
description: These prints are sections from series depicting famous Japanese routes and provinces, including the Tokaido Road and Fifty-three Stations, created as educational cutout puzzles (harimaze) during the Edo period.

label: Italian Renaissance Silk Velvets
description: These textile fragments are primarily Italian-made silk velvets from the Renaissance period, characterized by sophisticated weaving techniques combining pile warps with gilt-metal strips to create luxurious patterned fabrics.

label: Early Scottish Photography
description: These objects are primarily salted paper prints from the mid-19th century, predominantly depicting Scottish subjects including architectural landmarks in St Andrews, portraits of notable Scottish figures, and scenes of Scottish life and institutions.

label: Early Modern European Ceramic Jugs
description: This collection consists primarily of decorated earthenware and stoneware jugs from 16th-17th century Europe, particularly from German and Dutch ceramic traditions, many featuring heraldic arms, religious imagery, or relief decoration techniques.

label: 19th Century Albumen Photographs of Versailles
description: This collection comprises albumen print photographs documenting the architectural features, fountains, sculptures, and gardens of the Palace of Versailles from the 19th century.

label: Prodigal Son Biblical Narrative Prints
description: These objects are prints and drawings depicting scenes from the Parable of the Prodigal Son, created across Northern Europe (Netherlands, Germany, France) from the 16th-17th centuries using various printmaking techniques including engraving and etching.

label: Second Republic Constitution Commemorative Medals
description: These medals commemorate the adoption, proclamation, and promulgation of France's Second Republic Constitution in 1848, documenting key political events and celebrations across French cities.

label: Lyon Political Clubs 1848-1870
description: Commemorative medals documenting the diverse political clubs and provisional committees that emerged in Lyon during the revolutionary periods of 1848 and the founding of the Third Republic in 1870.

label: Rembrandt's Etching Prints
description: A collection of etchings and intaglio prints primarily created by Rembrandt van Rijn and his school in the 17th century, featuring everyday subjects such as beggars, elderly figures, nudes, and artistic studies on paper.

label: Early Modern Parade and Procession Prints
description: These are 16th-century Northern European prints, primarily from Hans Burgkmair's workshop, depicting elaborate processional scenes with riders, soldiers, musicians, and courtly figures arranged in formal arrangements typical of Renaissance triumphal processions and ceremonial pageantry.

label: Sri Lankan Buddhist Manuscript Components
description: These objects are individual leaves and covers from a single Buddhist manuscript created in late 17th century Sri Lanka using traditional fiber paper and gum materials, now housed at the Cleveland Museum.

label: German Nativity Scene Components
description: These objects are individual wooden figurines, animals, and architectural elements from a single German Nativity Scene, created with mixed media including wood, cloth, and paper to depict the birth of Christ and the journey of the Magi.

label: French Kings Character Cards Series
description: A complete 40-print engraved series depicting French monarchs from Merovingian through Renaissance periods, organized by character types and numbered sequentially with catalog references.

label: Prehistoric flaked stone blades
description: These objects are knapped stone blades and blade fragments primarily made of flint, dating from the Paleolithic to Neolithic periods and representing prehistoric cutting and scraping tool technology.

label: French military accounting tokens
description: These objects are French numismatic tokens (jetons) from the 17th-18th centuries used to track ordinary and extraordinary military expenditures during the Ancien Régime.

label: Photographs of artworks and events
description: These objects are photographs documenting paintings, sculptures, monuments, and significant Dutch historical events from the late 19th and early 20th centuries, primarily from The Hague and Amsterdam cultural institutions.

label: Northern Renaissance decorative design drawings
description: A collection of pen and ink design drawings from the 16th-17th century featuring cartouches, coats of arms, and ornamental frames, primarily by Dutch artist Pieter Jansz, created for heraldic displays and stained glass windows.

label: Northern European Oak Sculptures
description: These objects are primarily wooden sculptures carved from oak, created in the Low Countries (Netherlands, Belgium, Antwerp region) during the medieval and early modern periods, predominantly depicting religious subjects including the Virgin Mary, Christ, saints, and biblical narratives.

label: Japanese Bronze Mirrors
description: These objects are predominantly bronze mirrors from Japan spanning the Heian through Edo periods, with one Indonesian bronze mirror, all representing reflective metalwork objects used across East and Southeast Asian cultures.

label: Granite Shore Hotel Rockport Sketchbook
description: Pages from a single American artist's bound sketchbook documenting scenes from Rockport and the surrounding coastal area, featuring landscapes, figures, maritime views, and architectural studies created in graphite and watercolor.

label: 19th Century French Satirical Lithographs
description: A collection of French satirical and caricatural lithographic prints from the 1860s, primarily featuring social commentary on Parisian life, leisure activities, and everyday absurdities, with many being installments of serialized albums.

label: William Blake's Book of Job
description: These objects are engravings and prints from William Blake's illustrated series 'The Book of Job,' created in 18th-19th century England, depicting scenes from the biblical Book of Job through Blake's distinctive artistic style.

label: Dutch Wish Prints (Wensbrieven)
description: These are Dutch printed greeting cards (wensbrieven) from the Netherlands, typically featuring scenes of daily life, celebrations, religious scenes, and seasonal activities, created using various printing techniques like etching and lithography on paper.

label: African Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are utilitarian and ceremonial ceramic vessels from diverse African cultures, primarily made from earthenware or terracotta, representing functional and ritualistic pottery traditions across the continent.

label: 19th Century Greek Photography
description: These objects are albumen silver prints and early photographs of ancient Greek sites, primarily Athens and the Acropolis, created during the 19th century by photographers from multiple European nations documenting classical Greek monuments.

label: Dutch Maritime and Coastal Drawings
description: A collection of drawings from the Rijksmuseum depicting Dutch maritime scenes, ships, coastal landscapes, and beach activities, primarily created by Dutch Golden Age and later artists using paper-based media with ink, watercolor, and chalk.

label: Saint-Louis Glass Paperweights
description: A collection of glass paperweights from the Saint-Louis-lès-Bitche glassworks, a renowned French glass manufacturing center known for decorative and functional glass objects.

label: Zuma Beach Photography Series
description: This collection comprises color and black-and-white photographic prints documenting various views and details of Zuma Beach, created by American photographers using chromogenic, dye imbibition, and gelatin silver print techniques.

label: Eighteenth Century French Ornithological Drawings
description: A collection of French drawings from the 18th century depicting various bird species in different poses and activities, executed primarily in watercolor and black ink.

label: Russian Avant-Garde Photography
description: A collection of gelatin silver prints from the Getty Museum documenting Russian Constructivist and avant-garde artists, intellectuals, and cultural figures from the early 20th century, primarily featuring portrait and documentary photographs.

label: Early 1900s Albany Street Documentation
description: A collection of stereograph photographs from 1902-1903 documenting Albany, New York's streets, residents, businesses, and civic figures using gelatin silver print technology.

label: Pre-Columbian Personal Adornments
description: These objects are ornamental accessories worn on the body—particularly ear spools, ear flares, nose ornaments, and pendants—created by ancient Mesoamerican and South American cultures using materials like gold, stone, shell, and bone.

label: Reinaert de Vos Print Series
description: A collection of etched prints from the Dutch Golden Age depicting scenes from the medieval animal fable 'Reinaert de Vos,' featuring the cunning fox character and his interactions with other anthropomorphic animals in a satirical narrative.

label: Jost Amman's Military Occupations Series
description: A comprehensive collection of paper prints by 16th-century artist Jost Amman depicting various military personnel, ranks, and activities including soldiers, officers, commanders, and associated occupational roles.

label: Depictions of Beggars in Printmaking
description: These objects are unified by their focus on representing beggars and impoverished figures across European art from the 17th to 19th centuries, primarily through etching and aquatint print series.

label: Stipple-engraved portrait prints
description: These are predominantly portrait prints created using stipple engraving technique, featuring both historical figures and literary characters, produced in Italy and held in museum print collections.

label: Nineteenth-century Ithaca landscape stereographs
description: These objects are stereographic photographs documenting the natural waterfalls, gorges, and scenic landscapes surrounding Ithaca, New York, primarily created using albumen silver print technology during the American 19th century.

label: Ditamenpaankh's Egyptian Funerary Collection
description: These objects comprise a complete set of shawabtys and storage boxes from a single individual's tomb, used in Late Period Egypt to serve the deceased in the afterlife.

label: Folios from Perugian Missale
description: These objects are individual folios from a single illuminated Italian missale (liturgical manuscript) from Perugia, featuring calendar pages, musical notation, religious imagery, and decorative elements created with ink, tempera, and burnished gold on vellum.

label: Ancient Cypriot Pottery Collection
description: These objects are terracotta vessels and pottery fragments from various periods of Cypriot civilization (Bronze Age through Hellenistic period), all housed in the Musée des Beaux-Arts in Lille.

label: Ancient Mediterranean Figurative Sculpture
description: A collection of human and divine figurines, statuettes, and sculptural heads spanning from the 8th century BCE to the 4th century CE, primarily crafted from clay and stone materials representing various Mediterranean cultures and religious practices.

label: Fragmented Decorative Vessels
description: These objects are fragments of functional and decorative vessels (bowls, plates, platters, wall revetments) from Mediterranean cultures spanning the Roman Empire through Byzantine periods, made from glass and ceramic materials.

label: Natural History Animal Illustrations
description: These objects are illustrated folios depicting real and mythological animals, created using ink and watercolor techniques across Cretan and English artistic traditions.

label: French lithographic character studies
description: A collection of French lithographic prints from the early 19th century depicting diverse social types, everyday characters, and narrative scenes from Parisian society.

label: Decorative Personal Adornment Objects
description: These objects are wearable jewelry and accessories spanning multiple centuries and cultures, crafted from precious metals, gemstones, and minerals for personal decoration and status display.

label: Ancient Gold Jewelry
description: These objects are decorative adornments made primarily from gold, spanning multiple ancient and medieval civilizations from the Bronze Age through the Middle Ages, including Etruscan, Greek, Roman, Byzantine, and European cultures.

label: European Decorative Porcelain Vases
description: These objects are primarily European ceramic vases from the 16th-20th centuries, with particular emphasis on French (Sèvres, Vincennes) and German (Meissen) porcelain production, often decorated with enamels, gilding, and mounted in gilt bronze.

label: British Military Campaign Photography
description: These albumen silver prints document the British military expedition in Afghanistan and the Khyber Pass region during the Second Anglo-Afghan War, featuring landscapes, forts, troops, and strategic positions.

label: Mt. Washington Tourism Documentation
description: A collection of 19th-century stereograph albumen silver prints documenting the natural attractions, transportation infrastructure, and tourist facilities of Mt. Washington, New Hampshire, created to satisfy American travelers' appetite for scenic views and railroad tourism.

label: Religious Subjects in European Prints
description: These objects are European artworks from the Renaissance and Early Modern periods depicting Christian saints, biblical figures, and religious narratives through various printmaking techniques including engraving, etching, and woodcut.

label: Le Bien et le Mal series
description: A collection of French lithographic prints from the Musée Carnavalet exploring the moral dichotomy of good and evil through sequential numbered plates.

label: Eynard Family Daguerreotype Collection
description: Early pioneering daguerreotype photographs from 19th-century Geneva documenting Jean-Gabriel Eynard, his family, social circle, and residences.

label: Edward Curtis Native American Photography
description: These objects are photogravure prints and photographs documenting Native American tribes and their cultural practices, primarily from Edward Curtis's monumental ethnographic project 'The North American Indian' (1907-1930).

label: American Prints by Margaret Jordan Patterson
description: These are prints created by American artist Margaret Jordan Patterson, featuring her distinctive use of woodcuts, etchings, and lithographs often depicting domestic scenes, nature, and Japanese-influenced subjects.

label: Chinese Ceramic Wedding Procession Figures
description: These glazed and unglazed ceramic figurines from China depict participants in a wedding procession, including musicians, attendants, the bride, and family members, likely created as tomb goods or ceremonial objects.

label: German Porcelain Ceramics
description: These objects are primarily decorative porcelain wares manufactured in German-speaking regions (Meissen, Berlin, Vienna, Limbach-Oberfrohna, and Hesse-Kassel) during the 18th century, featuring figurative forms, tableware, and ornamental pieces made from hard-paste porcelain with polychrome enamel decoration.

label: British Drypoint Prints by Muirhead Bone
description: A collection of architectural and landscape prints primarily created using drypoint technique by Scottish-born artist Muirhead Bone, featuring buildings, cityscapes, and maritime scenes from Britain and abroad during the 20th century.

label: Biblical Story of Joseph
description: These objects are prints and drawings depicting scenes from the story of Joseph in the Bible, created across multiple European regions using various printmaking and drawing techniques.

label: British Cathedral Stereograph Collection
description: These objects are albumen silver print stereographs documenting the architectural details and interiors of British and Scottish cathedrals and chapels from the 19th century.

label: Reverberations of Taiga Album
description: These objects are individual leaves and volumes from a two-album Edo period Japanese portfolio work titled 'Reverberations of Taiga,' comprising 72 double-leaves total created during the 17th-19th century.

label: European Decorative Arts Collection
description: These objects are predominantly held in the Metropolitan Museum's Department of European Sculpture and Decorative Arts, representing a diverse range of applied arts including textiles, metalwork, accessories, and furnishings from European tradition.

label: 19th Century Saratoga Springs Resort Tourism
description: These albumen stereograph and early photographs document the mineral springs, grand hotels, and recreational attractions that made Saratoga Springs a celebrated American spa destination and tourist resort in the 1800s.

label: Nineteenth-century photographs of Ottoman and Egyptian monuments
description: This collection consists of photographic prints and stereographs primarily from the mid-to-late 1800s documenting architectural and archaeological sites in the Ottoman Empire (Constantinople) and Egypt (Cairo), made using early photographic techniques such as albumen silver prints and salted paper prints.

label: Thomas Rowlandson's Hand-Colored Etchings
description: This collection comprises satirical and narrative prints created by British artist Thomas Rowlandson in the late 18th and early 19th centuries, primarily using hand-colored etching and aquatint techniques to depict humorous scenes of English social life, leisure activities, and cultural commentary.

label: Photographs by Man Ray
description: This collection comprises photographic prints created by Man Ray, a pioneering photographer and artist of the 20th century, including his experimental rayographs and surrealist-influenced compositions held at the Getty Museum.

label: Piranesi's Imaginary Prisons Series
description: These are etchings from Giovanni Battista Piranesi's 18th-century series 'Carceri d'invenzione' (Imaginary Prisons), featuring fantastical architectural prison interiors that combine classical elements with dreamlike, nightmarish spaces.

label: Degas Studies of Parisian Performers
description: These works by Edgar Degas capture the lives of dancers, singers, and performers in 19th-century Paris through drawings, pastels, watercolors, and sculptures that emphasize movement, posture, and the theatrical world.

label: Dutch and Flemish Portrait Paintings
description: This collection comprises portrait paintings from the Dutch and Flemish regions (Holland, Netherlands, Flanders) created primarily in oil on canvas or panel, representing a distinctive tradition of Northern European portraiture.

label: Pages from Single American Sketchbook
description: These objects are individual pages or page spreads from a single bound graphite sketchbook held at the Cleveland Museum of Art, containing figure studies, landscape sketches, notes, and color notations.

label: Parisian Fashion and Social Types
description: A series of hand-colored etchings documenting Parisian fashion, professions, and social characters from the Revolutionary and Directoire periods, including both working-class trades and fashionable society figures like the Incroyables and Merveilleuses.

label: Italian Renaissance Velvet Fragments
description: These textile fragments are primarily Italian (with one Spanish example) featuring sophisticated cut and voided velvet techniques characteristic of Renaissance luxury textiles, predominantly composed of silk with various weaving foundations.

label: Alpine Landscape Photography Collection
description: A series of albumen silver prints documenting Swiss glaciers, mountains, and geological formations, primarily featuring the Bernese Oberland region.

label: French Revolutionary Federation Commemorations
description: These objects document the Fête de la Fédération and related patriotic events of 1790, commemorating the early revolutionary period through medals, drawings, and a fan from the Musée Carnavalet in Paris.

label: American Industrial Labor Photography
description: A collection of gelatin silver prints documenting American workers across various trades and industries, capturing mid-20th century vocational and manufacturing labor practices.

label: Portrait Photography of Female Athletes
description: These photographs document the muscular physiques and various poses of Lisa Lyon, a pioneering female bodybuilder, captured through gelatin silver prints by a photographer exploring the aesthetics of women's athletic bodies.

label: Biblical narratives in prints
description: A collection of prints depicting Old and New Testament stories, particularly those featuring Solomon, Salome, and other biblical kings, produced across Northern Europe from the Low Countries, Germany, and Italy.

label: Cruikshank's Comic Almanack Series
description: Steel etchings by George Cruikshank created for The Comic Almanacks (1835-1853), a British satirical publication combining calendar events with social and political commentary through humorous illustrations.

label: Ancient Greek Black-Figure Pottery
description: These objects are terracotta amphora fragments from ancient Attic Greece, decorated using the black-figure pottery technique and held in the Getty Museum's vessels collection.

label: Goya's Disasters of War
description: A series of etched prints by Francisco de Goya depicting the brutal violence, suffering, and atrocities of warfare and human cruelty, combining graphic scenes of executions, death, and anguish with surreal and symbolic imagery.

label: Tiffany Studios Decorative Arts
description: These objects are decorative arts from Tiffany Studios, primarily created in early 20th century New York, featuring distinctive favrile glass, leaded glass, and bronze construction techniques characteristic of the Art Nouveau movement.

label: Gauguin's Noa Noa Suite Prints
description: These are woodblock prints from Paul Gauguin's Noa Noa series, created in the 1890s, featuring Tahitian subjects and Polynesian titles that document his spiritual and artistic journey in French Polynesia.

label: Paris Siege Commemorative Medals
description: These medals commemorate the Siege of Paris (1870-1871) during the Franco-Prussian War, documenting key military events, defensive efforts, and notable commanders of this pivotal conflict in French history.

label: 19th-Century Canton Stereograph Documentation
description: These albumen silver print stereographs document Canton (Guangzhou), China in the 19th century, capturing its architecture, religious sites, officials, and daily life for Western audiences through a popular Victorian-era visual medium.

label: French Drawings from Cleveland Museum
description: A collection of drawings from the Cleveland Museum of Art spanning the 17th to early 20th centuries, primarily from French cultural traditions, executed in various media including graphite, chalk, ink, and wash.

label: Tale of Genji in Japanese Art
description: These objects are connected by their visual representations of scenes, characters, and narratives from The Tale of Genji, an 11th-century Japanese literary masterpiece, depicted across multiple artistic media including paintings, screens, albums, and decorative arts primarily from the Edo period.

label: Magdalenian Engraved Stone Pebbles
description: Upper Paleolithic pebbles and stones from the Magdalenian period (and Azilian) deliberately engraved, incised, or decorated, primarily housed in the French National Archaeology Museum's collection.

label: European Women in Regional Dress
description: A series of etchings by Wenceslaus Hollar documenting women from different European regions and social classes through their distinctive traditional costumes and attire.

label: Dutch Civic Guard and Volunteer Honors
description: These objects are medals, crosses, chains, and badges awarded to members of Dutch civic guard companies (schutterijen) and volunteer military organizations to recognize service, marksmanship competitions, and military campaigns from the 19th century.

label: Romanesque Architectural Stone Capitals
description: These sculptures are primarily decorative stone capitals and corbels from Romanesque churches across France, Italy, and Spain (12th-13th centuries), featuring carved figural and animal motifs that functioned as load-bearing ornamental elements in medieval architecture.

label: Oceanic Wood Sculptural Traditions
description: These objects represent the diverse wooden sculpture and woodworking practices of Pacific Island cultures, including Polynesian, Melanesian, and Micronesian peoples, created for ceremonial, ancestral, protective, and utilitarian purposes.

label: Medieval Illuminated Manuscript Letters
description: These are decorated initial letters from medieval manuscripts, primarily from French, Italian, and German scriptoriums, featuring tempera and gold leaf embellishment characteristic of illuminated manuscript production.

label: Eighteenth-century Parisian topographical etchings
description: A collection of French etching prints depicting architectural landmarks and landscape views of Paris and its environs, primarily created by Lantara using eau-forte (etching) technique during the 18th century.

label: Ancient Mediterranean Vessel Forms
description: These objects are functional and decorative vessels spanning multiple ancient Mediterranean cultures (Cypriot, Greek, Roman, Syrian, and related societies) from the Early Bronze Age through the Late Imperial period, made in diverse materials including terracotta, bronze, glass, and ceramic.

label: Asian Personal Adornment Objects
description: These museum objects represent decorative jewelry and personal ornaments from various Asian cultures, including China, Japan, and Thailand, created from precious and semi-precious materials for personal display and cultural expression.

label: European Tapestries and Narrative Textiles
description: A collection of wool and silk tapestries from European institutions (primarily French, Flemish, and Netherlandish) spanning the 16th-18th centuries, featuring allegorical, mythological, historical, and literary narratives.

label: Religious figure drawings from Rijksmuseum
description: A collection of paper drawings depicting Catholic saints, Virgin Mary, and religious scenes from various European artists, primarily created using ink, chalk, and pen techniques.

label: Platinum Print Orchid Documentation Series
description: A comprehensive photographic survey of northeastern United States orchid species documented through platinum prints, all numbered items from a single botanical illustration project.

label: Ancient Engraved Scarab Seals
description: These objects are decorative and functional scarab seals from Mediterranean and Near Eastern cultures (Greek, Etruscan, Phoenician, and others) crafted from gemstones like cornelian and jasper, featuring engraved designs and held primarily in jewelry departments.

label: Roman funerary monuments from Gaul
description: These objects are limestone and marble funerary stelae, cippi, and altars from Roman Gaul (1st-3rd centuries CE) depicting deceased individuals, families, and their professions, primarily held in French municipal museums.

label: The Compleat Angler Photogravure Album
description: These objects are photogravure illustrations from Peter Henry Emerson's illustrated edition of Izaak Walton's 'The Compleat Angler,' depicting English rivers, fishing locations, and countryside scenes associated with the classic fishing guide.

label: Illuminated Manuscript of Roman de la Rose
description: These folios are all pages from a richly decorated medieval French manuscript depicting allegorical scenes from the 13th-century poem 'Roman de la Rose,' featuring personified virtues, vices, and the narrative of courtly love rendered in tempera, gold leaf, and ink.

label: Asian Decorative Boxes and Vessels
description: These objects are primarily Chinese ceramics and decorative containers from the Qing Dynasty and related periods, featuring blue and white porcelain, cobalt decoration, and refined craftsmanship typical of East Asian decorative arts.

label: Portrait Photographs of Georgia O'Keeffe
description: These objects are photographic portraits of the American modernist artist Georgia O'Keeffe, created using various print techniques including platinum, palladium, and gelatin silver prints, held across major American museums.

label: Nikuhitsu Ukiyo-e Hanging Scrolls
description: A collection of Japanese painted woodblock-style artworks from the Edo period, primarily featuring ukiyo-e subjects such as courtesans, actors, and entertainers rendered as hanging scrolls using ink, color, and gold on paper or silk.

label: 19th Century European Card Photographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print card photographs from German and Austrian institutions documenting European landmarks, monuments, artworks, and cultural subjects during the mid-to-late 1800s.

label: European Royalty Portraits
description: A collection of printed and drawn portraits depicting monarchs and royal family members from 17th-19th century European courts, primarily British, Prussian, Spanish, and Austrian rulers, created using various printmaking and drawing techniques on paper.

label: Magdalenian Bone Harpoons
description: These are prehistoric hunting weapons from the Magdalenian period (Upper Paleolithic), primarily made from reindeer bone or antler and decorated with barbed ranks through carving and incision techniques.

label: French Pastoral Etching Series
description: A collection of French etching prints from the Dutuit collection depicting rural livestock and pastoral scenes, created primarily using etching technique (eau-forte).

label: African and Indonesian Edged Weapons
description: This collection comprises traditional bladed weapons and their associated sheaths from African and Southeast Asian cultures, spanning knives, swords, daggers, and axes crafted from iron, metal, and organic materials.

label: Vessels for Ritual and Funerary Use
description: These objects are urns and cinerary vessels from diverse cultures and periods, designed to hold ashes of the deceased or serve ceremonial functions across ancient to modern societies.

label: 18th-19th Century European Women's Garments
description: These objects are decorative women's clothing and accessories from the Rijksmuseum collection, predominantly from the 18th and 19th centuries, featuring silk, elaborate floral embroidery, and ornamental details characteristic of European fashionable dress.

label: Fatehpur Sikri Albumen Print Documentation
description: These are albumen silver prints documenting the architectural structures and monuments of Fatehpur Sikri, the sixteenth-century capital city of the Mughal Empire in India, created primarily for Western audiences.

label: 19th Century Holy Land Views
description: A collection of English prints and drawings from the 19th century depicting landscapes, religious sites, and architectural landmarks throughout the Holy Land region including Jerusalem, Petra, Nazareth, and other biblical locations.

label: Academic Male Nude Studies
description: These are figure drawing studies of male nudes in various poses, created by European artists as foundational exercises in academic art training, primarily using chalk on paper.

label: Roman Imperial Antoninianus Coins
description: These objects are antoninianus coins from the Roman Empire, a silver currency type issued during the 3rd century CE under various emperors including Probus, Trebonianus Gallus, Maximinus II, and Tacitus.

label: Pages from Single Sketchbook
description: All objects are individual pages or page spreads from the same bound sketchbook (The Dells, No. 127) created in America using graphite, held at the Cleveland Museum of Art.

label: Nineteenth-Century Daguerreotype Portraits
description: A collection of early photographic portraits created using the daguerreotype process, featuring various individuals in different poses and plate sizes, many with hand-tinting or hand-coloring applied.

label: Norblin de la Gourdaine Etchings
description: A comprehensive collection of intaglio prints by French artist Jean-Pierre Norblin de la Gourdaine, featuring character studies, genre scenes, and narrative compositions rendered primarily through etching techniques.

label: Indigenous Basketry Across Continents
description: These objects are baskets and basket-like containers made from plant fibers by indigenous cultures from the Americas, Africa, and Asia, representing essential utilitarian and cultural crafts across diverse societies.

label: French Decorative Design Study Sheets
description: These objects are a collection of French graphic art study planches featuring decorative motifs, Coptic civilization references, and diverse cultural designs, created primarily with graphite pencil and ink on paper as research and reference materials.

label: Asian Footwear and Adornment
description: These objects are wearable accessories from Asian cultures, primarily from China (Miao and general Chinese traditions), featuring shoes, boots, and jewelry crafted from natural materials like silk, cotton, and silver.

label: Wedgwood Jasperware Medallions and Plaques
description: These objects are decorative ceramics made from jasperware stoneware by the Burslem pottery culture (Wedgwood), featuring classical subjects, allegorical themes, historical figures, and mythological scenes rendered as relief medallions and plaques.

label: Nineteenth-century Holy Land photography
description: These objects are albumen and salt prints documenting biblical and archaeological sites in Jerusalem and surrounding areas, created primarily by English photographers during the Victorian era's fascination with the Holy Land.

label: Adoration of the Magi Artistic Tradition
description: These objects are diverse artistic interpretations of the biblical Adoration of the Magi scene across multiple media (paintings, prints, drawings, sculptures) created by European artists from the 15th-18th centuries.

label: Folios from Single Printed Book
description: These are individual pages from the same 112-folio printed Book of Hours (Use of Rome) produced in Paris, representing different sections of this devotional manuscript including calendar pages, biblical scenes, and saint illustrations.

label: French Military Historical Engravings
description: These are interpretive engravings documenting French naval and land battles, sieges, and military expeditions from the 15th to 19th centuries, primarily based on paintings by artists like Théodore Gudin, created to commemorate significant French military victories and historical events.

label: Japanese Drawings and Sketches
description: These objects are primarily drawings and sketches from Japanese culture, created across multiple museums using traditional materials like ink and paper, often organized as series or folios from the 19th century onward.

label: Dutch Cut-Paper Silhouettes
description: A collection of 19th-century Dutch cut-paper artworks primarily featuring silhouette portraits and decorative paper cuttings (knipsels) made through snipping and cutting techniques.

label: Ancient Egyptian Funerary Objects
description: These objects are ritual items used in Egyptian burial practices, including shabtis (servant figurines) and canopic jars (vessels for storing mummified organs) spanning from the Middle Kingdom through the Late Period.

label: Tristan Romance Manuscript Illustrations
description: These are illuminated folios from a medieval French manuscript depicting episodes from the Arthurian legend of Tristan, featuring characters such as Tristan, Iseult, King Mark, and various knights engaged in courtly encounters, battles, and adventures.

label: Ancient Eastern Mediterranean Bowls
description: A collection of decorative and functional bowls from ancient Near Eastern, Mediterranean, and Persian cultures spanning multiple millennia, made from materials including glass, silver, stone, and semi-precious materials.

label: Embroidered Needlework Pictures
description: These objects are primarily decorative textile artworks created through embroidery and needlework techniques, featuring pictorial scenes, portraits, and narrative subjects made with silk, linen, and wool threads from primarily English and European origins.

label: Wooden Architectural and Decorative Panels
description: This collection comprises wooden panels and panel-based furniture objects spanning multiple cultures and centuries, ranging from medieval decorative panels to furniture components like doors, screens, and choir stalls.

label: Decorative Vases Across Cultures
description: These objects are ceramic and ceramic-adjacent vessels spanning multiple continents and centuries, united by their classification as vases and their presence in museum decorative arts collections.

label: Catskill Mountains Landscape Photography
description: A collection of 19th-century stereographs and prints documenting the scenic natural landmarks, waterfalls, and geological features of the Catskill Mountains region in New York.

label: Fashion and Figure Photography
description: A collection of gelatin silver prints from the Getty Museum's photography department featuring fashion photography, celebrity portraits, and artistic nude studies from American photographers working primarily in the late 20th century.

label: Depictions of Beggars in Early Modern Art
description: These objects are connected by their common subject matter of beggars (bedelaars in Dutch), depicted across various printmaking and drawing techniques by prominent artists like Jacques Callot and Rembrandt, predominantly from the Netherlands and France during the 16th-17th centuries.

label: Japanese Lacquered Decorative Cases
description: These objects are inro and pipe cases from Japan (primarily Edo and Meiji periods) made using lacquer techniques, often featuring nature-inspired designs and serving as functional accessories for carrying small items.

label: Pages from Single German Sketchbook
description: These objects are individual pages from a unified German artist's album documenting Roman architecture, landscapes, and studies created during travels in Italy and its surroundings.

label: Psyche myth engraving series
description: A comprehensive series of engravings illustrating the narrative of Psyche from Apuleius's Golden Ass, created after Raphael's compositions and catalogued in Bartsch's print reference work.

label: Asian Tea Storage Vessels
description: These objects are decorative tea caddies (cha-ire) from Japan and China, crafted from glazed ceramic materials and used to store precious tea leaves in East Asian tea ceremonies and domestic settings.

label: Ancient Mosaic Architectural Fragments
description: These objects are mosaic floor panels and fragments from Roman and Byzantine periods, created using stone, glass, and marble tesserae, often depicting animals, figures, and decorative motifs for architectural contexts.

label: 19th-century Romantic architectural documentation
description: These prints document medieval and historic European architecture through the romantic and picturesque visual traditions of the 19th century, primarily from two major publishing series focused on France and England.

label: American Platinum Print Photographs
description: These objects are early 20th-century American photographs created using platinum print techniques, a prestigious photographic process favored by pictorialist photographers for its tonal range and permanence.

label: Albumen silver print photographic reproductions
description: These objects are all albumen silver prints held in the Getty's Department of Photographs, primarily consisting of photographic reproductions of sketches depicting narrative scenes with humor, rural life, and social commentary.

label: Victorian Royal Family Portraiture
description: A collection of albumen print photographs and card photographs depicting Queen Victoria, Prince Albert, and members of the British royal family, primarily from the 19th century Getty and Art Institute collections.

label: French Portrait Drawings Across Centuries
description: These objects are portrait drawings from French cultural contexts spanning the 16th through 19th centuries, executed primarily in chalk, pastel, and ink techniques on paper.

label: Eadweard Muybridge's Photographic Motion Studies
description: These objects are prints and photographs from Eadweard Muybridge's groundbreaking 'Animal Locomotion' series, a sequential photographic study documenting movement in animals and humans created in the 1880s.

label: Sports and Athletic Activities Art
description: Oil paintings from the 20th century depicting various sports and athletic pursuits, primarily housed in the French National Sports Museum, spanning boxing, running, rowing, rugby, tennis, fencing, and other sporting events.

label: Animal Carvings in Indigenous Art
description: These objects are primarily small-scale animal figurative sculptures created by Inuit and other indigenous cultures, predominantly carved from ivory and walrus ivory, representing hunting traditions and spiritual practices.

label: Ancient Storage and Liquid Containers
description: A collection of bottles and flasks from Roman and early medieval periods, crafted from ceramic or glass using traditional techniques (turning and blowing), used for storing and transporting liquids.

label: 1904 St. Louis World's Fair Photography
description: A collection of photographs documenting the Louisiana Purchase Exposition (1904 World's Fair) in St. Louis, featuring architectural landmarks, exhibition grounds, visitors, and attractions from the event.

label: Central Asian Embroidered Textiles
description: These objects are embroidered textiles from Central Asian cultures (primarily Lakai and Kungrat), featuring chain stitch and cross-stitch techniques on silk, wool, and cotton, including specialized garment types like ilgich (decorative panels) and at torba ilgich (horse trappings).

label: Early Modernist Industrial Photography
description: A series of collotype prints documenting urban infrastructure, machinery, and architectural structures from early 20th-century France and Europe, characterized by close-up perspectives emphasizing geometric forms and industrial materials.

label: Fragmentary Ancient Drinking Vessels
description: These objects are predominantly fragmentary kantharoi and rhyta (drinking vessels) from ancient Greek and Etruscan cultures, primarily featuring red-figure or black-glaze decoration techniques on terracotta.

label: Ancient and Medieval Belt Fasteners
description: These objects are decorative and functional belt buckles, hooks, clasps, and ornaments spanning multiple ancient and medieval cultures, serving as personal adornment and practical clothing fasteners across Eurasia from antiquity through the Migration Period.

label: Brocade Textiles Across Cultures
description: These objects are connected by their shared use of the brocade weaving technique, a decorative textile method employing silk and often metal threads, produced across multiple continents and centuries from the 16th to 19th centuries.

label: Fireplace and Lighting Metalwork
description: These objects are functional metal pieces used for managing fire and providing light in hearths and homes, including candlesticks, andirons, fire tongs, and related domestic metalwork from various European and American traditions.

label: British Decorative Glassware Collection
description: A collection of drinking vessels and decorative glass objects primarily from England, held in the applied arts of Europe department, representing various forms of ornamental glassware from the 18th-19th centuries.

label: Italian Renaissance Maiolica Ceramics
description: These objects are primarily tin-glazed earthenware plates, chargers, and dishes produced across Italian regions (Urbino, Faenza, Florence, Deruta, Castelli) from the 15th-18th centuries, featuring narrative scenes, heraldic designs, and decorative motifs characteristic of the maiolica tradition.

label: 19th Century British Photography of South Asia
description: These objects are early photographic prints documenting architectural and religious structures in India and Burma, created by British photographers during the colonial period using period photographic techniques like salted paper and albumen silver prints.

label: Battersea Bridge Printmaking Series
description: These objects are a series of prints depicting Battersea Bridge and surrounding areas in London, created using various intaglio and lithographic techniques by a single artist across multiple museum collections.

label: German Half-Timbered Architecture Documentation
description: A systematic photographic survey of traditional framework (half-timbered) houses from the Siegerland region of Germany, captured as gelatin silver prints documenting specific buildings with their street addresses and architectural perspectives.

label: Chinese Snuff Bottles
description: A collection of decorative vessels from China, typically small containers used for holding powdered snuff, crafted from various materials including jade, agate, glass, and porcelain with intricate carved or painted designs.

label: Dutch civic and commemorative medals
description: These objects are Dutch commemorative pieces from the Netherlands including schutterspenningen (civic guard medals) from various cities and memorial tablets, primarily from the medieval and early modern periods, representing local institutional pride and remembrance.

label: Optical Illusion Stereographs
description: A collection of lithographic stereograph cards from the Getty Museum designed to create optical illusions and visual tricks when viewed through a stereoscope, representing 19th-century recreational visual entertainment.

label: Chinese Woodblock Prints Ming-Qing
description: These objects are primarily Chinese woodblock prints from the Ming and Qing dynasties, featuring decorative subjects such as flowers, birds, beautiful women, and literary narratives, with many created using polychrome (color) woodblock printing techniques.

label: Etruscan and Italic Amber Pendants
description: These objects are amber pendants and beads from Etruscan and Italic cultures featuring anthropomorphic and zoomorphic heads, primarily used as jewelry ornaments in ancient Italy.

label: Maritime and Fishing Life Etchings
description: A collection of American etching prints depicting New England coastal scenes, vessels, and fishing activities from maritime communities in Salem, Gloucester, and Maine.

label: Eighteenth-Century Decorative Vessels
description: These objects are ornamental vases and bowls, primarily from French and English porcelain factories, designed as luxury decorative arts pieces featuring lidded forms and often embellished with gilt-bronze mounts or elaborate glazing.

label: Napoleonic Wars Commemorative Medals
description: Bronze medals created by French institutions documenting major military campaigns, battles, and political treaties from the Napoleonic era (1813-1815) and related European historical events.

label: Botanical Illustration Prints
description: These objects are hand-colored engravings and prints depicting flowers, fruits, and plants with scientific precision, primarily from 19th-century European botanical art traditions.

label: Ely Cathedral Architectural Photography
description: A collection of photographs and prints documenting the interior and exterior architectural features of Ely Cathedral in England, captured through various photographic and printing techniques across the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

label: 19th Century American Interior Stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting American interiors—including domestic spaces, commercial establishments, and industrial facilities—from the 19th century, created to provide three-dimensional viewing experiences of contemporary life and architecture.

label: Odilon Redon lithographs
description: A collection of French Symbolist prints created through lithography and color printing techniques, primarily by Odilon Redon, featuring dreamlike, fantastical imagery with literary and mystical themes.

label: French Etching Series of Female Types
description: A collection of French etching prints from Paris museums depicting various female character archetypes and social types through individual portrait compositions.

label: European Decorative Timepieces
description: A collection of ornamental clocks and timekeeping devices from European museums, spanning multiple centuries and cultures, crafted with luxury materials including gilt bronze, precious woods, and enamel for both functional and decorative purposes.

label: Korean Celadon Ceramics with Decorative Motifs
description: These objects are predominantly Korean celadon-glazed stoneware vessels featuring incised, inlaid, or underglaze decorative designs of naturalistic motifs such as flowers, birds, and botanical elements.

label: Spanish/North African figure studies
description: These prints predominantly feature etched depictions of Spanish and North African subjects, including street scenes, occupational figures, and cultural portraits from Spain, Morocco, and Algeria, reflecting 19th-century orientalist and regionalist artistic interests.

label: French Orientalist Photography of the Levant
description: A collection of albumen silver prints by French photographers documenting architectural and landscape views of Palestine, Syria, and Jerusalem during the 19th century.

label: Jamaican Flora Chromogenic Photography
description: A series of chromogenic color prints depicting botanical subjects from Jamaica, including leaves, flowers, trees, and plants, created by an American photographer and held in the Getty Museum's photography department.

label: American Drawings and Sketches
description: A collection of drawings from American museums (primarily Cleveland Museum of Art, with items from Met and Harvard) spanning various media including graphite, watercolor, and ink, depicting landscapes, nature studies, and architectural scenes from American and European locations.

label: Piranesi's Vedute di Roma
description: These are architectural prints depicting Roman monuments, basilicas, temples, and archaeological sites, primarily etched works by or in the style of Giovanni Battista Piranesi documenting Rome's classical and Renaissance architecture.

label: Medieval and Renaissance Religious Imagery
description: These objects are European artworks spanning the 13th-20th centuries that depict religious subjects—particularly saints, biblical narratives, and heraldic imagery—created across multiple mediums including stained glass, manuscript illumination, sculpture, and painted panels.

label: Diné Navajo Silver Jewelry
