description: These objects are silver and silverwork adornments created by the Diné (Navajo) people, often featuring traditional designs like necklaces, squash-blossom necklaces, and shoulder ornaments, frequently incorporating turquoise and other stones.

label: Spanish architectural travel sketches
description: A series of drawings and watercolors depicting iconic Spanish landmarks and streets from major cities like Toledo, Madrid, and Segovia, created by an American artist using mixed media techniques including litho crayon and charcoal.

label: 19th Century European Architectural Prints
description: A collection of lithographic prints and portfolio works documenting picturesque medieval and historic architecture in France, Belgium, and England, created in the 19th century by English artists and publishers.

label: Chinese Cricket-keeping Equipment
description: These objects are specialized tools and containers used in the traditional Asian practice of cricket-keeping and cricket fighting, primarily from China.

label: Ancient Greek Bronze Objects
description: These objects are all bronze artifacts from ancient Greece, primarily associated with Thessalian culture, encompassing decorative arts, costume accessories, and functional items from the Greek and Roman periods.

label: Medieval Eastern Mediterranean Textile Fragments
description: These are silk and linen textile fragments from Egypt and Syria, primarily from the medieval period, featuring decorative weaving techniques such as compound twill and tapestry weave.

label: Maximilian I Imperial Propaganda Prints
description: A collection of prints from German and Northern European institutions depicting Emperor Maximilian I through portraits, heraldry, military campaigns, and triumphal imagery, representing the first systematic use of prints for imperial self-promotion.

label: English Cotton Furnishing Fabrics
description: These objects are printed cotton textiles produced in England during the 18th-19th centuries using various printing techniques including copperplate, roller, and block printing methods.

label: Twelve Months Calendar Series
description: These are prints depicting the months of the year from Northern European (Dutch/Flemish) series, created in the 17th century using various printmaking techniques including etching and engraving.

label: Historical Storage Furniture Across Cultures
description: These objects are chests and chests of drawers from various time periods and cultural traditions spanning medieval Europe, colonial Americas, and early American regions, representing essential storage furniture that reflects regional woodworking styles and materials.

label: 19th Century American Railroad Survey Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver prints documenting railroad infrastructure, routes, and associated landscapes across Kansas and Missouri during the American westward expansion era.

label: Early 20th Century Gelatin Silver Prints
description: A collection of gelatin silver print photographs on baryta paper from the Rijksmuseum, primarily by Dutch photographer Jan van der Mey, documenting travels, leisure activities, and family portraits across Europe and North America circa 1900-1920s.

label: English Prints, 19th-20th Century
description: A collection of British prints from the late 19th and early 20th centuries, primarily depicting character types, animals, and cultural scenes through various printmaking techniques including lithography and woodcut.

label: Modernist Flower Photography Prints
description: A collection of American photographic prints from the early-to-mid 20th century featuring close-up studies of individual flowers and floral arrangements, primarily created using gelatin silver printing techniques.

label: German Drypoint Prints on Paper
description: A collection of German drypoint prints on paper from the Rijksmuseum, featuring religious subjects, allegorical figures, and genre scenes, many attributed to the same Early Modern German master.

label: French Photographs at Palais Gallia
description: All objects are photographs from French cultural heritage held in the Palais Gallia department of the Paris Museums collection.

label: Decorated Wrapper Designs with Gold
description: These are relief-printed paper wrappers from the Rijksmuseum featuring decorative compositions of flowers, fruits, and botanical motifs enhanced with gold leaf, likely used for luxury packaging or commercial purposes.

label: Colonial Pacific Stereograph Views
description: These albumen silver print stereographs document scenic and cultural landscapes of New Zealand and the Pacific Islands during the colonial era, primarily from the late 19th century.

label: Attic Black-Figure Column Krater
description: These fragments belong to ancient Greek terracotta vessels decorated with black-figure technique, primarily parts of a single or related column krater assemblage from Classical Athens.

label: Farallon Islands Stereograph Series
description: A collection of albumen silver stereographs documenting the natural features, wildlife, and human activity on California's Farallon Islands during the 19th century.

label: Renaissance Depictions of Caesar's Triumph
description: These objects are prints, drawings, and watercolors from Italian Renaissance art depicting Julius Caesar's triumphal procession, created across multiple museums using techniques like woodcut, engraving, and ink on paper.

label: European Bronze Sculptures
description: A collection of predominantly bronze sculptures from European museums (primarily France and Italy) spanning various subjects including allegorical figures, historical portraits, and classical-inspired works held in the Department of European Art.

label: Dance of Death allegorical prints
description: European prints from the 16th-20th centuries depicting the medieval allegorical theme of the Danse Macabre, where Death confronts figures from all social classes in a memento mori tradition.

label: Bohemian Glass Beakers
description: These objects are decorative drinking vessels from Bohemia, crafted from glass using various techniques including engraving, gilding, enameling, and cutting, and representing the region's renowned glass-making tradition.

label: Hand-colored botanical stipple engravings
description: These French prints from the 18th-19th centuries depict flowers, fruits, and plants rendered through stipple engraving techniques with hand-coloring or color printing, drawn from illustrated botanical and horticultural treatises.

label: Chinese Ink Sticks and Cakes
description: These objects are writing materials from Ming and Qing Dynasty China, consisting of molded ink cakes and sticks used in traditional calligraphy and painting, often decorated with decorative reliefs, auspicious motifs, and inscriptions.

label: Miniature Room Period Settings
description: These are detailed scale models of historical domestic and architectural interiors from various periods and regions, created as educational or decorative representations of historical design and furnishings.

label: French Popular Woodcut Calendars
description: These are French woodblock-printed calendars and almanacs from the 18th-19th centuries, many featuring contemporary historical events, political figures, and popular legends as decorative elements alongside calendrical information.

label: Four Seasons Print Series
description: These objects are prints depicting the four seasons, predominantly created through engraving and etching techniques in Northern Europe (Flanders, Netherlands, Germany, France, Italy, and England) during the 16th-19th centuries.

label: Franco-British Relations Commemorative Medals
description: These are French numismatic medals commemorating significant British events and figures, particularly the 1851 Great Exhibition at Crystal Palace and subsequent Anglo-French diplomatic exchanges during the Victorian era.

label: Manchester Ship Canal Documentation
description: These gelatin silver prints by G. Herbert and collaborators systematically document the construction, infrastructure, and features of the Manchester Ship Canal and surrounding waterways in northwest England.

label: Niagara Suspension Bridge Stereographs
description: A collection of stereograph photographs and prints documenting the historic Niagara Suspension Bridge from multiple perspectives and angles, captured primarily through 19th-century photographic processes like albumen silver prints and photolithographs.

label: 19th Century Holy Land Photography
description: A collection of French albumen print photographs documenting architectural and religious sites across the Middle East, particularly Jerusalem, Palestine, and surrounding regions, created during the era of European photographic exploration of the Orient.

label: Yvette Guilbert Artistic Portrayals
description: These objects are French prints and drawings from the 19th century depicting the celebrated cabaret performer Yvette Guilbert, created primarily through lithographic techniques.

label: 19th Century New York Street Photography
description: These objects are stereographs and early photographs documenting New York City streets and landmarks from the mid-to-late 1800s, primarily featuring Broadway and surrounding areas captured through albumen silver print technology.

label: Viennese Aristocratic Portraits
description: A series of etched and engraved portrait prints depicting 18th-century Viennese nobility, intellectuals, and prominent figures, created on paper as part of Vienna's cultural printmaking tradition.

label: Museum Collection Fragments
description: These objects represent incomplete or fragmented artifacts from distant cultures and time periods held across major museum institutions.

label: Paleolithic Hand Axes Collection
description: A collection of bifaces (hand axes) from the Lower and Middle Paleolithic periods, primarily made of flint or quartzite, all preserved in the Musée de Bretagne in Rennes.

label: Natural History Stereographs
description: Hand-colored albumen stereograph cards depicting North American wildlife organized by Linnaean taxonomic classification, likely from a nineteenth-century educational series.

label: Etruscan Cornelian Scarab Seals
description: These objects are decorative and functional scarab seals from Etruscan culture, carved from cornelian and black jasper stone, and housed in the Getty's jewelry collection.

label: 19th Century Orientalist Travel Photography
description: A collection of albumen print photographs documenting Egyptian and Ottoman monuments, temples, and urban landscapes from a mid-19th century expeditionary perspective, representing early European photographic exploration of North Africa and the Middle East.

label: French satirical lithograph series
description: A collection of numbered French lithographic prints from Paris museums featuring humorous dialogue and social commentary, likely from a serialized satirical publication of the 19th century.

label: Ancient and Medieval Glass Objects
description: A collection of glass vessels and artifacts spanning from Roman to 18th-century periods, primarily from the Musée d'Art et d'Archéologie du Pays de Laon, created through blown and molded glass techniques.

label: Céramique et poterie historique
description: Ces objets sont des artefacts en céramique et argile datant principalement de l'époque romaine et de périodes historiques ultérieures, conservés dans des musées français et représentant les formes usuelles de la vaisselle domestique (pots, assiettes, plats, coupes).

label: Rural Jura and Doubs regional architecture
description: These are early 20th-century graphite and mixed media drawings documenting traditional vernacular buildings, farms, and village scenes from the Jura and Doubs regions of eastern France, created as architectural or ethnographic records.

label: Historic New England Trees
description: A series of photogravure prints documenting notable trees throughout Massachusetts, each identified by their common name, owner or location, representing a systematic documentation of culturally and historically significant trees in 19th-century New England.

label: Austrian Lithographic Prints Collection
description: A cohesive collection of 19th-century lithographs depicting Austrian and Bavarian landscapes, architectural landmarks, and notable figures, created using lithographic printing techniques and housed at the Cleveland Museum of Art.

label: Chinese Ink Calligraphy on Paper
description: These objects are all Chinese calligraphic works created with ink on paper or silk using various traditional script styles, held in the Asian Art department of a museum.

label: Puck Magazine Political Satire
description: Color lithograph prints from the American satirical magazine Puck, featuring political commentary and social criticism through illustrated humor and caricature.

label: Gelatin Silver Prints from Early 20th Century
description: A collection of gelatin silver print photographs from the Getty Museum's photography department, primarily featuring Spanish and Mexican cultural subjects with narrative and allegorical themes.

label: Pre-Columbian Animal Pendants
description: These objects are decorative pendants from Central America and northern South America (Panama, Costa Rica, Colombia) dating from the 5th-16th centuries, predominantly made from cast gold and featuring zoomorphic or hybrid animal-human designs reflecting indigenous spiritual and social symbolism.

label: Commemorative medals of notable figures
description: A collection of French numismatic medals from the 19th century honoring military generals, politicians, and historical figures, primarily created to commemorate their deaths or contributions to French history.

label: English Staffordshire Pottery Teapots
description: This collection comprises primarily 18th-century ceramic teapots from the Staffordshire pottery tradition, featuring diverse techniques including salt-glazed stoneware, creamware, agateware, and redware with various decorative methods.

label: Civil War Military Engineering Documentation
description: These salted paper prints document Union Army engineering experiments and construction projects for military railroads and bridges during the American Civil War, primarily focused on truss bridge design, track destruction, and infrastructure at Alexandria, Virginia and Bull Run.

label: Early Modern Artist Self-Portraits
description: A collection of printed self-portraits by Dutch and Flemish artists from the 16th-19th centuries, primarily created through engraving and etching techniques on paper.

label: Early Chinese Buddhist Sculpture
description: These objects are primarily stone and bronze sculptures of Buddhist figures (Buddhas, Bodhisattvas, and votive stelae) created in China and Central Asia from the Northern Dynasties through Tang Dynasty periods, representing the development of Buddhist artistic traditions in East Asia.

label: English Literary Illustration Studies
description: These objects are preparatory drawings and prints for illustrated editions of English literary works, created for pocket books and engravings during the late 18th and early 19th centuries.

label: German Baroque Wooden Gaming Pieces
description: A collection of wooden gaming pieces from late 17th to early 18th century Germany, each with obverse and reverse sides, likely used in period board games or gambling activities.

label: French Glass Paperweights
description: A collection of decorative glass paperweights from major 19th-century French glassmaking centers including Baccarat, Saint-Louis-lès-Bitche, Clichy, and Lunéville, all characterized by colorful glass construction and held in European applied arts collections.

label: Nineteenth-century Strasbourg architectural views
description: This collection consists of drawings and sketches documenting the buildings, streets, monuments, and surroundings of Strasbourg, France during the 18th-19th centuries, primarily executed in ink, pencil, and watercolor.

label: Reynard the Fox narrative series
description: A complete Dutch etching series illustrating episodes from the medieval animal fable of Reynard the Fox, depicting the cunning fox's various tricks and encounters with other animals in the lion's court.

label: Miao Silver Ear Ornaments
description: A collection of silver earrings and ear plugs from the Miao culture of China, representing traditional metal jewelry-making practices and adornment customs within Asian ethnic communities.

label: Bound printed books French collection
description: These objects are all bound printed books and incunabula from French cultural institutions, predominantly featuring leather bindings (maroquin) with gilding, representing diverse subjects from religious texts to architectural documentation.

label: 19th-century French graphic satire series
description: These are plates from multiple lithographic and graphic art series depicting Parisian social life and leisure activities, primarily preserved in the Musée Carnavalet's Paris history collection.

label: Illuminated Manuscript Initial Letters
description: These are decorated folio pages from German and Austrian medieval manuscripts featuring ornamental capital letters embellished with gold leaf, tempera, and ink, often incorporating biblical or historical figures within the letter forms.

label: 19th Century British India Photography
description: A collection of albumen print photographs documenting landscapes, architecture, and people across British colonial India, created primarily using wet collodion negatives and associated with English photographic practices of the era.

label: Bacchic and Silenus Imagery
description: These objects depict scenes from classical mythology centered on Bacchus, Silenus, satyrs, and maenads in bacchanalian revelry, particularly emphasizing wine production and drunken celebration across European art from the 15th-17th centuries.

label: 19th-Century San Francisco Urban Views
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs and card photographs documenting San Francisco and surrounding California cities during the 19th century, capturing street scenes, landmarks, and urban development from specific vantage points.

label: Caspar Merian's Etched Character Studies
description: A collection of etched and letterpress prints by Caspar Merian depicting diverse historical, biblical, and allegorical figures engaged in various human activities and moral scenarios.

label: Nineteenth-Century Paris Chalk Lithograph Portraits
description: A collection of chalk lithograph prints on paper from Paris culture depicting prominent 19th-century French and international figures including politicians, military leaders, writers, and artists.

label: Antwerp Etched Portraits
description: A collection of portrait prints created using etching and related intaglio techniques on paper, associated with the Antwerp artistic tradition and depicting notable historical figures from the 16th-17th centuries.

label: Dutch Golden Age Figure Prints
description: A collection of prints from the Rijksmuseum primarily depicting figures from Dutch and Amsterdam culture, created using printmaking techniques such as etching and engraving on paper during the 17th-18th centuries.

label: Thames Riverscape Prints by Whistler
description: These objects are prints depicting scenes of Chelsea and the Thames River in London, primarily created by James McNeill Whistler using etching and lithography techniques, and held across major international museum collections.

label: Indian Mughal and Rajput Court Paintings
description: These objects are paintings from Indian courts (primarily Mughal, Rajput, and regional schools) created between the 16th-19th centuries, depicting courtly scenes, royal hunts, religious narratives, and aristocratic life, executed in traditional media such as opaque watercolor, gum tempera, and gold on paper.

label: Italian Combat Instruction Manuscript
description: These objects are illuminated folios from an Italian martial arts or fencing manual, created with tempera, gold leaf, and silver leaf, depicting various combat techniques with different weapons and fighting styles.

label: Ancient Scaraboid Gems
description: These objects are engraved and cast scaraboid gems from Greek and Greco-Persian cultures, made from various semi-precious stones and glass and housed in the Getty Museum's jewelry department.

label: Early Modern European Wheellocks
description: A collection of ornately decorated wheellock firearms manufactured across German-speaking territories and neighboring regions from the 16th-18th centuries, representing the technological transition from matchlock to wheellock ignition systems.

label: Six Jewel Rivers Japanese prints
description: These are Japanese woodblock prints depicting the Six Jewel Rivers (Mu Tamagawa) theme across various provinces and artistic series from the Edo period.

label: Western Railroad Survey Photography
description: These albumen print photographs document the landscapes, geological formations, and infrastructure of the transcontinental railroad route through the American West, primarily from the 1860s-1870s survey expeditions.

label: Functional Metal Objects and Tools
description: These museum objects are primarily metalwork items spanning various cultures and time periods, including utensils (forks, knives, scissors), personal accessories (rings, rattles), and practical tools (probe, shears, tape measure), all crafted from metals such as silver, tin, steel, and gold.

label: Diepenbrock Family Photographs
description: A collection of portraits documenting the Dutch Diepenbrock family, primarily composer Alphons Diepenbrock with his wife Elsa and daughters Joanna and Thea, taken in early 20th-century Netherlands and France.

label: Stereoview photographs of waterfalls
description: These albumen print stereographs document natural waterfalls and cascades, primarily from the Ithaca, New York region, created for 19th-century stereoview viewing devices that produced a 3D effect.

label: Stereograph Views of Panama
description: A collection of 19th-century stereographic cards documenting Panama's architecture, landscapes, and daily life, created for American audiences through albumen silver print technology.

label: 19th-century Egyptian temple stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting ancient Egyptian temples and monuments, primarily produced by English photographers during the mid-to-late 1800s as popular three-dimensional travel views.

label: Classical Female Figures in Art
description: These objects depict legendary ancient women—primarily Lucretia and Cleopatra—who were subjects of repeated artistic interpretation across European museums from the Renaissance through early modern periods, rendered in diverse media including prints, paintings, sculpture, and decorative arts.

label: Early Photography of Yosemite Valley
description: These objects are albumen silver print stereographs and photographs documenting Yosemite Valley's iconic landmarks, particularly the Three Brothers rock formation, created during the mid-19th century American landscape photography movement.

label: 20th Century American Sculptural Sketches
description: These drawings are preliminary sketches and studies by an American artist exploring sculptural and memorial design concepts through various drawing media during the 20th century.

label: Figure Studies and Portrait Drawings
description: A collection of chalk and pencil drawings on paper depicting human figures and heads in various poses and contexts, primarily from European collections spanning the 18th-19th centuries.

label: Théodore Géricault's Study Drawings
description: These objects are preparatory sketches and studies by French Romantic painter Théodore Géricault, featuring repeated motifs of horses, lions, and human figures in various poses that likely informed his major compositions.

label: Asian decorative buckles and fasteners
description: A collection of ornamental buckles from East and South Asian cultures, primarily Chinese, crafted from jade, brass, and precious materials for functional and decorative purposes.

label: Diné Silver and Turquoise Adornments
description: These objects represent traditional Diné (Navajo) jewelry and adornments crafted from silver and turquoise, including buttons and ketoh (wrist guards), reflecting the distinctive metalworking and stone-setting techniques of Southwestern Native American artisans.

label: Nineteenth-Century Parisian Vehicle Types
description: A comprehensive series of French lithographic prints documenting diverse horse-drawn carriages and vehicles that populated Paris during the 19th century, ranging from public transport to commercial and ceremonial conveyances.

label: 19th Century Parisian Public Venues
description: A collection of etchings and photographs documenting Parisian theaters, concert halls, public baths, and entertainment establishments from the 19th century, primarily housed in the Musée Carnavalet.

label: Early Photographic Portrait Processes
description: These objects are early portrait photographs using nineteenth-century photographic techniques including daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, and tintypes, primarily held in museum photography collections.

label: Qing Dynasty Court Robes
description: These objects are silk garments worn by members of the Manchu imperial court during China's Qing Dynasty, including semiformal robes (jifu), informal robes, and vests that denoted rank and ceremonial occasion.

label: Parable of the Prodigal Son
description: These objects are prints, drawings, and designs from the Low Countries and France depicting various scenes from the biblical parable of the Prodigal Son, created primarily through engraving and etching techniques.

label: Papal Medals 19th Century
description: This collection consists of commemorative medals from the Musée Carnavalet celebrating 19th-century popes, particularly Pope Pius IX, documenting significant ecclesiastical and political events from 1823-1869.

label: Qing Dynasty Embroidered Silk Garments
description: These objects are predominantly Chinese garments from the Qing Dynasty (1644-1911) made from silk with decorative embroidery and metallic threads, representing formal and ceremonial dress traditions.

label: Eighteenth-Century European Tureens
description: A collection of decorative ceramic tureens and tureen components from major European porcelain and faience manufacturing centers (Sèvres, Vienna, Chelsea, Strasbourg, Vincennes, Marseilles) from the 18th century, many featuring whimsical zoomorphic forms such as animals and birds.

label: French Revolutionary Political Figures
description: Commemorative medals and sculptures honoring key French Revolutionary leaders including Jean-Sylvain Bailly (first Mayor of Paris), Jean-Paul Marat, and Jérôme Pétion from the late 18th century.

label: Alpine stereographs of Swiss landscapes
description: These are nineteenth-century albumen silver print stereographs depicting Swiss and Alpine scenery, produced for British audiences using a photographic format popular for Victorian-era tourism and travel documentation.

label: Engravings of Cupid and Psyche
description: A series of Italian Renaissance engravings depicting scenes from the classical myth of Cupid and Psyche, primarily created as narrative print cycles on cream laid paper.

label: Ancient Greek Ceramic Vessel Fragments
description: These objects are all fragmentary pieces of Attic red-figure neck amphorae, a distinctive form of ancient Greek pottery decorated with red figures on a black background, held at the Getty Museum.

label: Second Anglo-Afghan War Photography
description: These albumen print photographs document the Second Anglo-Afghan War (1878-1880), featuring landscapes, fortifications, military personnel, and key figures from the conflict in Afghanistan including locations like Jellalabad, Khyber, and meetings with Amir Yakub Khan.

label: European Crossbow Weapons and Ammunition
description: This collection comprises crossbows and their bolts from medieval and early modern Europe (primarily Germany and France), representing both hunting/sporting and military weaponry made with wood, steel, and natural materials like horn and bone.

label: French etching landscape series
description: These are original French etchings from the Dutuit collection featuring diverse landscape scenes including pastoral settings, classical ruins, waterfalls, and rural buildings, all executed in the etching technique.

label: French Farm Animal Studies
description: A collection of French etching studies depicting domestic livestock and pastoral scenes, primarily featuring sheep, goats, cattle, and related rural subjects from the 16th-17th century.

label: Japanese lacquerware decorative objects
description: These objects are predominantly Japanese lacquered items from the Asian Art department, featuring various techniques including black and red lacquer, gold inlay (maki-e), mother-of-pearl inlay, and traditional Negoro ware, serving functional and decorative purposes.

label: Hercules Segers' Experimental Landscape Prints
description: These objects are innovative landscape etchings and prints by Dutch Golden Age master Hercules Segers, known for his experimental use of unconventional materials, hand coloring, and advanced intaglio techniques to create atmospheric natural scenes.

label: Ancient Bracelets and Arm Ornaments
description: These objects are personal adornments worn on the arms and wrists from ancient Mediterranean and Near Eastern cultures, spanning Egyptian, Greek, Roman, Bactrian, and Cypriot civilizations, made from precious metals, glass, ivory, and bronze.

label: Ausable Chasm stereograph series
description: A comprehensive collection of 19th-century stereograph and albumen print photographs documenting the scenic geological formations and tourist attractions of Ausable Chasm in upstate New York.

label: English Photography of Sri Lanka
description: This collection comprises English photographic prints documenting Sri Lankan Buddhist archaeological sites and landscapes, primarily created using woodburygravure and photogravure techniques in the 19th century.

label: Glazed Ceramic Decorative Tiles
description: These objects are all ceramic tiles made with glazed or tin-glazed earthenware techniques, originating from various European and American traditions including Dutch, Spanish, and early 20th-century American tile-making.

label: 19th Century Egypt Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver prints documenting Egyptian temples, monuments, and daily life from the 19th century, primarily produced by British photographers.

label: 17th Century French War Prints
description: These are French etched prints from the 17th century depicting scenes of military conflict, warfare, and its brutal consequences, primarily from Jacques Callot's series 'The Miseries of War' and 'The Military Exercises.'

label: Renaissance Prints of Sibyls and Prophets
description: A collection of Italian Renaissance engravings and woodcuts depicting sibyls (classical prophetesses) and biblical prophets, primarily from the 15th-17th centuries, representing a popular subject matter that bridged classical and Christian iconography.

label: French Faust Illustrations
description: These are 19th-century French lithographic prints illustrating scenes from Goethe's Faust, depicting key characters and dramatic moments from the literary masterpiece.

label: 19th Century French Printmaking
description: These objects are primarily prints created in 19th-century France using traditional intaglio techniques such as etching, aquatint, drypoint, and roulette, depicting landscapes, urban scenes, and daily life.

label: Last Supper Artistic Representations
description: These objects are artistic depictions of the Last Supper and related biblical meal scenes from European museums, created across multiple centuries using various printmaking and drawing techniques.

label: Italian Chiaroscuro Woodcut Prints
description: This collection consists of paper prints created using the chiaroscuro woodcut technique, predominantly from Italian artistic traditions, depicting religious subjects including saints, apostles, biblical scenes, and allegorical virtues.

label: Germanic Romantic Landscape Drawings
description: These works are primarily German and Austrian drawings and watercolors from the Romantic era featuring landscape subjects, architectural ruins, and human figures rendered with pen, ink, and watercolor techniques.

label: Japanese Photography Album Collection
description: A cohesive series of gelatin silver prints (many hand-tinted) from a single photography album documenting Japanese culture, people, landscapes, and daily life, held at the Museum of International Art.

label: Magdalenian engraved stone plaquettes
description: These are fragmented stone plaquettes from the Magdalenian period (Upper Paleolithic), primarily made of schist or sandstone and decorated with engraved or incised markings, representing portable art objects from prehistoric France.

label: Facsimiles of Sketches: Flanders and Germany
description: These prints are all individual plates from a single 19th-century lithographic publication documenting architectural landmarks and urban scenes across Flanders, Germany, and neighboring regions.

label: Merovingian Ceramic Vessels
description: Collection of hand-formed and turned ceramic vases from the Merovingian period (6th-7th centuries) made from various colored clays with engobes, primarily preserved at the Val-d'Oise archaeological museum.

label: Orléans Family Commemorative Medals
description: These copper medals from the Paris Museums document significant life events and honors of the House of Orléans family members during the 19th century, including births, marriages, deaths, and public ceremonies.

label: Sculptures from Lille Museum
description: All objects are sculptural works held in the collections of the Musée des Beaux-Arts in Lille, spanning multiple periods and materials from ancient to medieval times.

label: Early 20th Century Auvergne Architecture Studies
description: A collection of drawings and sketches documenting rural Auvergne buildings and farmhouses created in the second quarter of the 20th century using primarily pencil, ink, and paper media.

label: Medieval Manuscript Canon Tables
description: These objects are illuminated manuscript pages featuring canon tables—systematized reference guides organizing the four Gospels—created across Byzantine, Armenian, and French cultures using tempera, gold leaf, and ink on vellum folios.

label: Nazca Valley Ceramic Beakers
description: These objects are predominantly ceramic beakers from the Nazca Valley culture of Peru, decorated with painted abstract and figurative motifs including human heads, masks, warriors, and ritual imagery that reflect the culture's ceremonial and spiritual practices.

label: Marble and Stone Architectural Elements
description: This collection comprises sculptural and architectural fragments, including sarcophagi, guardian figures, and structural supports, primarily crafted from marble and limestone across Roman, Medieval Italian, and ancient Mediterranean cultures.

label: 19th Century French Drawings
description: A collection of landscape, figure study, and observational drawings predominantly from 19th-century French artists, executed in graphite, ink, and colored pencil on paper.

label: European Decorative Textile Borders
description: These objects are primarily decorative border textiles from France and Italy, created using needle lace and bobbin lace techniques on linen foundations, representing the refined lacework traditions of Renaissance and early modern Europe.

label: Northern Renaissance Illuminated Manuscripts
description: These are folio pages from Flemish and Dutch illuminated manuscripts featuring biblical narratives rendered in tempera, gold leaf, and gold paint, characteristic of 15th-16th century Northern European manuscript decoration.

label: Ancient Egyptian Royal Seals
description: These objects are personal ornaments and administrative seals bearing the names and titles of Egyptian pharaohs and rulers, used for identification and legitimization of authority during the New Kingdom period.

label: Cleveland Print Collection
description: A collection of prints from the Cleveland Museum of Art featuring diverse subjects—urban scenes, rural landscapes, European vistas, and cultural moments—created through various printmaking techniques including etching, lithography, and linoleum cut.

label: French Modern Figure Studies
description: A collection of French modernist artworks from Paris museums depicting the human figure through various media including drawings, paintings, sculptures, and prints from the early-to-mid 20th century.

label: East Asian literati painting subjects
description: These paintings feature traditional East Asian literati art depicting auspicious natural subjects—plum blossoms, bamboo, orchids, birds, and landscapes—created primarily in Korea and China during the Joseon Dynasty and 19th-20th centuries using ink on silk or paper.

label: Decorative Glass Vessels for Spirits
description: These objects are primarily decanters and carafes, vessels designed for storing and serving beverages, crafted from glass with decorative techniques across various periods from the Aesthetic Movement through Art Deco.

label: Bastille Storming Commemorative Objects
description: A collection of medals, insignia, prints, and commemorative objects from French museums celebrating the storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789, and subsequent anniversary celebrations of this pivotal French Revolution event.

label: Architectural Ruins in Drawing
description: A collection of drawings depicting ruins of historical structures including castles, abbeys, temples, and ancient Roman buildings from various periods and locations, rendered in various media on paper.

label: 19th Century British Sketchbook Pages
description: A collection of metalpoint drawings on prepared paper from a 19th-century British artist's sketchbook, featuring recto-verso pairings of inscriptions, architectural studies, figure sketches, and landscape drawings on individual pages.

label: Turkish Embroidered Textiles
description: These objects are decorative and functional textiles from Turkish cultural tradition, predominantly featuring embroidered embellishments on towels and bath linens made from cotton, linen, and silk materials.

label: Musée Vivant Denon Collection
description: A diverse assemblage of archaeological and historical metal artifacts—weapons, tools, and structural elements spanning from the Neolithic to the Iron Age—all preserved at the Musée Vivant Denon in Chalon-sur-Saône, France.

label: Silver Cups from European Metalwork Traditions
description: These objects are decorative and functional silver cups from European centers of production (primarily London, Boston, Edinburgh, and Nuremberg) spanning the 17th-19th centuries, often featuring two handles, covers, and sometimes exotic materials like coconut shells or ostrich eggs mounted in gilded silver.

label: Encyclopédie Technical Arts Illustrations
description: These are primarily French engraved prints from the Encyclopédie documenting various printmaking and writing techniques, featuring instructional illustrations of artistic and craft processes.

label: Dutch Golden Age River Landscapes
description: A collection of drawings depicting river scenes and landscapes from the Dutch Golden Age, created primarily on paper using ink, chalk, and watercolor by artists such as Jan van Goyen, Philips Huygens, and other 17th-century Dutch masters.

label: Nineteenth-century French architectural views
description: This collection comprises prints and drawings depicting notable buildings, landmarks, and urban scenes from various French cities including Paris, Nantes, Bourges, Brest, Angers, Dinan, Blois, Orléans, and Sens, primarily from the 19th century.

label: Nineteenth-century travel photography
description: These albumen print photographs document European expeditions to Egypt and Italy during the 1800s, capturing both architectural monuments and ethnographic subjects from colonial and orientalist perspectives.

label: Dutch Golden Age Genre Etchings
description: These prints are all etchings from the Netherlands/Holland depicting everyday scenes of peasant and village life, characteristic of 17th-century Dutch printmaking traditions.

label: Dutch Peasant Life Etchings
description: A collection of 17th-century prints depicting rural Dutch peasant scenes, primarily etchings on paper from Haarlem and Antwerp cultures, capturing everyday moments of farmer life including domestic interiors, social gatherings, and daily activities.

label: Dutch Golden Age Portrait Busts
description: A collection of etched and engraved portrait prints from the Netherlands depicting individual busts of men, philosophers, and children, predominantly created on paper using printmaking techniques characteristic of 17th-century Dutch art.

label: Turkish Embroidered Towels and Napkins
description: These are textile objects from Turkish culture, primarily towels or napkins decorated with embroidery techniques using silk, metallic threads, and various decorative motifs.

label: Guanyin Woodblock Print Series
description: These objects are individual pages from a Chinese woodblock print album depicting the fifty-three different compassionate manifestations of Guanyin, the Buddhist goddess of mercy.

label: Spanish architectural views print series
description: A comprehensive series of original etchings depicting Spanish palaces, gardens, and landmarks from the 17th century, including Madrid, Aranjuez, Granada, Toledo, Segovia, El Escorial, and Buen Retiro.

label: 19th Century Greek Archaeological Documentation
description: A series of Swiss-produced heliogravure and photolithograph prints systematically documenting ancient Greek architectural monuments and archaeological sites from the Classical period.

label: Wood engravings after Old English Masters
description: This collection comprises wood engravings on chine/china paper reproducing celebrated works by renowned English painters and depicting notable historical figures, landscapes, and literary subjects from British cultural heritage.

label: Miao Culture Textiles and Garments
description: These objects represent traditional clothing and textiles from the Miao (Hmong) culture of China, primarily consisting of hand-crafted coats and dresses made from cotton, silk, and embellished materials held in the MIA's Asian Art collection.

label: French Etchings of Farm Animals
description: A collection of original French etchings primarily depicting rural livestock scenes, horses, cattle, and pastoral imagery, predominantly created using the etching technique and cataloged in the Bartsch reference system.

label: Sonoma County Geysers Stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereograph cards documenting the geothermal features and tourist attractions of the Geysers region in Sonoma County, California, produced in the 19th century for stereoscopic viewing.

label: Belgian commemorative medals 19th century
description: A collection of medals commemorating Belgian institutions, exhibitions, officials, and historical events from the mid-to-late 1800s, primarily centered on Brussels and Schaerbeek.

label: 19th Century English Sinai Expeditions
description: These albumen silver prints document English photographic expeditions to Mount Sinai and the Sinai Peninsula, capturing sacred and archaeological sites including religious monuments, inscriptions, and desert landscapes.

label: Architectural Etchings by Scottish Artist
description: Collection of etched and drypoint prints depicting architectural landmarks, landscapes, and historical buildings from Scotland, England, France, Italy, and the Netherlands, created by a Scottish printmaker and held in European art departments.

label: French Islamic design studies
description: Collection of graphic art drawings and studies of Islamic and Moorish ornamental patterns, motifs, and decorative arts created by French artists using graphite pencil and ink on paper.

label: China and Its People Album
description: These are collotype photographs from a 19th-century Scottish-produced documentary album titled 'Illustrations of China and Its People,' capturing Chinese landscapes, architecture, people, occupations, and daily life across multiple regions including Canton, Hong Kong, Amoy, and Foochow.

label: Ancient Bronze Coins
description: These objects are predominantly bronze coins from Roman civilization, curated in museum coin departments and representing various periods and denominations of ancient monetary exchange.

label: Paleolithic Flaked Stone Tools
description: These objects are all debitage flakes and related tool forms created through the intentional knapping of silex and quartzite during the Paleolithic periods, primarily held in Breton museum collections.

label: Ancient Greek Deity Coins
description: These objects are ancient Greek bronze coins primarily depicting the god Apollo, with one representing Asklepios, unified by their origin in Greek culture and their function as monetary objects bearing divine iconography.

label: French Political Satire Lithographs
description: A series of 19th-century French lithographic prints using caricature and allegory to satirize Russian imperial politics, particularly Tsar Nicolas I's foreign policy and territorial ambitions.

label: World War I Industrial Production
description: These lithographic prints document British and English wartime manufacturing and industrial efforts during World War I, depicting factories, shipyards, and munitions production.

label: Japanese Twelve Months Series Prints
description: These are Japanese woodblock prints organized into series depicting the twelve months of the year, often showcasing seasonal customs, activities, and cultural practices.

label: French Glass Decorative Objects
description: Collection of French glass vessels and decorative items from Paris museums, primarily comprising cups, bowls, and vases crafted using glass and paste-glass techniques.

label: Jacques Callot's Beggars Series
description: A collection of original etchings from Jacques Callot's two famous series depicting marginalized figures: 'Les Gueux' (The Beggars), a suite of 25 prints showing various impoverished and disabled beggars, and 'Les Bohémiens' (The Gypsies), a suite of 4 prints portraying nomadic life, created in the early 17th century.

label: Decorative Writing Desk Accessories
description: These objects are functional yet ornamental inkstands, inkwells, and related desk accessories produced across Europe from the 18th-20th centuries, crafted from luxurious materials including porcelain, metals, glass, and marble to serve both practical and aesthetic purposes in scholarly or aristocratic settings.

label: Natural History Study Drawings
description: A collection of detailed naturalistic drawings of animals, insects, and marine creatures from Low Countries culture, primarily rendered on paper using ink and brush techniques.

label: Original Views of London series
description: A 19th-century lithographic series documenting iconic London landmarks and architectural views, issued as both individual hand-colored prints and bound volumes.

label: American South Photographic Survey
description: These prints document American Southern cities and locations, primarily Memphis, Mississippi, and New Orleans, created through various photographic processes and held in major museum collections.

label: Academic Studies of Classical Antiquity
description: These drawings document systematic studies of ancient Roman and Greek sculptures and bas-reliefs, created by an English artist as part of classical art training and reference material.

label: La fin de Satan Print Series
description: A comprehensive collection of etching plates with aquatint and roulette techniques from Victor Hugo's illustrated work, representing a significant French Romantic artistic project.

label: European Tableware Glass Sets
description: These objects are functional drinking and serving glasses from matched tableware sets, primarily French and English cut glass from the European art collection, designed for different beverages and occasions.

label: Single Album Collection Leaves
description: These objects are individual leaves from a unified Southern Song Dynasty album depicting Daoist and Buddhist religious themes, including deities, underworld rulers, and narrative scenes.

label: Japanese Ceremonial Gift Covers
description: These objects are fukusa, traditional Japanese textile wrappers used to present gifts at formal ceremonies, made from luxurious materials like silk and gold-leaf decorated paper with various decorative techniques including embroidery and weaving.

label: Roman Silver Denarii with Deities
description: A collection of silver denarius coins from the Roman Empire and Italian regions featuring depictions of Roman gods and goddesses, used as currency and propaganda tools to promote religious and political ideals.

label: Merovingian fibulae brooches
description: A collection of decorative metal brooches from the Merovingian period (5th-8th centuries) made with precious metals, alloys, and gemstones, primarily housed in French museums.

label: Victorian Street Life Documentation
description: A series of woodburytype photographs from the 1880s documenting London's working-class street vendors, laborers, and marginalized populations, primarily from John Thomson's 'Street Life in London' album and 'Street Incidents' book.

label: Parisian municipal tokens (15th-18th centuries)
description: These objects are French numismatic tokens from Paris spanning from the 15th to early 18th century, primarily representing municipal series cast in various metals including copper, brass, tin, and silver.

label: Depression-era American vernacular architecture
description: These gelatin silver print photographs document everyday buildings, streetscapes, and working-class structures across the American South and industrial Northeast during the Great Depression era, capturing the material culture and built environment of ordinary communities.

label: Turkish Embroidered Textile Covers
description: A collection of decorative textile covers and handkerchiefs from Turkish cultural heritage, primarily made through embroidery techniques on linen or silk bases with ornamental patterns including floral, geometric, and traditional Ottoman motifs.

label: Myanmar textile garments and clothing
description: These objects are traditional woven garments and clothing items from various ethnic cultures of Myanmar (Burma), primarily skirts, longyi, and ceremonial pieces made from natural fibers like cotton and silk.

label: French Academic Figure Drawing Studies
description: A collection of French graphic arts drawings primarily featuring figure studies of women, men, and family groups executed in charcoal, black stone, and graphite with white highlights, representing academic artistic practice and preparatory studies for larger compositions.

label: Brussels and Flemish Tapestries
description: A collection of narrative and allegorical wool and silk tapestries produced in Brussels and Flanders during the Renaissance, featuring historical, religious, and mythological scenes executed in slit and dovetailed weaving techniques.

label: Prairie School Movement Architecture
description: These objects represent architectural elements and designs from the Prairie School movement, characterized by leaded glass windows, art glass panels, and decorative features from early 20th-century American residential and commercial buildings, particularly associated with the Midwest.

label: European Woodworking and Measuring Tools
description: These objects are hand tools and instruments used for carpentry, metalworking, and precision measurement, primarily from Europe spanning the 15th-18th centuries, representing the craft traditions of skilled artisans.

label: French Revolutionary Administrative Insignias
description: These objects are official badges and medals worn by French administrators, officials, and public servants during the Revolutionary and Napoleonic periods (1790-1814) to denote their governmental functions.

label: French Medical Photography Collection
description: These objects are albumen silver prints from 19th-century France documenting medical conditions, clinical cases, and patient portraits, likely compiled for medical education or research purposes.

label: Stereograph Views of East Asia
description: These gelatin silver print stereographs document daily life, landscapes, and cultural scenes across East and Southeast Asia, created for American audiences during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

label: English Natural History Prints
description: These objects are primarily English woodcuts and wood engravings depicting animals and birds, many from illustrated natural history books and publications.

label: Colonial-era Indian Architecture Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver print photographs documenting major architectural monuments and structures in Delhi and Lahore during the British colonial period.

label: Classical Mythology in European Art
description: These objects depict scenes from Greek and Roman mythology across multiple European institutions, featuring gods and goddesses such as Venus, Diana, Aurora, and Pan rendered primarily in oil paintings and drawings from the 15th-19th centuries.

label: Dutch Golden Age Study Sheets
description: These are all preparatory study drawings on paper from the Rijksmuseum collection, featuring sketches of human figures, animals, and anatomical details created by Dutch and European artists primarily from the 16th-19th centuries.

label: Derrer Family Genealogical Records
description: These German folios document successive generations of the Derrer family through illuminated manuscript portraits, created using tempera with gold and silver highlights to preserve their ancestral lineage.

label: Liber Studiorum Softground Etchings
description: These are numbered plates from J.M.W. Turner's Liber Studiorum, a 19th-century English publication of softground etchings depicting landscapes, castles, and studies from England and Wales.

label: Ancient Coinage Across Cultures
description: These objects are coins and coin-like artifacts spanning multiple ancient and medieval civilizations, predominantly from Hellenistic Greek cultures, representing the widespread use of coined currency as a medium of economic exchange and cultural expression.

label: Belgian Lace Edgings and Panels
description: A collection of decorative lace textiles produced in Belgium, primarily from Brussels and Mechelen, using traditional techniques such as bobbin lace and needle lace from the 18th century onward.

label: Magdalénian Bone and Antler Points
description: These are Upper Paleolithic hunting weapon points from the Magdalénian period (roughly 17,000-12,000 years ago) made from reindeer antler and bone, decorated with incision and engraving techniques, housed in French archaeological museums.

label: Japanese Edo Period Tobacco Pipes
description: A collection of smoking pipes from Japan's Edo and Meiji periods, crafted from natural materials like bamboo and wood combined with metals such as silver, brass, and gold, reflecting the widespread use and artistic refinement of tobacco pipes in Japanese culture.

label: Picturesque Views of British Castles
description: A collection of 18th-century etching and aquatint prints depicting castles and landscapes across Wales and England, organized into thematic series documenting British architectural heritage and scenic views.

label: Traditional Bhutanese Textiles
description: These objects represent the diverse range of traditional Bhutanese woven garments and textiles, including wrapped skirts (kira), tunics, shawls, and ceremonial cloths made from silk, cotton, and wool using traditional weaving techniques.

label: Franco-Prussian War commemorative medals
description: These medals commemorate key figures and events from the Franco-Prussian War (1870-1871), particularly celebrating Giuseppe Garibaldi's military support for France and Henri Rochefort's political role during the conflict.

label: Flemish Medieval Historical Manuscripts
description: These illuminated manuscript folios from Flemish culture depict 14th-century political and military events involving European nobility, created using tempera, gold leaf, and ink.

label: East Asian Ink Painting and Calligraphy
description: These objects are primarily Chinese and Japanese landscape paintings and calligraphy works created using ink on paper or silk in hanging scroll format, emphasizing nature themes and poetic sensibility characteristic of literati art traditions.

label: Belgian Prints by Félicien Rops
description: This collection comprises printmaking works from Belgian artist Félicien Rops, utilizing various intaglio and etching techniques including heliogravure, drypoint, and soft varnish etching to create figurative and allegorical compositions.

label: American Civil War Military Portraits
description: These card photographs document Union and Confederate military officers and soldiers from the American Civil War era, primarily created as albumen silver prints for identification and commemorative purposes.

label: Guanyin/Avalokiteshvara Representations
description: These objects are representations of Guanyin (known as Avalokiteshvara in Sanskrit), a central bodhisattva figure in Chinese Buddhism, created across multiple dynasties and in various media including sculpture, ceramics, painting, and precious materials.

label: Hindu Deity Shiva Iconography
description: These objects are sculptures and architectural elements depicting the Hindu god Shiva and related deities from Indian and South Asian cultures across multiple centuries and regions.

label: Chinese Rubbing Prints
description: These objects are wrijfprenten (rubbing prints), a traditional Chinese printmaking technique created by rubbing paper and ink over textured surfaces to transfer impressions.

label: Akan Brass Goldweights
description: These are miniature brass weights from Akan culture used to measure gold dust in trade, featuring diverse forms including animals, tools, weapons, figures, and household objects that reflect daily life and social hierarchy.

label: Le Goût nouveau fashion series
description: A collection of French lithographic prints from the 19th century depicting fashionably dressed women, part of a series titled 'Le Goût nouveau' (The New Taste) that documented contemporary fashion and style trends.

label: Chicago School Architecture Fragments
description: These objects are architectural fragments and decorative elements salvaged from late 19th and early 20th-century commercial and residential buildings in Chicago and the Midwest, representing the innovation and design of the Chicago School of Architecture.

label: Eighteenth-century French book illustrations
description: These objects are preparatory studies and drawings for vignettes and frontispieces commissioned for French literary works from the 18th century, created primarily in watercolor and ink media.

label: Asian Seals and Chop Marks
description: These objects are seals and chop seals from East Asia, primarily China and Japan, crafted from various materials including stone, jade, ivory, and bone, used for marking documents and artwork with personalized impressions.

label: East Asian Glazed Porcelain Tableware
description: A collection of decorative and functional porcelain vessels from China and Japan, primarily made with glaze and cobalt mineral, featuring painted landscapes, figures, and ornamental designs characteristic of Ming and Qing dynasty ceramics.

label: Académical figure drawing studies
description: A collection of French graphite drawings depicting nude male figures in various poses and positions, representing classical academic art training exercises focused on human anatomy and form.

label: Eighteenth-Century French Drawings
description: A collection of predominantly French drawings and sketches from the 18th century, primarily executed in black chalk, graphite, and ink on cream antique laid paper, featuring architectural and observational studies.

label: Early Photography of Pompeii and Naples
description: These are 19th-century albumen silver prints documenting the archaeological sites and landmarks of Naples and Pompeii, Italy, representing an important period of early photographic tourism and scientific documentation.

label: Portrait Photography Series by Artist
description: A collection of gelatin silver print portraits from the Getty Museum's photography department, featuring named individuals from American culture, likely part of a single photographer's systematic portrait series.

label: Odilon Redon Symbolist Works
description: A collection of late 19th-century French prints and paintings by Symbolist artist Odilon Redon, featuring mystical, dreamlike imagery with recurring themes of visions, spiritual beings, and psychological symbolism rendered primarily through lithography.

label: French Tree Studies in Print
description: A collection of French prints from the 19th century featuring trees and woodland landscapes, predominantly created using etching techniques on paper, often attributed to artists like Jean-Baptiste-Camille Corot.

label: Islamic Ceramic Architectural Tiles
description: These objects are decorative and functional ceramic tiles from Islamic cultures spanning the medieval to early modern periods, primarily from Iran, Syria, and the Ottoman Empire, used as architectural elements in buildings and characterized by glazing techniques and geometric or figural designs.

label: Etruscan Bone Funeral Couch Appliqués
description: These objects are decorative bone appliqués from Etruscan or Italic culture designed to ornament funeral couches, representing funerary furniture embellishments.

label: Paiute Life Stereograph Series
description: A collection of stereograph cards documenting the daily life, customs, games, and culture of the Kai-Vav-Its band of Paiute Native Americans living on the Kaibab Plateau near the Grand Canyon in Arizona during the 19th century.

label: Miao Traditional Textile Accessories
description: These objects represent the textile-based costume and adornment practices of the Miao people from China, encompassing garment components and worn accessories that formed integral parts of traditional dress.

label: French Othello Print Series
description: A sequential series of French intaglio prints illustrating scenes from Shakespeare's Othello, created using etching, engraving, and related techniques.

label: Bobbin lace technique samples
description: These objects are instructional textile samples from Amsterdam demonstrating various bobbin lace-making techniques and stitches, often paired with fragments showing their practical application in finished lace.

label: Upper Magdalenian Reindeer Bone Tools
description: A collection of chisels and related cutting tools carved from reindeer bone and decorated with incisions, dating to the Upper Magdalenian period and preserved in the National Archaeology Museum.

label: Dutch Schutterij Association Badges
description: These objects are commemorative medals and badges worn by members of Dutch civic militia (schutterij) organizations and their officer associations, primarily from Amsterdam and other Dutch cities during the 19th century.

label: Nineteenth-century glacier photography
description: These objects are albumen silver prints and related publications documenting Alpine glaciers in the Oberland region, representing early scientific photography of glacial phenomena and geological formations.

label: Biblical Story of Lot
description: These objects across multiple museums depict various scenes from the biblical narrative of Lot, including his exodus from Sodom and his relationship with his daughters, rendered through drawings, paintings, prints, and engravings primarily from Northern European Renaissance and Early Modern periods.

label: Prince Imperial commemorative medals
description: These objects are French numismatic medals from the Second Empire commemorating the birth, baptism, and life of Prince Napoléon Eugène Louis Jean Joseph (1856-1879), the son of Emperor Napoléon III and Empress Eugénie.

label: Chinese Silk Robes Collection
description: These objects are primarily Chinese robes made from silk and related materials, held in the Asian Art department, representing various styles from court wear to theatrical and informal garments.

label: Tibetan Buddhist Initiation Cards (Tsakali)
description: These objects are tsakali cards used in Tibetan Buddhist ritual practice, featuring symbolic imagery created with ink and watercolor on paper to guide religious initiations and teachings.

label: Asian Decorative Silk Textiles
description: These objects are predominantly silk-based decorative textiles from East and South Asia, primarily used for furniture coverings, wall hangings, and temple decorations, representing traditional craftsmanship across China, Japan, India, Indonesia, and Cambodia.

label: Asian Shawls and Wraps
description: A collection of decorative shawls and wraps from Asian cultures, primarily from India, Iran, Turkey, Pakistan, China, and Bangladesh, crafted using traditional textile techniques including weaving, embroidery, and needlework.

label: American Nature and Wildlife Prints
description: A collection of American prints from the 20th-21st centuries depicting fish, lakes, and natural landscapes, created using screen printing and digital printing techniques.

label: Good Samaritan Biblical Narrative Prints
description: These objects are prints and drawings depicting scenes from the biblical parable of the Good Samaritan, created primarily by Northern European artists (Dutch, German, and French) from the 16th-17th centuries using etching and engraving techniques.

label: Platinum Print Photography Collection
description: These objects are primarily platinum print photographs from early 20th-century America, many featuring members of the Flory family and created by a pictorialist photographer who often hand-applied drawing media like graphite, pencil, and watercolor to enhance the prints.

label: Amsterdam Kostverlorenvaart Landscape Drawings
description: A series of drawings depicting various views of the Kostverlorenvaart waterway and surrounding landmarks in Amsterdam, created using different drawing media on paper.

label: Biblical Narrative of Joseph
description: These objects depict episodes from the story of Joseph in the Hebrew Bible, including his dreams, his coat brought to Jacob, and his interpretations of others' dreams, rendered across multiple media and cultures from Renaissance to 19th-century European art.

label: Dutch Golden Age Church Interiors
description: These objects are architectural paintings and drawings depicting the interiors of Dutch and Northern European churches from the 16th-17th centuries, a genre that flourished during the Dutch Golden Age.

label: Volpini Suite and Related Works
description: These objects are prints from or related to Paul Gauguin's Volpini Suite (1889), a pioneering portfolio of zincographs depicting Breton life, laundresses, and maritime scenes from late 19th-century France.

label: American Stereographic Views of Mexico
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs created for American audiences depicting Mexican landscapes, archaeological sites, colonial architecture, and indigenous peoples during the 19th century.

label: French Urban Architectural Etchings
description: A collection of prints depicting Parisian and French city streets, bridges, and notable buildings, primarily created as etchings and drypoints on paper documenting 19th-century urban architecture and street scenes.

label: 19th Century Pennsylvania Stereographs
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting Pennsylvania landscapes, industrial sites, and communities during the oil boom and industrial era of the 1800s.

label: American Stereograph Comedy Scenes
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs from 19th-century America depicting humorous domestic and social situations, designed for entertainment viewing through stereoscope devices.

label: Prints of the Apostle James
description: A collection of early modern prints depicting either James the Greater or James the Lesser, two apostles from Christian tradition, rendered through various engraving and etching techniques across European artistic centers.

label: American Nocturne Print Series
description: A collection of etchings and drypoints by James McNeill Whistler depicting urban nocturnal scenes, primarily featuring titles like 'Nocturne: Palaces' and 'Salute' that showcase his innovative printmaking techniques.

label: Carriage and Wagon Design Drawings
description: These are technical design drawings for various horse-drawn vehicles from the Metropolitan Museum's collection, rendered primarily in ink and watercolor with gouache.

label: Middle Bronze Age Socket Axes
description: Collection of socketed axes from the Middle Bronze Age, crafted from copper and bronze using casting and hammering techniques, primarily from Atlantic zone workshops and preserved in French archaeological museums.

label: French monetary circulation (1870s-1900s)
description: Collection of coins from the Third French Republic and French Indochina spanning the late 19th century, representing metropolitan French currency and colonial monetary systems.

label: Jacques Necker Commemorative Objects
description: These museum objects are various medals, portraits, and commemorative items depicting Jacques Necker, the influential French finance minister during the French Revolution (1776-1790).

label: Commemorative tokens of Paris
description: A collection of French numismatic tokens (jetons) from various periods depicting Notre-Dame de Paris and the City of Paris, cast in base metals such as copper, brass, and lead.

label: 18th Century British Portrait Medallions
description: These objects are predominantly glass paste or jasperware portrait busts and heads from 18th-century Britain, featuring both contemporary figures and classical subjects rendered in relief or as three-dimensional sculptures.

label: Anishinaabe Ojibwe Beaded Objects
description: These objects represent the distinctive beadwork and material culture traditions of the Anishinaabe (Ojibwe) people from the Great Lakes and Northeastern Woodlands region, featuring decorative and functional items embellished with glass beads on leather, textiles, and natural materials.

label: Sketches for French Public Buildings
description: Oil paintings and gouaches created as preparatory sketches for decorating various French municipal buildings and public institutions, including city halls, the Paris Town Hall, and theaters.

label: 20th Century Woodcut Prints
description: A collection of woodcut and linoleum block prints from the 20th century, primarily from American and Cuban cultural contexts, depicting scenes of urban life, immigration, education, and social experiences.

label: Persian/Iranian Dress Fabric Fragments
description: These textile fragments are decorative dress fabrics from Iran (historical Persia), predominantly made of silk with gold or silver metallic threads, representing various weaving techniques including brocade and compound weaves.

label: German Printmaking Series and Collections
description: These objects are German prints from the 18th-19th centuries organized as multi-plate series and collections, including narrative sequences, illustrated literary works, and technical demonstration portfolios.

label: Photographs of Artworks and People
description: These are 19th-century photographs—primarily salted paper and albumen silver prints—documenting both Old Master drawings held in museums and contemporary military figures and scenes from the Crimean War era.

label: 19th-century French imperial photography
description: These objects are albumen silver print photographs and stereographs documenting the Château de Saint-Cloud imperial residence and Boulogne-sur-Mer, created during the Second Empire period to capture France's grand architectural and leisure destinations.

label: 19th Century Indian Landscape Photography
description: English albumen silver prints documenting the landscapes, valleys, peaks, and colonial settlements of the Indian subcontinent during the Victorian era.

label: Early Yosemite Valley Photography
description: A collection of albumen print photographs documenting Yosemite Valley's iconic landscapes, waterfalls, and granite formations from the mid-19th century American West.

label: Woodburytype portraits of 19th-century French notables
description: These are all woodburytype portrait prints from the Getty's photography collection depicting prominent French artists, writers, composers, engineers, and public figures of the 19th century.

label: Italian Capricci Etching Series
description: These are individual prints from Italian etching series titled 'Capricci' or 'Vari Capricci' (Various Caprices) from the 17th-18th centuries, featuring imaginative scenes of everyday figures like soldiers, beggars, and philosophers.

label: Views of Rome series
description: These are prints and paintings from the 18th-century documentary series 'Views of Rome' (Vedute di Roma) depicting Roman ruins, temples, monuments, and landmarks throughout Rome and its environs.

label: French Portraits in Etching
description: A collection of portrait prints created primarily through etching techniques, depicting notable French figures from the 16th-19th centuries including intellectuals, artists, clergy, and men of letters.

label: Religious figures in prints
description: These objects are primarily paper-based prints from the Rijksmuseum depicting monks from various religious orders, created using engraving and lithography techniques.

label: 18th Century The Hague Portraits
description: A collection of printed portrait engravings and etchings created in The Hague during the 18th century, depicting notable local figures including clergy, scholars, and artists on paper.

label: English satirical caricature prints
description: A collection of hand-colored and plain etchings from early 19th-century England depicting social satire, fashion ridicule, and humorous scenes of contemporary life, primarily from Rowlandson's caricature publications and the 'Monstrosities' series.

label: Jerome Xavier's Mirror of Holiness
description: These manuscript pages are from Father Jerome Xavier's Mir'at al-quds (Mirror of Holiness), a Christian text created in Mughal India for a royal prince, representing cross-cultural religious literature of the 16th-17th century.

label: Illustrated Edition of Mistral's Memoirs
description: A complete portfolio of illustrations from the 20th-century French edition of Frédéric Mistral's autobiographical work 'Mémoires et Recits,' featuring diverse subjects including portraits, architectural elements, and Provençal scenes distributed throughout the text.

label: Roger Rouffiange Fashion Archives
description: These objects are graphic design drawings from the Roger Rouffiange archives documenting French fashion designs including clothing, hats, and accessories from mid-20th century couture houses.

label: Jacques Callot's Comic Characters
description: A series of etched prints by Jacques Callot depicting diminutive caricatured figures engaged in various activities with musical instruments, weapons, and objects, representing the theatrical tradition of commedia dell'arte.

label: Mid-20th Century Korean Street Life
description: A collection of gelatin silver prints documenting everyday scenes of South Korean urban and street life, featuring ordinary people engaged in daily activities, social interactions, and work.

label: Magdalenian bone smoothing tools
description: These are bone lissoirs (smoothing/polishing tools) from the Magdalenian period (Upper Paleolithic), created through incision, engraving, and polishing techniques, and preserved in the French National Museum of Archaeology.

label: Ancient Greek Painted Ceramics
description: A collection of Greek pottery vessels from the 7th to 4th centuries BCE, primarily featuring painted decorative scenes with mythological narratives, athletic scenes, and ornamental patterns on various vessel forms.

label: Norfolk Broads platinum print album
description: These photographs are platinum prints from Peter Henry Emerson's influential album 'Life and Landscape on the Norfolk Broads,' documenting the English wetland landscape and traditional rural life in Norfolk.

label: Haudenosaunee Beaded Utility Whimseys
description: These are decorative beaded objects created by Haudenosaunee (Iroquois) artisans that repurpose European-style utilitarian forms like match containers, pincushions, and pouches as ornamental showpieces adorned with beadwork and sequins.

label: Sequential French lithographic comic suite
description: These objects form a numbered series of French lithographic prints featuring humorous dialogue and domestic scenes, likely illustrating a serialized narrative or theatrical work from the 19th century.

label: Eighteenth and Nineteenth Century Scent Bottles
description: These objects are decorative containers used for holding perfume or scent, crafted across Europe primarily during the 18th and 19th centuries using luxury materials such as porcelain, glass, gemstones, and precious metal mounts.

label: Indigenous Textile Garments
description: These objects are woven or embroidered textiles created by Asian and Pacific indigenous cultures, primarily serving ceremonial, protective, or everyday clothing purposes.

label: Ancient Eastern Mediterranean Vessels
description: These objects are jars and vessels from ancient Mediterranean and Near Eastern cultures, predominantly made through glass-blowing techniques or terracotta, representing trade and domestic storage practices across Syrian, Levantine, Phoenician, and Greek civilizations.

label: European Decorative Vessels and Containers
description: A collection of small-scale functional European objects from the 17th-18th centuries, primarily glass bottles, perfume vessels, cups, and salt cellars from Switzerland, England, and surrounding regions, representing domestic luxury items and tableware.

label: Mirrors across cultures and time
description: These objects are all mirrors or mirror-related items spanning multiple cultures, centuries, and materials, from ancient Egyptian glass to 19th-century American furniture pieces.

label: European Porcelain Tea Service Ware
description: These objects are ceramic porcelain pieces from European tea services, primarily from England, designed for serving and consuming tea and related beverages during the 18th-20th centuries.

label: Gilt Decorative Wall Lighting
