description: These objects are primarily 18th-century European wall-mounted light fixtures and candleholders made from gilt materials, featuring ornamental designs characteristic of Rococo and Neoclassical styles.

label: 19th-century American landscape photography
description: These objects are albumen and gelatin prints documenting American scenery, railroads, and geological formations from the mid-to-late 1800s, primarily created as promotional or documentary records of westward expansion and railroad routes.

label: Thomas Eakins' Photographic Circle
description: A collection of American photographs primarily documenting Thomas Eakins, his family, students, and artistic circle in late 19th-century Philadelphia, featuring portraits, studio scenes, and documentary images of classical costume studies and life modeling sessions.

label: Advertising Trade Cards with Photoreproductions
description: A collection of late 19th-century promotional trade cards from American and European businesses that feature photographic reproductions of prints, portraits, and artwork combined with merchant advertisements.

label: 19th Century Central America Stereographs
description: These are stereograph cards depicting landscapes, architecture, and daily life in Guatemala and Ecuador, produced for American audiences during the mid-to-late 1800s using albumen and gelatin silver print photography.

label: Colonial India Albumen Photographs
description: These are albumen silver print photographs documenting British colonial India, primarily featuring landscapes, architecture, and urban scenes from Calcutta and surrounding regions during the 19th century.

label: Dutch Printed Portraits
description: A collection of portrait prints from the Netherlands, primarily created using etching and aquatint techniques on paper, depicting notable Dutch figures and citizens from the 18th-19th centuries.

label: 19th Century Florida Stereograph Views
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs and card photographs documenting architectural landmarks and streetscapes in St. Augustine and Jacksonville, Florida during the American post-Civil War period.

label: French Satirical Character Studies
description: These objects are primarily 19th-century French lithographs and drawings depicting humorous character types and social satire, many from Honoré Daumier's series 'Les Toquades' and similar satirical works exploring Parisian society and human folly.

label: Printed Portraits of European Rulers
description: This collection comprises paper-based prints depicting emperors and military leaders from various European dynasties, spanning from ancient Roman rulers to early modern Holy Roman Emperors and German nobility.

label: Mughal Dynasty Paintings
description: These objects are primarily paintings from the Mughal dynasty period, created using opaque watercolor and other traditional media on paper, depicting imperial court figures, nobles, religious subjects, and animals from South Asian and Himalayan cultures.

label: Singhalese Buddhist Manuscript Pages
description: These objects are identical leaves from a single Buddhist manuscript from late 17th-century Sri Lanka, created on fiber paper using traditional Singhalese manuscript production techniques.

label: Jerome Xavier's Mir'at al-quds Manuscript
description: These objects are consecutive text pages from Father Jerome Xavier's Mirror of Holiness, a Christian theological manuscript created in Mughal India for a royal patron at Allahabad.

label: Asian Incense Game Sets
description: These objects are ornamental boxes with matching implements used for playing kōdō (the incense game), a sophisticated Japanese pastime that involved guessing and appreciating different incense scents, crafted from luxurious materials like lacquered wood, ivory, gold, and silver.

label: Ancient Greek Terracotta Wine Vessels
description: These objects are predominantly Attic red-figure stamnos fragments made from terracotta, representing ancient Greek (primarily Attic) wine storage and serving vessels, with one Etruscan example showing Mediterranean pottery influence.

label: Upper Paleolithic Stone Tool Kit
description: These objects represent a collection of flaked stone tools and animal remains from the Upper Paleolithic period, primarily made of worked flint and preserved at the Brittany Museum in Rennes, documenting early human hunting and tool-making technologies.

label: Prehistoric Stone and Bone Tools
description: This collection comprises prehistoric cutting, scraping, and piercing implements crafted from flint, bone, and deer antler, spanning from the Paleolithic through Predynastic periods and held at the National Archaeology Museum.

label: 16th Century French Apocalypse Engravings
description: A series of engraved prints from 16th-century France depicting scenes from the Book of Revelation, primarily by or associated with the artist Jean Duvet, illustrating biblical apocalyptic visions.

label: Traditional Asian Wrapped Garments
description: These objects are traditional draped textiles from across Asia—primarily saris from South Asia and sarongs from Southeast Asia—that function as essential clothing items worn by wrapping around the body.

label: French Still Life Paintings
description: A collection of predominantly oil paintings from Paris museums featuring the still life genre, depicting inanimate objects such as fruits, flowers, vessels, and dead animals arranged in artistic compositions.

label: French Political Leaders Commemorated
description: A collection of medals, busts, and decorative objects commemorating prominent French political and military figures—particularly Adolphe Thiers and Marshal Mac-Mahon—from the mid-to-late 19th century, reflecting France's transitional political periods including the Franco-Prussian War and early Third Republic.

label: Ancient Egyptian Protective Religious Objects
description: These objects are amulets and talismanic items from ancient Egypt designed to provide spiritual protection and divine favor, featuring Egyptian deities and sacred symbols worn or carried by the living or placed with the deceased.

label: Porcelain Plates and Platters
description: These objects are primarily decorative and functional tableware made from porcelain, spanning multiple cultures and centuries from Ming Dynasty China through 18th-19th century European and Chinese export traditions.

label: Annunciation Prints on Paper
description: This collection comprises prints depicting the Annunciation narrative, primarily created on paper using various printing techniques such as engraving and etching across European cultural contexts from Italy, Germany, and the Low Countries.

label: Prints of French King Louis XIV
description: A collection of paper-based prints depicting various members of the French royal family, particularly Louis XIV and his descendants, created through etching and engraving techniques in the 17th-18th centuries.

label: Antwerp Shell Study Etchings
description: A series of naturalistic etched prints depicting various mollusc shells, produced in Antwerp during the Renaissance as scientific studies of natural specimens.

label: French Monogram Design Studies
description: A collection of French graphic art drawings and designs featuring monograms, created primarily with graphite, ink, and gouache on paper, representing various name initials and personal marks from Paris museum collections.

label: Chauvigny Museum Archaeological Collection
description: These objects are all held in the archaeological and ethnographic museum of Chauvigny, France, spanning from Roman period artifacts (1st century bronze items) to 19th-20th century building materials (terra cotta tiles and bricks).

label: Twenty-four Paragons of Filial Piety
description: These objects represent Japanese artistic interpretations and adaptations of the classical Chinese moral exemplars of filial piety, depicted across multiple series and formats from the 17th century onward.

label: Kisokaido Station Series Prints
description: These Japanese woodblock prints depict individual stations along the Kisokaido, a major inland route during the Edo period, forming a comprehensive documentary series of 69 stations.

label: French Social Types Lithographs
description: A series of 19th-century French lithographic prints depicting everyday Parisian characters, occupations, and social types including workers, children, and domestic scenes.

label: Masques de diverses cultures et époques
description: Collection de masques provenant de différentes traditions culturelles et périodes historiques, conservés dans les musées parisiens, incluant des masques rituels africains, des masques mortuaires, des masques de guerre et des masques d'art moderne.

label: French Studies of Antique Sculpture
description: These drawings document 17th-century French artists' studies of ancient Roman and Greek sculptures, primarily from major Italian collections like the Borghese, Medici, Ludovisi, and Vatican galleries.

label: American Wooden Domestic Objects
description: These objects are primarily wooden furnishings and domestic items from American and Peruvian cultures, spanning from the late 17th century through modern times, including boxes, frames, and household storage items crafted from native woods.

label: 19th Century Albumen Prints of Benares
description: A collection of albumen silver prints documenting architectural and landscape views of Benares (Varanasi) and surrounding sites in India, created during the British colonial period as part of early photographic documentation of Indian heritage.

label: Eighteenth-Century Mezzotint Prints
description: These objects are all prints created using the mezzotint technique, predominantly from England and Ireland during the 18th century, featuring portraits, allegorical scenes, and narrative subjects.

label: 19th Century Parisian Stereograph Photography
description: These objects are albumen silver print stereographs depicting iconic Parisian landmarks and street scenes, produced in the mid-to-late 19th century as 'Vue Instantanée' (instantaneous views) for stereoscopic viewing.

label: Mt. Washington Stereograph Series
description: A collection of albumen silver print stereographs documenting the landscape, buildings, and extreme weather conditions at Mount Washington's summit in New Hampshire during the 19th century.

label: Ecce Homo Biblical Narrative Representations
description: A collection of artworks across multiple media and European cultures depicting the Ecce Homo scene, the biblical moment when Christ is presented to the people before crucifixion, realized through prints, drawings, paintings, and engravings primarily from the Northern European Renaissance and Early Modern periods.

label: Flemish Golden Age Artist Portraits
description: Etched and engraved portrait prints from the Rijksmuseum depicting prominent 16th-17th century Low Countries artists and cultural figures, produced on paper using printmaking techniques.

label: Albumen prints of religious engravings
description: These objects are nineteenth-century albumen silver print reproductions of religious engravings, predominantly after Albrecht Dürer, depicting scenes from Christian theology including the Passion, the Virgin Mary, and the Holy Family.

label: Equine subjects in prints
description: These objects are primarily paper-based prints and cartoons from 19th-century France and Paris that depict horses as their central subject, employing various lithographic and printing techniques.

label: Portrait bust drawings across cultures
description: A collection of portrait drawings depicting busts of individuals from various European and American cultures, created primarily using dry media techniques such as chalk, charcoal, graphite, and crayon on paper.

label: Chinese Scholar's Desk Implements
description: These objects are utilitarian tools and containers used by Chinese scholars, calligraphers, and painters to organize and support their writing and painting implements, primarily from China and crafted in materials like bamboo, jade, wood, and ivory.

label: Zuni Silver and Turquoise Jewelry
description: This collection comprises jewelry and adornments created by the A'shiwi (Zuni) people, primarily featuring combinations of silver, turquoise, and natural materials, representing a distinctive tradition of Southwestern Native American metalwork and jewelry craftsmanship.

label: Old Judge Cigarette Cards
description: Albumen photograph baseball cards and sports celebrity portraits produced as cigarette premiums by Old Judge Cigarettes in the 1880s, representing early mass-produced sports collectibles.

label: Iron Age British Gold Coins
description: These gold staters and quarter staters were minted by Celtic tribes in pre-Roman Britain (1st century BCE-1st century CE), featuring characteristic designs of horses, wheels, crescents, and abstract symbols that represent early British coinage traditions.

label: Picturesque Paris Views Print Series
description: A series of early 19th-century English prints depicting iconic Parisian landmarks and vistas from various viewpoints, likely part of a comprehensive topographical documentation of the city.

label: French Satirical Marriage Lithographs
description: A numbered series of French lithographic prints from the 19th century depicting humorous and satirical scenes of conjugal life and domestic relationships.

label: Hand-colored Tarot Card Prints
description: A comprehensive collection of tarot cards from a Swiss origin, all created as hand-colored woodcut prints with uniform dimensions, representing various suits (Swords, Cups, Coins) and major arcana cards.

label: Native American Turquoise Jewelry
description: These objects are predominantly jewelry pieces from Navajo and Pueblo cultures crafted with silver and turquoise, representing distinctive Southwest Native American artistic traditions.

label: Ivory Carving and Decorative Arts
description: This collection comprises primarily Japanese Meiji period ivory objects including sculptures, functional items, and chess pieces that demonstrate the skilled carving techniques and decorative arts traditions of late 19th-century Japan.

label: Asian Textile Floor Coverings
description: These objects are woven and textile floor coverings primarily from Turkey, Iran, and other Asian regions, made from natural fibers like wool and cotton, housed in museum Asian art departments.

label: French porcelain figurative ceramics
description: Decorative French ceramic figurines and sculptural groups from the 18th-19th centuries, typically crafted from hard porcelain with enameling, depicting theatrical characters, animals, domestic scenes, and social types.

label: Decorative plates across cultures
description: A collection of plates from Delft, China, and Japan traditions, primarily made from earthenware with tin glaze or porcelain with glaze, decorated with diverse motifs including religious scenes, heraldic emblems, animals, and landscapes.

label: Asian Tea Ceremony Vessels
description: A collection of decorative teapots and tea service vessels from China and Japan, crafted from porcelain, stoneware, and other materials, reflecting the cultural significance of tea drinking in East Asian traditions.

label: 17th Century European Women Costumes
description: A series of Bohemian etchings documenting the regional dress and social status of women across different European cities and countries during the 17th century.

label: Domestic and Decorative Textiles
description: These objects are functional household textiles and decorative fabrics used for domestic purposes, including linens, scarves, panels, and table coverings from various cultures and time periods.

label: Persian and Islamic Textiles
description: These objects are predominantly Iranian/Persian textiles spanning various periods and techniques, including dress fabrics, furnishings, and costume elements made from silk, often embellished with gold thread and created through diverse weaving and embroidery methods.

label: Precious metal functional vessels
description: These objects are decorative drinking vessels and small functional containers crafted from precious metals (silver, gold, bronze) across various European and Asian cultures spanning multiple centuries, serving both practical and ceremonial purposes.

label: Marie-Antoinette Commemorative Medals
description: These numismatic medals from the Musée Carnavalet document key moments in Marie-Antoinette's life and legacy, from her 1770 arrival in France through her execution in 1793 and subsequent commemoration during the Revolutionary period.

label: Chinese Funerary Ceramic Figures
description: These objects are earthenware ceramic figures created in China for burial in tombs, depicting court attendants, musicians, and retainers meant to serve the deceased in the afterlife.

label: 19th Century American Photography
description: This collection consists of American photographic prints and stereographs from the Getty Museum's photography department, primarily created using albumen silver prints in the 19th century, featuring both atmospheric cloud studies and scientific documentation of fossil traces.

label: Japanese Meiji Porcelain Tea Wares
description: A collection of Japanese porcelain teapots, teacups, lids, and bowls from the Meiji period (1868-1912) and early 20th century, predominantly featuring decorative techniques such as underglaze blue and featuring naturalistic motifs including flowers, landscapes, and bamboo.

label: Ancient Mythological Bronze Statuettes
description: These objects are small-scale bronze sculptures depicting classical mythological figures (primarily Venus/Herakles) from Mediterranean cultures spanning Etruscan, Roman, Greek, and Cypriot civilizations.

label: 1848 French Presidential Election Medals
description: Commemorative medals documenting Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte's election as President of the French Republic on December 10, 1848, marking a pivotal moment in Second Republic history.

label: Early 20th Century German Expressionist Prints
description: These works are primarily German Expressionist prints from the early 20th century, featuring socially conscious themes of urban life, labor, grief, and human suffering depicted through bold graphic techniques like etching, drypoint, and lithography.

label: Monthly Calendar Print Series
description: A collection of engravings representing the twelve months of the year, primarily from Haarlem culture, created as paper prints that together form a complete calendrical or seasonal cycle.

label: Wise and Foolish Virgins Prints
description: These engravings and etchings depict scenes from the biblical parable of the wise and foolish virgins, a popular subject in early modern European printmaking primarily produced by German, Dutch, and French artists.

label: 19th Century British Landscape Photography
description: A collection of albumen print photographs documenting picturesque natural landscapes, bridges, lakes, and rural scenery throughout England, Wales, and Cumberland from the 19th century.

label: Nineteenth-century albumen prints of European cathedrals
description: A collection of albumen silver print photographs documenting architectural details and interior/exterior views of Gothic and Romanesque cathedrals and churches across Europe, created during the 19th-century photographic documentation of religious architecture.

label: Mid-20th Century Middle East Photography
description: American gelatin silver print photographs documenting daily life, cultural scenes, and political events across the Middle East and North Africa during the mid-20th century colonial and post-colonial period.

label: Shakespeare's Hamlet Illustrated Series
description: A comprehensive series of 19th-century French lithographic illustrations depicting scenes and characters from William Shakespeare's tragedy Hamlet, created across multiple museum collections.

label: Biblical Solomon in Renaissance Art
description: These objects depict scenes from the Old Testament involving King Solomon—including his famous judgment, his idolatry, and biblical narratives—created primarily as 16th-century Northern European prints, engravings, and decorative glass.

label: German Renaissance Apocalypse Woodcuts
description: A series of German woodcut prints from the Renaissance period depicting scenes from the Book of Revelation, likely from Albrecht Dürer's famous Apocalypse series.

label: Victorian London Stereograph Collection
description: Albumen silver print stereographs documenting iconic London landmarks and architectural monuments from the 19th century, created for the popular stereoscopic viewing medium of the Victorian era.

label: Martyr saint woodcuts and engravings
description: A collection of early modern printed religious images depicting the suffering and execution of Christian saints, executed primarily as engravings and etchings on paper across European cultures.

label: Italian Academic Life Drawing Studies
description: These objects are nude figure studies created in Italy using various chalk and ink media, representing the foundational practice of academic art training in depicting the human form.

label: Fin-de-siècle Female Figure Prints
description: This collection comprises predominantly French prints from the late 19th century depicting women and girls as subjects, created using various printmaking techniques including lithography, etching, and wood engraving on paper.

label: Japanese Functional Decorative Objects
description: These objects represent Japanese domestic and ceremonial vessels spanning from the Muromachi through Meiji periods, unified by their sophisticated craftsmanship in lacquer, ceramic, and clay materials, and their integration of aesthetic design into everyday functional items.

label: Inca Royal History Manuscript
description: These are folios from a postconquest Peruvian manuscript documenting Inca rulers and nobility, likely from Martin de Murúa's Historia general del Piru, combining Spanish colonial documentation with indigenous Andean history.

label: Venetian Bobbin Lace Insertions
description: A collection of decorative lace insertions crafted using traditional bobbin lace techniques from Venice, Italy, all housed in the Cleveland Museum's textile department.

label: Dutch Decorated Paste Papers
description: Collection of ornamental starch-based painted papers (stijfselverfpapier) from the Rijksmuseum, featuring various decorative techniques including veining, brushwork, stamped patterns, and color applications, primarily used as wrappers and decorative papers.

label: American Engraved Bookplates Collection
description: A collection of engraved prints from 18th-19th century America used as ownership marks in books, featuring heraldic coats of arms or personalized inscriptions identifying prominent individuals and institutions.

label: Roman Ceramic Bowls
description: Collection of Roman-period ceramic bowls of various morphological types (round, carinated, collared, oblique-sided) held at the Musée Antoine Vivenel in Compiègne, primarily made using wheel-thrown pottery techniques.

label: Saint-Germain-l'Auxerrois Church Tokens
description: French numismatic tokens from the 17th-18th centuries associated with the Saint-Germain-l'Auxerrois church in Paris, made from various metals including copper, brass, and silver.

label: Saltmarket Closes Glasgow Documentation
description: These objects are photographic prints from a systematic documentary series depicting the historic closes (narrow alleyways) of Saltmarket in Glasgow, Scotland, created through various printing techniques including photogravure and carbon printing.

label: Ancient Greek Panathenaic Amphorae
description: These objects are terracotta amphora vessels from ancient Attic Greece, many fragmentary, that were awarded as prizes during the Panathenaic Games held in honor of the goddess Athena.

label: South Asian Ceremonial Veils and Shawls
description: These objects are decorative textiles worn by women for weddings and festivals across South Asia, characterized by regional variations in embroidery, dyeing techniques, and cultural significance.

label: Witte Leeuw shipwreck cargo
description: Chinese porcelain tableware recovered from the wreck of the Dutch East India Company (VOC) trading ship Witte Leeuw, representing colonial maritime trade goods from the 17th century.

label: Indian Miniature Paintings and Drawings
description: A collection of Indian artistic works from the Asian Art Department featuring religious, mythological, occupational, and narrative subjects created primarily using watercolor, gouache, and ink on paper or mica.

label: Valances: Decorative Textile Furnishings
description: These objects are all valances—decorative textile window or bed furnishings from diverse cultures spanning from China and Bali to Europe and America, created using various techniques including embroidery, weaving, printing, and ornamentation across multiple centuries.

label: Early New York City Street Photography
description: A series of platinum and palladium prints documenting Manhattan and outer borough streets, intersections, and neighborhoods from the early 20th century American urban landscape.

label: Ancient Korean Ceramic Vessels
description: These objects are primarily ceramics from ancient Korean kingdoms and periods (Bronze Age through Unified Silla), featuring functional vessels such as burial urns, jars, and lidded containers made from earthenware or stoneware.

label: Medieval Illuminated Manuscript Leaves
description: These objects are individual pages or sections excised from medieval religious manuscripts (psalters, gospels, and prayer books) featuring illuminated miniatures, decorative initials, and religious scenes created with tempera, gold leaf, and ink across various European scriptoria from the 13th-16th centuries.

label: Figure Study Prints and Drawings
description: These objects are primarily lithographic prints and drawings from American museums featuring nude and draped figure studies in various poses, representing foundational academic art practice from the late 19th or early 20th century.

label: Staffordshire Pottery Tableware
description: These objects are decorative and functional ceramic vessels from Staffordshire, England, primarily from the 18th and 19th centuries, including teapots, coffee pots, and related tableware made using various glazing and stoneware techniques.

label: 1878 Paris Exposition Universelle Medals
description: Commemorative medals and numismatic objects documenting the iconic buildings, events, and international participation at the 1878 Paris World's Fair.

label: Early Scottish Highland Photography
description: These objects are early photographic prints and stereographs documenting Loch Katrine and the Scottish Highlands, created using pioneering photographic techniques such as salted paper and albumen silver prints from the 19th century.

label: European Etched Prints on Paper
description: A collection of prints from the Rijksmuseum primarily created using etching and engraving techniques on paper, predominantly from French cultural origins, depicting religious narratives, allegorical scenes, and mythological subjects from the early modern period.

label: Renaissance Heraldic Prints
description: A collection of European printed engravings and etchings from the 16th century depicting coats of arms, family crests, and heraldic shields, often accompanied by decorative figures or supporters.

label: Dutch Golden Age Intellectual Portraits
description: These are oil paintings and drawings depicting prominent Dutch scholars, poets, physicians, clergymen, and civic leaders from the 17th and 18th centuries, predominantly from Amsterdam and other Dutch cities, many created by artists like Arnoud van Halen and Jan Maurits Quinkhard.

label: German Brass Tokens of French Kings
description: These objects are brass numismatic tokens (jetons) featuring French monarchs Louis XV and Louis XVI, produced in Germany and preserved in Parisian museum collections.

label: Saint-Louis Glass Paperweights
description: A collection of glass paperweights produced by the Saint-Louis-lès-Bitche glassworks, representing French decorative applied arts from the 19th century.

label: Akimel O'odham (Pima) Basketry
description: These objects are woven baskets and miniature baskets created by the Akimel O'odham (Pima) people of the American Southwest, made from natural plant fibers.

label: Islamic and South Asian Coinage
description: These objects are coins from the Islamic Ottoman Empire (Egypt, Tunisia, Libya, Algeria) and Nepal spanning the 16th-19th centuries, representing monetary systems across geographically and culturally distinct but historically interconnected regions.

label: Commemorative medals of French revolutionary leaders
description: These museum objects are commemorative medals from Paris museums depicting prominent figures of the French Revolution and early 19th-century France, primarily the Marquis de Lafayette and his contemporaries, created across different decades from 1789 to 1838.

label: Teneriffe Scientific Expedition Photography
description: British albumen silver print photographs documenting a 19th-century scientific expedition to Mount Teneriffe in the Canary Islands, capturing geological formations, vegetation, astronomical instruments, and measurement activities.

label: La Fontaine Tales Lithographic Album
description: A French lithographic album illustrating the narrative tales and fables of Jean de La Fontaine, consisting of individual story plates and covers.

label: Roman and Early Medieval Glassware
description: Collection of functional glass vessels from Roman and early medieval periods (1st-6th centuries), including drinking cups, pitchers, and storage containers, primarily made using blown glass techniques.

label: Zodiacal Calendar Illustrations
description: These objects are decorated folios and coins from medieval and Renaissance manuscripts that pair zodiacal signs with corresponding labor activities, historical figures, or celestial deities, representing the astrological calendar system.

label: Silver Bracelets Across Cultures
description: These objects are all silver or silver-combined bracelets classified as jewelry/adornment from diverse geographic origins, primarily from the Americas and Africa, unified by their shared material composition and wearable function.

label: European Textile Panels
description: A collection of textile panels primarily from France and one from medieval Spain, all held in museum textile departments as examples of historical fabric art and decorative textiles.

label: Maya Tzute Ceremonial Textiles
description: These objects are traditional woven cloths (tzutes) created by various Maya cultures of Guatemala, used for ceremonial purposes and daily wear, made primarily from cotton with decorative techniques including supplementary weft patterning, embroidery, and ikat.

label: European Rapiers and Accessories
description: A collection of rapiers and rapier-related objects from 16th-17th century Europe, primarily from Italy, Germany, Spain, and England, representing the development of this iconic thrust-focused sword used by nobility and wealthy merchants.

label: Japanese bamboo basketwork
description: These objects are Japanese baskets crafted from bamboo and related materials, representing a traditional decorative arts practice spanning functional and aesthetic applications.

label: Pre-Columbian Andean Metal Objects
description: These objects are metalwork artifacts from ancient Peruvian and Andean cultures spanning from the Lambayeque to Inca periods, including ceremonial knives (tumis), tweezers, plaques, and figural vessels crafted from copper, gold, silver, and bronze.

label: Southeast Asian Ethnic Textiles
description: These objects are decorative and functional garments and accessories from Hmong, Miao, Yi, and Coin Yao cultures of Southeast Asia and southern China, characterized by handcrafted techniques including embroidery, needlework, and the incorporation of metallic threads and beads.

label: Japanese Syllabary Picture Series
description: A collection of color woodblock prints from the Edo period organized around the Japanese hiragana syllabary (iroha), illustrating scenes from the classic literary work 'Tales of Ise' and other narratives.

label: Chinese Literati Painting Traditions
description: These objects represent classical Chinese ink paintings featuring symbolic plants (bamboo, plum, pine) and auspicious subjects (birds, landscapes) that embody literati aesthetic values and philosophical meanings from various dynasties.

label: Decorated Towels from Mediterranean and Eastern European Cultures
description: These objects are functional household textiles from various European and Mediterranean regions (Greece, Turkey, Spain, Italy, Russia, Mexico, and the Aegean islands) decorated through diverse embellishment techniques including embroidery, drawn threadwork, macramé, and supplementary weaving.

label: Edo Period Kabuki Actor Prints
description: These are Japanese woodblock prints from the Edo period depicting kabuki actors in various theatrical roles and characters.

label: Picturesque Selections Lithographic Series
description: A collection of 19th-century lithographic prints depicting scenic landscapes across England, Wales, and Continental Europe, primarily from the 'Picturesque Selections' series that captured romantic landscape views for English audiences.

label: 19th Century Colonial India Photography
description: A collection of albumen silver prints documenting landscapes, architecture, and infrastructure across British India, primarily focusing on Kashmir and major cities during the colonial period.

label: Colonial India Photography Series
description: A collection of albumen silver prints documenting Simla (Shimla), the British hill station in colonial India, featuring landscapes, landmarks, and colonial settlements from the English perspective.

label: Rembrandt's Nativity and Shepherd Scenes
description: A collection of etchings and drypoints depicting biblical scenes of the Nativity, Adoration of the Shepherds, and related events, primarily created by or attributed to Rembrandt van Rijn and held across major European and American museums.

label: Chromogenic Photographs of Plains, Georgia
description: A collection of chromogenic prints documenting the town of Plains, Georgia, featuring street views, civic buildings, churches, and local landmarks from the Getty Museum's photography collection.

label: Odilon Redon Symbolic Prints
description: This collection comprises printworks by French Symbolist artist Odilon Redon, created primarily through etching and drypoint techniques, featuring spiritual, biblical, and fantastical themes characteristic of late 19th-century French artistic innovation.

label: Winslow Homer American Works
description: These objects are works by American artist Winslow Homer, primarily watercolors and drawings depicting outdoor scenes including maritime subjects, wilderness landscapes, and coastal Maine environments created in the late 19th century.

label: Cleveland Museum Collection Holdings
description: These objects are all part of the Cleveland Museum of Art's permanent collection, primarily consisting of American drawings and paintings created in or associated with Ohio and Cleveland.

label: Figure studies of standing men
description: A collection of hand-colored prints from the Rijksmuseum depicting individual male figures in various poses, gestures, and clothing styles, primarily created through etching techniques.

label: Goryeo Dynasty Bronze Chopsticks
description: This collection comprises bronze eating utensils from Korea's Goryeo Dynasty (918-1392), held at the Cleveland Museum of Art's Korean art department.

label: Ancient Egyptian Funerary Figurines
description: These are multiple shawabtys of Ditamenpaankh, terracotta funerary figurines from Late Period Egypt (Dynasty 25) created to serve the deceased in the afterlife.

label: 19th Century British India Photography
description: A collection of albumen print photographs from glass plate negatives documenting Indian architectural monuments, palaces, and landscapes during the 19th century colonial period.

label: French graphic design studies
description: A collection of French graphical drawings and technical studies depicting jewelry designs (rings, bracelets, masks) executed primarily in graphite pencil on vellum paper, housed in Paris museums.

label: Late 19th-century French architectural photography
description: A collection of albumen prints and gelatin-bromide glass plate negatives documenting French architectural heritage, particularly focusing on châteaux, churches, and townscapes from the Poitou region, created in the final quarter of the 19th century.

label: Edo Period Japanese Steel Arrowheads
description: These are identical steel arrow points from Japan's Edo period (1615-1868), classified as arms and armor and housed in the Cleveland Museum of Art's Japanese collection.

label: Decorated Papers with Hand Coloring
description: A collection of decorated papers from the Rijksmuseum featuring solid color applications and stenciled patterns, created through hand coloring, painting, and lacquer coating techniques on paper.

label: The Last Supper Depictions
description: These objects are artistic representations of the Last Supper (Laatste Avondmaal), a central Christian subject depicted across multiple media including prints, drawings, and sculpture from Italian, German, and Low Countries artistic traditions.

label: Renaissance miracle print series
description: These objects form a complete 42-print series depicting miracles attributed to the Annunciate Madonna of Florence, created as engravings during the Renaissance period and catalogued in major print reference works.

label: French Revolutionary Assembly Deputies
description: These objects commemorate members of the French Constituent Assembly (1789-1791) through prints, medals, and memorabilia, with particular emphasis on prominent figures like Mirabeau and other revolutionary political leaders.

label: Franco-Prussian War Medical Services
description: Commemorative medals documenting military ambulances and medical relief efforts in Paris during the 1870-1871 Franco-Prussian War and the Siege of Paris.

label: Medieval Paris pilgrimage tokens
description: These objects are méreaux (tokens) from Saint-Jacques churches in Paris, used as pilgrimage tokens or charitable markers from the 15th-18th centuries, primarily minted in copper, tin, and lead for the famous Saint-Jacques-de-la-Boucherie and Saint-Jacques-de-l'hôpital institutions.

label: American Drawings and Sketches
description: This collection consists of graphite sketches and watercolor drawings held in the Department of Arts of the Americas at MIA, primarily depicting scenes from Montana Indian Reservations and other American landscapes.

label: Early Modern Gunpowder Containers
description: These objects are decorated functional containers designed to safely store and dispense gunpowder for early modern firearms, created across Europe from the 16th-18th centuries using materials such as horn, bone, leather, and ivory.

label: Ancient Peruvian Textile Fragments
description: These objects are fragmented textiles from various pre-Columbian Peruvian cultures spanning different regions and time periods, all housed in the Art Institute of Chicago's textile department.

label: Ancient animal figurines and sculptures
description: These objects are small-scale representations of animals across ancient civilizations (Greek, Roman, and other cultures), created using various materials including bronze, marble, terracotta, and bone, primarily serving religious, decorative, or utilitarian purposes.

label: Pitchers Across Cultures
description: These objects are all pitchers—functional vessels designed for pouring liquids—created across multiple continents, time periods, and materials including glass, ceramics, and metalwork.

label: French Portrait Busts and Sculptures
description: These objects are French sculptural portraits in various forms (busts, statuettes, and head sculptures) depicting notable figures from French cultural, theatrical, and political history, created primarily in plaster, marble, and terra cotta.

label: Asian Sacred and Decorative Sculpture
description: These objects are primarily sculptures from Asia spanning multiple cultures and periods, depicting religious figures, deities, architectural elements, and devotional objects created from various materials including stone, terracotta, bronze, and metal.

label: 18th-century satirical etchings
description: A collection of cartoons and satirical prints from the London and European cultural sphere, created on paper using etching and stipple engraving techniques, depicting social commentary through character types and everyday scenes.

label: 18th Century Colonial American Silver
description: These objects are predominantly silver vessels and tableware made in colonial America (Boston, Salem, Philadelphia) and England during the 18th century, representing shared transatlantic metalworking traditions.

label: Hyakunin Isshu Poetry Series Prints
description: Japanese woodblock prints depicting individual poets and their poems from various editions of the Hyakunin Isshu (One Hundred Poems by One Hundred Poets), a canonical anthology of classical Japanese poetry.

label: Willem III Military Encampment Drawings
description: A series of ink and paper drawings documenting the locations and views of army encampments of Dutch ruler Willem III across the Spanish Netherlands and Low Countries regions during the late 17th century.

label: Nineteenth-century photographs of Baalbek
description: A collection of albumen prints and stereographs depicting the ancient Roman ruins and temples at Baalbek (Baalbec), created primarily by English photographers during the 19th century as part of the broader tradition of archaeological and orientalist documentation.

label: Complete Print Series by J.B. Huet
description: These objects form a comprehensive collection of etched prints comprising the complete 38-plate series 'Oeuvres de J. B. Huet,' a French printmaking work from the 18th century.

label: Amsterdam Fortifications and Mills
description: A series of drawings documenting the defensive bulwarks (bolwerken) and windmills that protected and serviced Amsterdam's 17th-century fortified perimeter.

label: 20th Century Urban Landscape Prints
description: A collection of early 20th-century prints featuring architectural and urban scenes of British cathedrals, American city streets, and European landmarks, created primarily through etching and drypoint techniques.

label: American Botanical Photography Studies
description: A collection of chromogenic color prints from the Getty Museum's photography department featuring close-up studies of plants, branches, flowers, and vines from American locations.

label: Dutch Golden Age Figure Studies
description: These are chalk and ink drawings by Herman Saftleven and contemporaries depicting everyday figures and social interactions from 17th-century Dutch life.

label: Dutch Genre Prints of Daily Life
description: A collection of Northern European etchings and engravings from the 16th-17th centuries depicting everyday scenes and character studies of ordinary people in domestic and tavern settings.

label: Religious Prints from Europe
description: These objects are all prints depicting religious subjects, primarily Madonna and Child and saint scenes, created across European cultures (Italy, France, Spain, England) from the 16th-19th centuries using techniques such as etching, engraving, and woodcut.

label: Amsterdam prints on paper
description: A collection of prints from the Rijksmuseum created in Amsterdam using various printmaking techniques (etching, engraving, lithography, wood engraving, and letterpress) on paper, depicting diverse subjects ranging from regional Dutch figures and occupations to allegorical scenes and landscapes.

label: Architectural Etchings of European Streets
description: A collection of prints depicting urban architectural scenes from Brussels, London, and French towns, created primarily through etching techniques by American-associated artists.

label: Ancient Chinese Ceremonial Weapons
description: These objects are primarily axes, daggers, and dagger-axes from ancient Chinese dynasties (Neolithic through Han period) that functioned as both weapons and ceremonial/ritual objects, crafted from bronze, jade, and other materials.

label: Han Dynasty Funerary Models
description: These objects are earthenware models and architectural elements created during China's Han Dynasty (202 BCE–220 CE) as grave goods to represent buildings, domestic structures, and household items for use in the afterlife.

label: American Venice Series Etchings
description: A collection of prints and related materials depicting Venetian scenes, created by an American artist using etching and drypoint techniques, held across multiple major U.S. museum institutions.

label: Goryeo Dynasty Bronze Metalwork
description: These objects are predominantly bronze or gilt bronze items from Korea's Goryeo period (918-1392), including ornaments, tools, and functional metalwork that showcase the dynasty's sophisticated metalworking techniques.

label: Chinese Imperial Decorative Textiles
description: These objects are silk and wool textiles from Ming and Qing dynasty China, including tapestries (kesi), brocades, and furnishing covers featuring auspicious symbols, nature motifs, and imperial imagery for court and elite domestic use.

label: East Asian Buddhist Religious Art
description: These objects are paintings and ceramics from Chinese, Korean, and Sino-Tibetan cultures depicting Buddhist deities, bodhisattvas, and arhats (lohans) created primarily during the Ming and Qing dynasties.

label: Chinese Birds and Flowers Paintings
description: These objects are ink and color paintings on silk from Chinese dynasties (Yuan through Qing) depicting naturalistic scenes of birds, insects, flowers, and plants in the traditional birds-and-flowers genre.

label: Ancient Chinese Bronze Ornaments
description: These objects are decorative and functional bronze pieces from various Chinese dynasties (Shang through Qing), featuring animal motifs, masks, and finials that reflect the aesthetic and cultural practices of imperial China.

label: Edo Period Japanese Ceramics
description: A collection of functional ceramic vessels and wares from Edo period Japan (1615-1868), representing diverse regional pottery traditions including tea bowls, serving vessels, and storage jars used in daily life and tea ceremony practice.

label: Islamic Ceramic and Metal Vessels
description: These objects are decorative vessels and vessel fragments from Islamic cultures (primarily Iranian and Syrian), spanning the Fatimid, Saljuq, and Safavid periods, created using stone-paste and earthenware with painted, lustred, or glazed decoration techniques.

label: Ancient Glass Inlay Components
description: These objects are fragments and tools related to ancient Egyptian and Syrian glass inlay production and decoration, primarily dating to the Ptolemaic and Roman periods.

label: Ancient Chinese Jade Bi Disks
description: These objects are ritual jade disks (bi) from the Liangzhu culture and early Chinese dynasties, characterized by their circular form with a central hole and use in ceremonial contexts spanning the late Neolithic through Shang periods.

label: Ancient Chinese Garment Hooks
description: These objects are decorative metal fasteners (daigou/gou) used to secure clothing in ancient China from the Eastern Zhou through Qing dynasties, crafted from bronze, jade, iron, and precious metal inlays.

label: Edo Period Japanese Silk Textiles
description: A collection of silk brocades and textiles from Edo period Japan, including garment components (obis, kimonos), religious textiles (altar cloths, temple covers), and decorative pieces unified by their Japanese cultural origin, silk construction, and historical period.

label: Ancient Syrian Glass Beads
description: A collection of glass beads and related ornaments from ancient Syrian/Middle Eastern cultures, featuring various shapes and forms including cubical, almond-shaped, spherical, and triple configurations, all crafted from glass and used as personal adornment.

label: Japanese Buddhist Monastic Vestments
description: These objects are silk brocade Buddhist monk robes (kesa) and stoles (ohi) from Edo period Japan, many patched for continued use, representing essential religious garments in Japanese Buddhist practice.

label: Chinese Dynasty Paintings and Scrolls
description: These objects are predominantly paintings from the Yuan, Ming, and Qing dynasties made with ink and color on silk or paper, featuring themes of landscape, figures, animals, and literary subjects that exemplify Chinese artistic traditions.

label: Neolithic Chinese Jade Ornaments
description: These objects are jewelry and ceremonial items crafted from jade during China's Late Neolithic period, primarily associated with Liangzhu culture and other contemporary Chinese cultures.
